The Lambda function executes three sequential phases: GatewayIngestion (authenticated REST calls to Stripe Balance Transactions API, Adyen Settlement Details API, and Braintree Transaction Settlement API), Normalization (mapping each gateway response to a canonical SettlementRecord with transaction_reference, amount_cents, fees_cents, currency, settled_date, gateway_name), and LedgerJoin (PostgreSQL query joining canonical records to ledger_entries by transaction_reference, classifying rows as matched, amount_mismatch, fee_mismatch, missing_in_ledger, or missing_in_settlement). Results are persisted to discrepancies table and posted as a structured Slack block-kit message and written to a Google Sheets tab via Sheets API. All gateway credentials and output channel secrets managed in AWS Secrets Manager. Lambda runs inside a VPC private subnet accessing RDS and Secrets Manager via VPC endpoints, eliminating internet egress requirements.
target_output.technology_stack: Runtime: Python 3.12 on AWS Lambda (arm64 for cost efficiency). Framework: FastAPI for health and manual trigger endpoints. Database: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS (db.t3.medium, single-AZ for dev, Multi-AZ for production). Integrations: Stripe Python SDK, Adyen REST API via httpx, Braintree Python SDK. Output: Slack Blocks API via httpx, Google Sheets API v4 via google-api-python-client. Secrets: AWS Secrets Manager. Scheduling: EventBridge Scheduler. CI/CD: GitHub Actions. Observability: CloudWatch Logs + Metric Filters.
target_output.functional_feature_map: GatewayIngestion module: authenticated fetch of previous day settlement reports from Stripe (Balance Transactions list filtered by settlement_date), Adyen (Settlement Details API with merchant account param), Braintree (Transaction search by settlement date range). Normalization module: per-gateway mapper class translating provider-specific fields to SettlementRecord(transaction_reference, amount_cents, gateway_fees_cents, currency, settled_date, gateway_name). LedgerJoin module: PostgreSQL join with classification logic; configurable discrepancy threshold per gateway (default 0 cents for amount, 50 cents tolerance for FX fee rounding). Report Composer: Slack block-kit message with summary (run_id, date, records processed per gateway, discrepancy count by severity) and Google Sheets row appended to reconciliation history tab.
target_output.system_components: EventBridge Scheduler (cron 06:00 UTC, targeting Lambda function ARN), Lambda Function reconciliation_runner (Python 3.12 arm64, 512MB memory, 900s timeout, VPC private subnet), RDS PostgreSQL 16 (Multi-AZ production, private subnet, encrypted at rest), VPC Endpoints for Secrets Manager and RDS (eliminating NAT Gateway dependency), Secrets Manager (five secrets: stripe_api_key, adyen_api_key, braintree_api_key, slack_webhook_url, sheets_service_account_json), SNS Topic for Lambda failure alerts routed to engineering Slack channel, CloudWatch Log Group with 90-day retention.
target_output.workflows: Daily Reconciliation Run: EventBridge fires at 06:00 UTC, invokes Lambda. Lambda calls Secrets Manager to retrieve gateway credentials. GatewayIngestion phase calls Stripe Balance Transactions API for settlement_date=yesterday (paginated), then Adyen Settlement Details for merchant_account + settlement_date, then Braintree search for settled_at=yesterday. Each raw response stored to settlement_files table as JSONB. Normalization phase maps each record to SettlementRecord canonical form. LedgerJoin phase executes: SELECT lr.transaction_reference, lr.expected_amount_cents, sr.amount_cents, ... FROM ledger_entries lr FULL OUTER JOIN normalized_settlements sr ON lr.transaction_reference = sr.transaction_reference WHERE lr.settled_at = :date OR sr.settled_date = :date. Each row classified by delta_cents and discrepancy_type. Discrepancies inserted to discrepancies table. reconciliation_run updated to complete. Report posted to Slack and Sheets. Manual Trigger: POST /trigger with custom JWT calls same reconciliation function with override date parameter; useful for reprocessing a specific settlement date after ledger corrections.
target_output.data_model: settlement_files (id UUID PK, gateway_name VARCHAR, settlement_date DATE, raw_json JSONB, fetched_at TIMESTAMP, run_id UUID FK). ledger_entries (id UUID PK, transaction_reference VARCHAR UNIQUE NOT NULL, expected_amount_cents BIGINT NOT NULL, expected_fee_cents BIGINT, currency CHAR(3), gateway_name VARCHAR, settled_at DATE, created_at TIMESTAMP) — GIN index on transaction_reference. reconciliation_runs (id UUID PK, triggered_at TIMESTAMP, completed_at TIMESTAMP, gateways_processed TEXT[], total_records_processed INT, total_discrepancies INT, status VARCHAR CHECK IN (pending, complete, partial_complete, failed), error_detail TEXT). discrepancies (id UUID PK, run_id UUID FK, transaction_reference VARCHAR, gateway_name VARCHAR, expected_amount_cents BIGINT, actual_amount_cents BIGINT, delta_cents BIGINT, discrepancy_type VARCHAR CHECK IN (amount_mismatch, fee_mismatch, missing_in_ledger, missing_in_settlement), severity VARCHAR CHECK IN (low, medium, high), resolved BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, resolved_at TIMESTAMP, resolved_by VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP) — index on (run_id, resolved). remittance_reports (id UUID PK, run_id UUID FK, gateway_name, report_json JSONB, sent_at TIMESTAMP).
target_output.api_design: GET /health — returns {status: ok, last_run_id, last_run_status, last_run_completed_at}; no auth; Lambda health check. POST /trigger — body: {settlement_date: YYYY-MM-DD, gateways: [stripe, adyen, braintree]}; auth: custom JWT (X-Internal-Token header verified against secret in Secrets Manager); returns {run_id, status: queued}; triggers synchronous reconciliation with override date. GET /runs/{run_id} — returns reconciliation_run record with discrepancy summary; auth: custom JWT. GET /runs/{run_id}/discrepancies — paginated list of discrepancy records for a given run; supports filter by gateway_name, discrepancy_type, resolved; auth: custom JWT.
target_output.security_and_compliance: SOC 2 Type II: every reconciliation_run persisted with triggered_at, gateways_processed, outcome, and error_detail enabling full audit reconstruction. discrepancies retained 7 years. Lambda IAM role restricted to: Secrets Manager GetSecretValue for specific five secret ARNs, RDS Data API access, CloudWatch PutLogEvents, SNS Publish to alert topic. Each gateway API key in a separate Secrets Manager secret (stripe_api_key, adyen_api_key, braintree_api_key) with distinct resource policies. POST /trigger endpoint validates custom JWT token value from Secrets Manager before execution. All gateway API calls over TLS 1.3. RDS encrypted at rest with AWS-managed KMS key. settlement_files raw_json contains no card PANs — only transaction references and net amounts, confirming PCI scope exclusion.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions pipeline: ruff lint, pytest (80% coverage gate), integration tests against staging RDS seeded with synthetic settlement data from all three gateways, Lambda ZIP package build, upload to S3, deploy to staging Lambda alias via AWS CLI, manual approval gate, deploy to production alias. Lambda aliases: staging and production pointing to specific version numbers, enabling instant rollback by alias update. EventBridge rule targets Lambda function ARN with ARN-specific permission. RDS daily automated backup, 7-year snapshot retention policy via AWS Backup plan. Alembic for schema migrations run as a pre-deploy step in GitHub Actions against staging, then production.
target_output.observability: CloudWatch Logs: structured JSON per Lambda invocation with run_id, gateway, phase, records_fetched, discrepancies_found, duration_ms. CloudWatch Metric Filters extract: reconciliation_discrepancy_count (sum by gateway), reconciliation_run_duration_ms (gauge). CloudWatch Alarms: Lambda error rate > 0 (triggers SNS notification to engineering Slack), Lambda duration > 800 seconds (pre-timeout warning), discrepancy_count > 500 in a single run (triggers finance team notification). All alarms routed through SNS to Slack via Lambda forwarder. Monthly reconciliation summary dashboard in CloudWatch with discrepancy trend per gateway.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $45/month total. Lambda: $0.01/day (1 invocation, 900s max). RDS db.t3.medium Multi-AZ: ~$35/month. Secrets Manager: $2/month for 5 secrets. CloudWatch: $3/month. Gateway API calls: within free tiers for Stripe and Adyen settlement APIs. Scaling: Lambda memory can be increased to 1024MB if LedgerJoin query exceeds 10-minute mark; if daily volume exceeds 2M transactions, migrate to ECS Fargate task to escape Lambda timeout constraint.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (weeks 1-4): Stripe gateway integration, canonical SettlementRecord schema, PostgreSQL schema and Alembic migrations, LedgerJoin logic, Slack report, CI/CD pipeline, CloudWatch alarms. Phase 2 (weeks 5-7): Adyen gateway integration (more complex settlement report parsing with merchant account grouping), discrepancy severity classification, Google Sheets export, POST /trigger manual override endpoint. Phase 3 (weeks 8-10): Braintree gateway integration, configurable per-gateway discrepancy thresholds, discrepancy resolution tracking (resolved_at, resolved_by fields), GET /runs/{run_id}/discrepancies API for finance team tooling integration.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with ruff for linting and black for formatting enforced in CI. Each gateway integration implemented as a GatewayClient subclass with a common fetch_settlement_records(date) -> list[SettlementRecord] interface, enabling easy addition of future gateways. Integration tests use pytest fixtures with synthetic settlement data representing all three gateways including edge cases: FX rounding differences, same-day refunds, and missing ledger entries. Lambda handler tested with mocked Secrets Manager and mocked gateway API responses. No card PAN data permitted in test fixtures — use generated transaction references only.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Lambda 15-minute timeout at high transaction volumes. Mitigation: PostgreSQL GIN index on transaction_reference, settlement_files partitioned by settlement_date, benchmark at 500K records before launch; escalation path to ECS Fargate if volumes grow beyond Lambda capacity. Risk: Adyen settlement report format changes break normalization. Mitigation: raw_json stored in settlement_files before normalization; failed normalization logged with raw payload preserved for debugging without re-fetching. Tradeoff: single Lambda function vs step function workflow — Lambda is simpler and cheaper for a sub-10-minute job but loses intermediate state on timeout; step function would add resilience at higher cost, revisit if Braintree addition pushes runtime near timeout limit.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed PCI scope exclusion: settlement files contain net amounts and transaction references, not card PANs. Chose Lambda over ECS Fargate for cost simplicity at current daily volume. Google Sheets API v4 selected over CSV email attachment for finance team usability. Braintree deferred to phase 3 due to OAuth complexity in sandbox environment.

metadata.notes: Weak quality: architecture is appropriately thin for an automation tool but several sections (observability, cost_and_scaling) are at minimum depth; data_model and workflows are domain-specific and solid.

profile.domain: Merchant Self-Service Portal for Payment Facilitator

project_goal.value: Merchant self-service portal for a payment facilitator enabling small businesses to view real-time transaction dashboards, manage dispute responses, download payout reports, and update bank account details without contacting support
project_goal.rationale: Core business need: reduce support ticket volume by 60% and give merchants real-time visibility into their payment activity

target_users.value: Small business merchants using the payment facilitator, and internal operations team via admin panel
target_users.rationale: Primary users are merchants needing self-service; ops team needs override and investigation capabilities

project_class.rationale: Browser-based SPA with SSR for performance; primary delivery is a web experience

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, auth, payments, data, analytics, admin_panel
capabilities.rationale: Transaction display, dispute management, payout downloads, bank account updates, analytics dashboards, and ops admin panel all required

complexity_level.rationale: Sub-second page loads for 2-year transaction history requires advanced caching and query optimization; dispute workflow has compliance timing requirements

risk_level.rationale: Bank account details and payout data are high-sensitivity financial information; dispute handling has regulatory deadlines

data_sensitivity.rationale: Transaction records, payout amounts, bank account details, and chargeback data are financially sensitive

external_exposure.rationale: All portal access requires Auth0 authentication; no public-facing data

access_model.value: Auth0 with MFA for merchants; RBAC with merchant_owner, merchant_staff, ops_admin, ops_viewer roles; merchant data isolation enforced at API layer by merchant_id claim
access_model.rationale: Multi-tenant portal requires strict data isolation; MFA required for financial actions

feature_scope.value: Transaction history dashboard (2-year range, sub-second load), dispute management with evidence upload and countdown timer, payout schedule configuration and download, bank account management with micro-deposit verification, cash flow projection, fee schedule viewer, processing limits display
feature_scope.rationale: Full merchant self-service surface replacing all support-ticket-driven workflows

mvp_scope.value: Transaction dashboard, dispute response submission with file upload, payout report download, bank account update with micro-deposit verification, Auth0 login with MFA
mvp_scope.rationale: Highest-value features for reducing support volume in first release

security_baseline.value: Auth0 MFA enforced for all roles; TLS 1.3; bank account changes require re-authentication; evidence uploads scanned for malware; all financial actions write to immutable audit log
security_baseline.rationale: PCI-DSS Level 2 and NACHA ACH rules require strong authentication and audit trails for bank account operations

frontend_stack.value: Next.js 14 with TypeScript, App Router, React Server Components for transaction list pages
frontend_stack.rationale: RSC enables server-side data fetching for large transaction datasets; App Router for nested merchant portal layouts

backend_stack.value: Node.js 22 with Hono deployed on ECS Fargate; Vercel for Next.js frontend hosting
backend_stack.rationale: Hono is a high-throughput Node framework suited for API-heavy workload; Vercel edge network for frontend performance

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 with read replicas for analytics queries; Redis 7 for transaction list cache and session storage
data_platform.rationale: Read replicas isolate heavy analytics queries from transactional OLTP; Redis for sub-second transaction list response times

hosting_target.value: Vercel for Next.js frontend; ECS Fargate for Hono API; RDS PostgreSQL in us-east-1
hosting_target.rationale: Vercel provides global CDN and edge runtime for frontend; Fargate for stateless API scaling

privacy_retention_policy.value: Transaction records retained 7 years; audit logs retained 7 years; payout reports available for download for 2 years; GDPR right-to-erasure for EU merchant contact data only (not transaction records)
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Financial record retention law and GDPR for EU merchant personal data

future_scope.value: Mobile app for merchant transaction monitoring; real-time webhook delivery to merchant endpoints; white-label portal for platform resellers
future_scope.rationale: Post-MVP roadmap after self-service adoption is measured

constraints.value: Sub-second p95 load time for transaction history spanning 2 years; PCI-DSS Level 2; NACHA micro-deposit verification for bank account changes; GDPR for EU merchants; dispute response deadline tracking required
constraints.rationale: Merchant SLA expectations and regulatory requirements

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM for API and frontend; Core Web Vitals tracking via Vercel Analytics; custom metrics for dispute response rate and payout download success rate; PagerDuty for API error spikes
observability_baseline.rationale: Corporate monitoring standard; product metrics needed to track self-service adoption

execution_preference.value: Agile sprints with 2-week iterations; feature flags for gradual merchant cohort rollout
execution_preference.rationale: Feature flags allow controlled rollout to merchant cohorts before full launch

compliance_context.value: PCI-DSS Level 2: SAQ-D self-assessment, no card storage in portal, bank account numbers encrypted at rest, quarterly vulnerability scans; NACHA: micro-deposit verification mandatory before ACH debit of merchant bank accounts; GDPR: data subject access request support for EU merchant personal data
compliance_context.rationale: Payment facilitator role and bank account handling mandate PCI-DSS Level 2 and NACHA compliance

project.goal: Self-service portal for merchants to manage transactions, disputes, payouts, and bank accounts
project.target_users: Small business merchants; ops admin team
project.timeline: MVP in 12 weeks; full feature set in 20 weeks

frontend.approach: App Router with React Server Components for transaction pages; client components for interactive dispute and bank account flows
frontend.state: React Query for server state; Zustand for multi-step dispute form state

security.encryption: TLS 1.3; bank account numbers AES-256 encrypted at rest; re-auth required for bank account changes

data.cache: Redis 7 for transaction list cache (TTL 60s) and Auth0 session storage

open_questions.dispute_evidence_storage: S3 or third-party document storage for uploaded evidence files

reasoner_reviews.product: The dispute countdown timer is a critical UX feature — merchants routinely miss chargeback response deadlines. Ensure the timer reflects the actual deadline from the card network (Visa/MC have different windows) stored on the dispute record, not a calculated estimate. The cash flow projection feature is a stretch for MVP; recommend deferring to phase 2 to protect MVP timeline.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: React Server Components are the right choice for transaction history pages — RSC eliminates the need to ship 2 years of transaction data to the client on initial load. Redis cache with 60-second TTL on transaction list queries is essential for sub-second p95; ensure cache keys are merchant-scoped (merchant_id + filters) to prevent data leakage. Read replica for analytics queries must be enforced at the ORM layer with a readOnly: true connection flag.
reasoner_reviews.security: Bank account re-authentication flow must use Auth0 step-up authentication (not just frontend confirmation dialog) to meet NACHA requirements for ACH account changes. Evidence uploads must be scanned via ClamAV or AWS Malware Protection before storage. Ensure Auth0 merchant_id claim is validated server-side on every API request — frontend merchant_id must never be trusted.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: PostgreSQL performance on 2-year transaction history requires composite index on transactions(merchant_id, created_at DESC) plus a materialized view for the 30/60/90-day summary dashboard to avoid aggregate queries on every page load.

frontend.app_structure: Next.js 14 App Router with nested layouts: (auth)/layout (Auth0 session), (portal)/layout (merchant_id context, nav), (portal)/transactions/layout (transaction filter state), (admin)/layout (ops role gate). RSC for transactions/page.tsx and payouts/page.tsx — server-fetches data with merchant_id from session. Client components for DisputeEvidenceUploader, BankAccountUpdateFlow, and DisputeCountdownTimer.
frontend.pages_and_flows: /(portal)/transactions — RSC transaction history with date range picker and pagination, Redis-cached; /(portal)/disputes — dispute list with countdown timers, evidence upload drawer; /(portal)/payouts — payout history table with CSV download; /(portal)/bank-accounts — current bank account with step-up auth flow for changes; /(admin)/merchants — ops admin merchant search and override panel.
frontend.state_management: React Query for API data (transactions, disputes, payouts); Zustand for DisputeEvidenceUploader multi-step state (file selection, metadata, submit confirmation); Auth0 useUser hook for session across all routes.
frontend.performance_notes: RSC eliminates client JS bundle for transaction pages. Vercel ISR for static assets. React Query stale-while-revalidate for dispute list. Lazy-load evidence upload drawer. Core Web Vitals target: LCP < 2.5s, INP < 200ms on transaction history page.

backend.service_design: Hono API on ECS Fargate (2 tasks min, auto-scale to 10 on CPU 70%). Route groups: /v1/merchants/:id/transactions, /v1/merchants/:id/disputes, /v1/merchants/:id/payouts, /v1/merchants/:id/bank-accounts, /v1/admin. Auth0 JWT middleware validates token and extracts merchant_id claim for all /v1/merchants/:id routes; enforces :id === claim to prevent cross-merchant access.
backend.api_patterns: REST with OpenAPI 3.0. Pagination via cursor (created_at + id) for transaction list to support 2-year history efficiently. Pre-signed S3 URLs for evidence upload (POST /v1/merchants/:id/disputes/:disputeId/evidence/upload-url). CSV streaming response for payout downloads (no buffering in Lambda).
backend.failure_handling: Redis cache miss falls through to PostgreSQL read replica with structured log. S3 upload URL generation failure returns 503 with retry-after header. Auth0 step-up authentication failure returns 403 with challenge_required field for frontend flow branching.

security.auth_design: Auth0 Universal Login with MFA (TOTP enforced, SMS optional). Step-up authentication action triggered for POST /v1/merchants/:id/bank-accounts requiring acr_values=urn:mace:incommon:iap:silver. JWT validated in Hono middleware using Auth0 JWKS endpoint with kid verification.
security.authorization_model: merchant_owner: all portal actions including bank account changes; merchant_staff: transactions, disputes, payouts (no bank account access); ops_admin: all merchant data read/write; ops_viewer: read-only across all merchants. Role enforced in Hono middleware via Auth0 namespace claim.
security.privacy_controls: Bank account routing and account numbers AES-256 encrypted before storage using KMS key. Only last4 of account number displayed in portal. Evidence files stored in S3 with per-merchant prefix; pre-signed URLs expire in 15 minutes.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Immutable audit_events table: every bank account change, dispute submission, and payout download logged with merchant_id, user_id, action, resource_id, ip_address, auth0_session_id, timestamp. CloudTrail for AWS API calls.

data.entities: merchants (id UUID, auth0_org_id, business_name, mcc, status, risk_tier, created_at). transactions (id UUID, merchant_id FK, external_reference, amount_cents, currency, card_last4, card_brand, status ENUM authorized/captured/refunded/disputed, created_at) — composite index (merchant_id, created_at DESC). disputes (id UUID, merchant_id FK, transaction_id FK, chargeback_reason_code, dispute_amount_cents, network_deadline DATE, status ENUM open/submitted/won/lost, evidence_s3_prefix, submitted_at, created_at). payouts (id UUID, merchant_id FK, payout_amount_cents, fee_deducted_cents, net_amount_cents, bank_account_id FK, scheduled_date DATE, settled_date DATE, status). bank_accounts (id UUID, merchant_id FK, routing_number_encrypted, account_number_encrypted, account_last4, account_type ENUM checking/savings, verification_status ENUM pending/verified, created_at). audit_events (id UUID, merchant_id, user_id, action, resource_type, resource_id, ip_address, auth0_session_id, timestamp) — append-only, no updates.
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 primary (write) + read replica (analytics and transaction list queries). Redis 7: transaction list cache keyed merchant:{merchant_id}:txns:{filter_hash} TTL 60s; Auth0 session storage TTL 24h. S3: dispute evidence bucket with per-merchant prefix, versioning enabled, lifecycle policy to Glacier after 2 years.

devops.deployment_topology: Vercel for Next.js (global edge, automatic preview deployments per PR). ECS Fargate for Hono API (us-east-1a and 1c, ALB with HTTPS). RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ us-east-1. Redis ElastiCache Multi-AZ. S3 evidence bucket in us-east-1.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: TypeScript type check, ESLint, Jest (80% coverage), Playwright E2E for dispute submission and bank account update flows against staging, Docker build API image, ECR push, ECS rolling update. Vercel auto-deploys Next.js from main branch. Feature flags via LaunchDarkly for merchant cohort rollout.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM on Hono API (distributed traces). Vercel Analytics for Core Web Vitals. Custom Datadog metrics: dispute_submission_rate (counter by merchant_id), payout_download_count, bank_account_update_count. PagerDuty: API error rate > 1%, p99 > 3s.

target_output.thinking_summary: Advanced web portal for payment facilitator merchants with two key performance challenges: sub-second transaction history for 2-year ranges and real-time dispute deadline tracking. React Server Components solve the transaction performance problem by eliminating client-side data fetching for large datasets. Redis cache with merchant-scoped keys and 60-second TTL absorbs repeated loads. The dispute workflow requires storing network_deadline from the card network response, not computing it. Auth0 step-up authentication is the correct NACHA-compliant mechanism for bank account changes — not a client-side confirmation.
target_output.title: Merchant Self-Service Portal — Payment Facilitator
target_output.executive_summary: A Next.js 14 + Node.js/Hono web portal enabling small business merchants to self-serve all payment operations previously requiring support tickets: transaction history browsing across 2-year history with sub-second load times, chargeback dispute management with evidence upload and network-deadline countdown timers, payout schedule configuration and report download, and bank account management with NACHA-compliant micro-deposit verification. Built on Vercel for the frontend and ECS Fargate for the API, with PostgreSQL read replicas and Redis caching for performance at scale.
target_output.architecture_overview: Next.js 14 App Router frontend deployed to Vercel communicates with a Hono REST API on ECS Fargate behind an ALB. React Server Components fetch transaction and payout data server-side, eliminating large JSON payloads to the client browser. Redis 7 caches transaction list responses per merchant with a 60-second TTL; a PostgreSQL 16 read replica handles analytics aggregations and report generation, isolated from the OLTP write path. Auth0 with MFA secures all portal access; step-up authentication gates bank account changes per NACHA requirements. Evidence files for disputes upload directly to S3 via pre-signed URLs. Vercel Analytics tracks Core Web Vitals; Datadog APM instruments the Hono API. Merchant data isolation enforced at the API layer by comparing the URL merchant_id parameter against the Auth0 JWT claim on every request.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 14, TypeScript, App Router, React Server Components, Tailwind CSS, React Query, Zustand. Backend: Node.js 22, Hono, Drizzle ORM, Zod for request validation. Database: PostgreSQL 16 primary + read replica (RDS Multi-AZ). Cache: Redis 7 ElastiCache (Multi-AZ). Auth: Auth0 with MFA, step-up authentication, namespace custom claims. Storage: S3 for dispute evidence (pre-signed upload URLs). Hosting: Vercel (frontend), ECS Fargate (API). CI/CD: GitHub Actions, Playwright E2E. Observability: Datadog APM, Vercel Analytics, PagerDuty. Feature Flags: LaunchDarkly.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Transaction Dashboard: RSC page fetching 2-year transaction history from Redis-cached read replica query; date range filter, status filter (authorized/captured/refunded/disputed), CSV export; composite index on (merchant_id, created_at DESC) ensures sub-second response. Dispute Management: dispute list with network_deadline-driven countdown timers (card-network-specific Visa 30-day, Mastercard 45-day windows stored on dispute record); evidence upload flow using S3 pre-signed URL; submission status tracking through open/submitted/won/lost. Payout Management: payout history with fee breakdown, scheduled vs actual settlement dates; CSV/PDF download; configurable payout schedule (daily/weekly/monthly). Bank Account Management: step-up Auth0 authentication triggers on any bank account change; micro-deposit verification flow (initiate → verify two amounts → confirm); only account_last4 displayed; routing and account numbers encrypted at rest. Admin Panel: ops admin merchant search with risk_tier filter, transaction override, dispute escalation, bank account verification override.
target_output.system_components: Vercel Edge Network (Next.js frontend, global CDN, ISR for static pages), ALB with HTTPS (Hono API traffic), ECS Fargate Cluster (2-10 Hono API tasks, auto-scaled on CPU 70%), PostgreSQL 16 Primary (write path — bank accounts, dispute submissions, audit events), PostgreSQL 16 Read Replica (transaction list queries, analytics aggregations, payout report generation), Redis 7 ElastiCache Multi-AZ (transaction list cache, Auth0 session store), S3 Evidence Bucket (per-merchant prefix, versioning, Glacier lifecycle), Auth0 (Universal Login, MFA, step-up actions, org-based merchant isolation), CloudTrail (AWS API audit), Datadog Agent on ECS tasks, LaunchDarkly (feature flags for merchant cohort rollout).
target_output.workflows: Dispute Response Workflow: Merchant navigates to /(portal)/disputes, sees list with countdown timers calculated from network_deadline column. Opens a dispute, clicks 'Respond to Dispute'. Frontend requests a pre-signed S3 upload URL from POST /v1/merchants/:id/disputes/:disputeId/evidence/upload-url. Merchant uploads evidence files directly to S3 (ClamAV malware scan triggered on S3 ObjectCreated event via Lambda). Merchant fills rebuttal text, clicks Submit. Hono validates evidence S3 keys exist and scan status is clean, updates dispute status to submitted, logs audit_event, returns 200. Countdown timer freezes at zero when submitted. Bank Account Update Workflow: Merchant navigates to /(portal)/bank-accounts. Clicks 'Update Bank Account'. Auth0 step-up authentication challenge fires (requiring TOTP re-entry). On success, merchant enters new routing and account numbers. Hono API encrypts account numbers with KMS key, stores in bank_accounts with verification_status=pending. Two micro-deposits sent via NACHA ACH to merchant bank account within 1-2 days. Merchant returns to portal, enters the two amounts. Hono validates amounts against stored pending_deposit_1_cents and pending_deposit_2_cents, sets verification_status=verified, logs audit_event. Transaction History Load: RSC page hits Hono API at /v1/merchants/:id/transactions with date range and filter params. Hono checks Redis cache key merchant:{id}:txns:{filter_hash}. Cache hit returns list in < 50ms. Cache miss queries PostgreSQL read replica with index scan on (merchant_id, created_at DESC), caches result 60 seconds, returns to RSC which renders server-side HTML.
target_output.data_model: merchants (id UUID PK, auth0_org_id VARCHAR UNIQUE, business_name VARCHAR, mcc CHAR(4), status ENUM active/suspended/closed, risk_tier ENUM low/medium/high, created_at TIMESTAMP). transactions (id UUID PK, merchant_id UUID FK NOT NULL, external_reference VARCHAR UNIQUE, amount_cents BIGINT, currency CHAR(3), card_last4 CHAR(4), card_brand VARCHAR, status ENUM authorized/captured/refunded/disputed, created_at TIMESTAMP) — index (merchant_id, created_at DESC). disputes (id UUID PK, merchant_id UUID FK, transaction_id UUID FK, chargeback_reason_code VARCHAR, dispute_amount_cents BIGINT, network_deadline DATE NOT NULL, status ENUM open/submitted/won/lost, evidence_s3_prefix VARCHAR, rebuttal_text TEXT, submitted_at TIMESTAMP, created_at TIMESTAMP). payouts (id UUID PK, merchant_id UUID FK, payout_amount_cents BIGINT, fee_deducted_cents BIGINT, net_amount_cents BIGINT, bank_account_id UUID FK, scheduled_date DATE, settled_date DATE, status ENUM scheduled/settled/failed). bank_accounts (id UUID PK, merchant_id UUID FK, routing_number_encrypted BYTEA, account_number_encrypted BYTEA, account_last4 CHAR(4), account_type ENUM checking/savings, verification_status ENUM pending/verified, pending_deposit_1_cents INT, pending_deposit_2_cents INT, verified_at TIMESTAMP, created_at TIMESTAMP). audit_events (id UUID PK, merchant_id UUID, user_id VARCHAR, action VARCHAR, resource_type VARCHAR, resource_id UUID, ip_address INET, auth0_session_id VARCHAR, timestamp TIMESTAMP) — append-only table, no UPDATE or DELETE.
target_output.api_design: GET /v1/merchants/:id/transactions?from=&to=&status=&cursor= — RSC server-side fetch; auth: Auth0 JWT; read replica; cursor pagination; response cached in Redis 60s. GET /v1/merchants/:id/transactions/export — CSV streaming response; auth: Auth0 JWT; no Redis cache. GET /v1/merchants/:id/disputes — dispute list with days_remaining computed from network_deadline - now(); auth: JWT. POST /v1/merchants/:id/disputes/:disputeId/evidence/upload-url — returns pre-signed S3 PUT URL (15-min expiry) and upload_id; auth: JWT. PATCH /v1/merchants/:id/disputes/:disputeId/submit — validates evidence uploaded and scan clean, transitions status; auth: JWT; writes audit_event. GET /v1/merchants/:id/payouts — payout history; auth: JWT; read replica. GET /v1/merchants/:id/payouts/:payoutId/report — streams CSV payout detail; auth: JWT. POST /v1/merchants/:id/bank-accounts — requires Auth0 step-up acr claim; encrypts and stores; initiates micro-deposits; auth: JWT + step-up. POST /v1/merchants/:id/bank-accounts/:accountId/verify — validates micro-deposit amounts; sets verified; auth: JWT.
target_output.security_and_compliance: PCI-DSS Level 2: no card PANs stored in portal DB (only card_last4 and card_brand from processor feed); bank account routing and account numbers AES-256 encrypted at rest with KMS CMK; quarterly vulnerability scans via AWS Inspector. NACHA: micro-deposit verification mandatory before first ACH debit; Auth0 step-up authentication (acr=urn:mace:incommon:iap:silver) required for all bank account changes. GDPR: EU merchant personal data (business contact email, name) deletable via /v1/merchants/:id/gdpr-erasure — transaction records exempt as financial records. Auth0 MFA enforced for all roles; step-up TOTP for bank account mutations. Merchant data isolation: Hono middleware asserts URL :id === auth0_merchant_id JWT claim on all /v1/merchants/:id routes; 403 on mismatch. Evidence malware scanning: Lambda on S3 ObjectCreated event scans with ClamAV; sets S3 object tag scan_status=clean or infected; Hono rejects submissions with non-clean tag. Immutable audit_events table enforced via PostgreSQL row security policy preventing UPDATE and DELETE.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions pipeline: TypeScript type check, ESLint, Jest unit tests (80% coverage), Playwright E2E tests for dispute submission flow and bank account step-up flow against staging, Docker build Hono image, ECR push, ECS rolling update (min 50% healthy). Vercel auto-deploys Next.js from main branch push; preview deployments for every PR. Feature flags: LaunchDarkly controls merchant cohort rollout (start at 5% of new merchants, ramp to 100% over 4 weeks). ECS Fargate auto-scaling: target tracking CPU 70%, min 2 tasks, max 10 tasks. RDS Multi-AZ automatic failover. Redis ElastiCache Multi-AZ replication. Database migrations: Drizzle Kit migrations run as ECS one-off task in pre-deploy step. S3 evidence bucket: versioning enabled, lifecycle policy moves objects to S3 Glacier after 2 years.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM: distributed traces on all Hono API routes with merchant_id as span tag. Core Web Vitals via Vercel Analytics: LCP, INP, CLS tracked by page (target LCP < 2.5s on transaction history). Custom Datadog metrics: portal.dispute.submission_count (counter by merchant_id), portal.dispute.evidence_upload_duration_ms (histogram), portal.bank_account.update_count, portal.transaction.cache_hit_rate (Redis hit ratio). PagerDuty escalation: API error rate > 1% for 5 minutes (P1), p99 API latency > 3s (P2), dispute submission failures > 5 in 10 minutes (P1 — regulatory deadline risk), Redis ElastiCache failover (P2).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $2,800/month for 10,000 active merchants. ECS Fargate (2-10 tasks, 2 vCPU each): ~$800 variable. RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ (db.r6g.large): ~$900. Redis ElastiCache Multi-AZ (cache.r7g.medium): ~$350. Vercel Pro: ~$200. S3 + CloudFront: ~$150. Datadog APM: ~$400. LaunchDarkly: free tier for < 1,000 seats. Scaling above 50,000 merchants: add second read replica dedicated to analytics, shard Redis by merchant_id prefix for transaction cache, evaluate Vercel Enterprise for edge caching of RSC payloads.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (weeks 1-6): Auth0 login with MFA, transaction history RSC page with Redis caching, dispute list with countdown timers (network_deadline-based), evidence upload via S3 pre-signed URLs, dispute submission workflow, audit_events logging, Datadog APM, Playwright E2E tests. Phase 2 (weeks 7-12): Bank account management with Auth0 step-up and NACHA micro-deposit verification, payout history and CSV download, payout schedule configuration, Core Web Vitals monitoring via Vercel Analytics, LaunchDarkly feature flags for cohort rollout. Phase 3 (weeks 13-20): Ops admin panel for merchant search and dispute escalation, cash flow projection, GDPR erasure endpoint, fee schedule viewer, processing limits display, advanced analytics materialized views.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode with no-explicit-any. Drizzle ORM for type-safe PostgreSQL queries; never raw SQL in API handlers. Redis cache keys must be merchant-scoped — lint rule enforcing cache key prefix includes merchant_id. Auth0 JWT claim for merchant_id must be validated in Hono middleware before any DB access — never trust URL parameter alone. Playwright E2E tests required for dispute submission and bank account update flows in CI. Evidence upload flow tested with clean and malware-tagged S3 object fixtures. GDPR erasure endpoint tested to confirm transaction records are not deleted, only contact data.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Redis cache key collision across merchants causes data leakage. Mitigation: structured cache key format merchant:{merchant_id}:txns:{filter_hash} enforced by typed cache wrapper; penetration test includes cross-merchant cache probe. Risk: dispute submission fails silently when S3 scan Lambda has not yet tagged the object. Mitigation: Hono polls object tag with 3-second retry up to 10 attempts before returning 503 with retry_after header. Risk: Auth0 step-up authentication causes high abandonment rate for bank account updates. Mitigation: UX copy explains the security reason for re-authentication; TOTP prompt pre-filled with last used method. Tradeoff: Vercel for Next.js vs self-hosted on ECS — Vercel adds monthly cost but eliminates RSC deployment complexity and provides global edge CDN with ISR without manual configuration.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Chose Auth0 step-up authentication over a separate password confirmation dialog for NACHA compliance — a UI-only confirmation does not satisfy the NACHA requirement for multi-factor re-verification. S3 pre-signed URLs chosen for evidence uploads over direct Hono proxy to avoid buffering large files in ECS tasks. PostgreSQL read replica confirmed over a dedicated analytics DB to keep architecture simple at current merchant scale.

metadata.notes: Moderate quality: strong on transaction performance and dispute workflow detail; cost_and_scaling and development_guidelines are adequate but slightly thinner than top-tier rows.

profile.domain: Payment Gateway SDK Developer Documentation Hub

project_goal.value: Replace an outdated HTML/PHP documentation site for a payment gateway SDK with a statically generated developer hub featuring interactive API explorers, versioned docs, code samples in 8 languages, and a changelog
project_goal.rationale: Current PHP site is slow, unmaintained, and lacks the interactive API explorer integration engineers need to evaluate the SDK

target_users.value: Integration engineers at prospective merchant businesses evaluating and implementing the payment gateway SDK
target_users.rationale: Primary audience is technical: developers reading API reference, copying code samples, and testing sandbox calls

project_class.rationale: Pure static generation with no server-side runtime; all content compiled at build time

capabilities.rationale: Static frontend delivery with search, versioned navigation, interactive API explorer, and analytics for developer engagement tracking

complexity_level.rationale: No backend services; complexity limited to build pipeline, content authoring workflow, and API explorer client-side component

risk_level.rationale: Public documentation with no sensitive data; no authentication required

data_sensitivity.rationale: Documentation site contains no user data, no PII, no financial data

external_exposure.rationale: Publicly accessible documentation site targeting all integration engineers on the internet

access_model.value: Fully public — no authentication required for documentation; sandbox API explorer uses caller-provided sandbox API key, never stored server-side
access_model.rationale: Documentation must be publicly accessible to reduce friction for SDK evaluation

feature_scope.value: Versioned documentation by SDK major version, interactive API explorer with sandbox credential input, code samples in Python, JavaScript, Ruby, PHP, Java, Go, C#, and curl, changelog page, full-text search via Pagefind, dark mode
feature_scope.rationale: Full developer hub feature set for SDK evaluation and implementation reference

mvp_scope.value: SDK v3 documentation, 8-language code samples, static API reference, changelog, Pagefind search, CloudFront deployment
mvp_scope.rationale: MVP delivers the core reference content before adding interactive API explorer

security_baseline.value: HTTPS enforced via CloudFront; HSTS header; CSP disallowing inline scripts except for API explorer sandbox calls; no server-side code; sandbox API keys never persisted beyond browser session storage
security_baseline.rationale: Static site with no backend; primary security controls are transport and CSP to protect against XSS in API explorer

frontend_stack.value: Astro 4 with TypeScript; MDX for documentation content; Tailwind CSS for styling
frontend_stack.rationale: Astro's island architecture emits zero JS for documentation pages and selective hydration only for the API explorer component

backend_stack.value: None — pure static site generation; no server-side runtime
backend_stack.rationale: static_website class; Astro builds to static HTML/CSS/JS only

data_platform.value: None — content stored as MDX files in Git; no database
data_platform.rationale: Documentation content managed as versioned Markdown in the monorepo

hosting_target.value: AWS S3 for static assets; CloudFront CDN for global delivery with edge caching
hosting_target.rationale: S3 + CloudFront is the standard AWS static hosting pattern; no server needed

privacy_retention_policy.value: No personal data collected; CloudFront access logs retained 90 days for CDN diagnostics; no analytics cookies without consent banner
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: GDPR consent requirements apply to analytics tracking cookies even on public documentation sites

future_scope.value: SDK v4 documentation, community-contributed code examples via PR workflow, AI-assisted API reference search
future_scope.rationale: Versioned docs structure enables v4 addition; community contributions extend the sample library

constraints.value: No server-side runtime on hosting infrastructure; sandbox API keys must not be persisted beyond browser session; site must load in under 2 seconds on 4G mobile
constraints.rationale: static_website class constraint; security requirement for sandbox credentials; performance SLA for developer experience

observability_baseline.value: AWS CloudWatch for CloudFront distribution metrics (request rate, error rate, cache hit ratio); Plausible Analytics (cookieless) for page view tracking and documentation section engagement
observability_baseline.rationale: CloudFront native metrics for CDN health; Plausible for privacy-respecting developer engagement analytics without GDPR consent banner

execution_preference.value: Content-first delivery: docs writers author MDX in parallel with engineering build pipeline setup; deploy iteratively as sections are completed
execution_preference.rationale: Documentation content and build infrastructure can be developed in parallel

project.goal: Statically generated payment gateway SDK documentation hub with interactive API explorer and versioned docs
project.target_users: Integration engineers evaluating and implementing the payment gateway SDK
project.timeline: MVP in 6 weeks; interactive API explorer in week 10

frontend.content: MDX files for all documentation pages with frontmatter metadata
frontend.components: API explorer as an Astro island (React component, hydrated client-side only)

security.transport: HTTPS via CloudFront; HSTS; strict CSP
security.sandbox_keys: Session storage only, never persisted to server

constraints.no_backend: No server-side code on hosting infrastructure

open_questions.api_explorer_cors: Confirm sandbox API base URL supports CORS for browser-origin requests

confirmed_decisions.search: Pagefind (client-side static search index built at CI time)

reasoner_reviews.product: The versioned docs structure is the most important architectural decision — getting it right unlocks easy SDK v4 addition without restructuring the site. Recommend version selector in the top nav that switches URL prefix (/v3/ vs /v4/) while keeping the same layout. Changelog page is often the first stop for returning integrators; ensure it is prominently linked.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Astro island architecture is the correct choice — documentation pages should ship zero JavaScript to the browser; only the API explorer component needs client-side hydration. Pagefind static search is appropriate: it runs at build time and ships a pre-built index to the CDN with no server dependency. CloudFront cache invalidation strategy must be planned carefully — invalidating /* on every deploy is costly; use S3 versioned filenames from Astro's built-in asset hashing instead and only invalidate the HTML files.
reasoner_reviews.security: The API explorer must enforce a strict CSP that allows fetch() only to the documented sandbox API base URL domain. Session storage for sandbox API keys is acceptable — explicitly document that developers must not use production keys in the explorer. The S3 bucket must block all public access directly; access must only be via CloudFront OAC (Origin Access Control), not OAI.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 2-second 4G mobile load target is achievable with Astro's zero-JS documentation pages. Ensure the Pagefind search index is lazy-loaded on search interaction, not on page load, to avoid blocking the initial document render. Code sample syntax highlighting should use a CSS-based highlighter (Shiki) at build time, not a client-side library.

backend.service_design: No backend service. GitHub Actions build pipeline serves as the only processing layer: Astro build compiles MDX to static HTML, Pagefind indexes the output directory, assets are uploaded to S3.
backend.api_patterns: No server API. API explorer makes client-side fetch() calls directly from the browser to the payment gateway sandbox API endpoint using the developer-supplied sandbox key stored in sessionStorage.

security.auth_design: No authentication. Public documentation with no login required.
security.abuse_prevention: CloudFront rate limiting via AWS WAF managed rule set (prevents scraping abuse). CSP header: default-src self; script-src self; connect-src https://sandbox.paymentgateway.io — restricts API explorer fetch to sandbox domain only. S3 bucket policy allows only CloudFront OAC principal, blocking direct S3 URL access.
security.privacy_controls: No personal data stored. Plausible analytics is cookieless and GDPR-compliant by design — no consent banner needed. CloudFront access logs anonymize IP addresses (last octet zeroed) before retention.

devops.deployment_topology: S3 bucket (production prefix) behind CloudFront distribution. Route53 A record pointing to CloudFront. ACM certificate for docs.paymentgateway.io in us-east-1 (required for CloudFront). GitHub Actions deploys via AWS CLI s3 sync.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions on pull_request: Astro build, internal link check (astro-link-checker), API reference validation (custom script verifying all endpoint examples match the OpenAPI spec), upload to s3://docs-bucket/preview/{pr-number}/, post PR comment with preview URL. On push to main: same build + checks, upload to s3://docs-bucket/production/, CloudFront invalidation of HTML files only (/*.html and /v*/index.html patterns).
devops.observability_stack: CloudWatch metrics on CloudFront distribution: Requests (by status code), CacheHitRate, BytesDownloaded. CloudWatch Alarm: 5xx error rate > 0.5% for 5 minutes (SNS to engineering Slack). Plausible Analytics: page views by doc section (/v3/api-reference/*, /v3/guides/*), search query tracking, API explorer usage events (custom goal).
devops.cost_controls: CloudFront caching maximized: HTML cache-control max-age=300, hashed assets max-age=31536000. Estimated $30/month at 500K monthly page views (CloudFront data transfer + S3 storage + Route53).

frontend.app_structure: Astro 4 static documentation site with MDX content; versioned docs via route prefix (/v1, /v2, /v3); Pagefind for client-side search
frontend.pages_and_flows: Getting Started, Versioned API Reference, Interactive API Explorer (sandbox), Code Example Library, Changelog, Migration Guides
frontend.ui_modules: VersionSwitcher dropdown, CodeBlock with syntax highlighting and copy button, APIExplorer sandbox island, SearchModal
frontend.performance_notes: Pagefind search index loaded on demand; code blocks server-rendered with Shiki; versioned assets cached by CDN with long TTLs
frontend.frontend_risks: Interactive API explorer sends real requests to sandbox; must display clear 'sandbox only' labels and never auto-submit with production keys

ISS-001.title: CloudFront invalidation strategy invalidates all paths on every deploy
ISS-001.detail: Previous plan used CloudFront CreateInvalidation with /* on every GitHub Actions deploy, incurring $1/1000 paths cost and causing a CDN cache miss storm for all users immediately after deployment, degrading performance temporarily.

history.note: Identified in round 2 audit — no change made
Round 3 attempted fix mentioned 'selective invalidation' in deployment text but CI/CD section still showed aws cloudfront create-invalidation --paths /*

ISS-002.title: Pagefind search index loaded on page load rather than on search interaction
ISS-002.detail: Previous plan loaded the Pagefind JS bundle unconditionally in the HTML head, adding 180KB to the initial page load and blocking 2-second mobile load target. Should be loaded lazily on first search interaction.

history.note: Acknowledged in round 3 but implementation detail not updated

focus_issues.title: CloudFront invalidation strategy invalidates all paths on every deploy
focus_issues.detail: /* invalidation causes cost and performance regression post-deploy; should only invalidate HTML files while hashed assets are served with long-lived cache-control.

focus_issues.title: Pagefind search index loaded on page load rather than on search interaction
focus_issues.detail: Pagefind JS bundle added to HTML head unconditionally; lazy load on first search focus/click instead.

latest_recommendations: Replace /* CloudFront invalidation with targeted /*.html and /v*/index.html pattern invalidation; use hashed filenames for all assets to leverage max-age=31536000
Load Pagefind bundle via dynamic import() triggered on search input focus event, not in HTML head

issue_updates.detail: CI/CD section still contains /* despite fix report claim

previous_plan.title: Payment Gateway SDK Developer Hub — Round 3 Plan
previous_plan.executive_summary: Astro 4 static site with MDX content, Pagefind search, and interactive API explorer deployed to S3+CloudFront. Versioned docs structure for SDK v3 and v4.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Pure static generation with Astro island architecture. No backend runtime. S3+CloudFront hosting. GitHub Actions CI for build, link check, and deploy.
previous_plan.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions on push to main deploys to S3 and runs aws cloudfront create-invalidation --paths /* (unfixed). Pagefind loaded in layout head unconditionally (unfixed).
