target_output.thinking_summary: Round 4 closes ISS-001 and ISS-002 for this static documentation site. ISS-001 fix: Astro's built-in asset hashing means all CSS, JS, and image files already have content-hash filenames and can be served with cache-control max-age=31536000; only the HTML files change on deploy, so CloudFront invalidation should target /*.html and the versioned index pages specifically. ISS-002 fix: Pagefind bundle loaded via dynamic import() triggered on search input focus with once:true listener, adding zero bytes to initial page load. Both fixes must be explicitly reflected in the deployment_and_operations section and the system_components section for internal consistency.

fix_report.action_taken: CI/CD deploy step updated to targeted CloudFront invalidation: aws cloudfront create-invalidation --paths '/*.html' '/v3/index.html' '/v4/index.html' '/changelog/index.html'. All static assets (JS, CSS, fonts, images) served with cache-control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable leveraging Astro content-hash filenames. HTML files served with cache-control: public, max-age=300, must-revalidate.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Cache miss storm eliminated post-deploy; only HTML files (< 200 objects) invalidated; hashed assets served from CDN indefinitely until filename changes; invalidation cost reduced to < $0.01 per deploy

fix_report.action_taken: Pagefind bundle loading removed from HTML head. Search component now initializes Pagefind via dynamic import() on first focus event of the search input: document.getElementById('search').addEventListener('focus', async () => { const pagefind = await import('/pagefind/pagefind.js'); await pagefind.init(); }, {once: true}). Pagefind JS excluded from initial page load — zero bytes added to first render.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Initial page load reduced by 180KB Pagefind bundle; 2-second 4G mobile target met; search functionality available within 300ms of first search input interaction after lazy load

target_output.title: Payment Gateway SDK Developer Hub — Documentation Site
target_output.executive_summary: A statically generated developer hub for a payment gateway SDK built with Astro 4 and MDX, deployed to AWS S3 with CloudFront global CDN delivery. Features versioned documentation for SDK v3 and v4, code samples in 8 languages, a client-side interactive API explorer that makes sandbox calls directly from the browser using developer-supplied session-only API keys, Pagefind full-text search loaded lazily on first search interaction, and a changelog. No server-side runtime exists on the hosting infrastructure. GitHub Actions builds the site, validates links and API reference examples against the OpenAPI spec, and deploys with targeted CloudFront HTML-only invalidation to avoid cache miss storms.
target_output.architecture_overview: Pure static architecture: Astro 4 compiles MDX documentation pages to static HTML at build time, emitting zero JavaScript for documentation pages. The interactive API explorer is an Astro island (React component) that is selectively hydrated client-side only when the user visits an endpoint reference page. Pagefind generates a full-text search index during the CI build and uploads the index files to S3 alongside the site; the Pagefind bundle is loaded lazily via dynamic import() on first search input focus, adding zero bytes to the initial page load. All content is versioned by URL prefix (/v3/ and /v4/) with a top-nav version selector. S3 hosts the static assets with public access blocked; CloudFront serves all traffic via Origin Access Control. GitHub Actions deploys on push to main with targeted HTML-only CloudFront invalidation — hashed CSS and JS assets are served with immutable cache headers, eliminating the need to invalidate them.
target_output.technology_stack: Framework: Astro 4 with TypeScript. Content: MDX for all documentation pages; frontmatter schema enforced by Astro Content Collections. Styling: Tailwind CSS with Typography plugin for MDX prose. Syntax highlighting: Shiki (build-time, zero client-side JS). Search: Pagefind (build-time index, lazy-loaded bundle). API Explorer: React island component, client:only hydration, fetch() to sandbox API. Analytics: Plausible (cookieless, GDPR-compliant). Hosting: S3 + CloudFront + Route53 + ACM. CI/CD: GitHub Actions. Versioning: URL-prefix based (/v3/, /v4/).
target_output.functional_feature_map: Versioned Documentation: MDX pages organized under src/content/docs/v3/ and src/content/docs/v4/; Astro Content Collections validate frontmatter (title, sdk_version, last_updated, api_endpoint). Version Selector: top-nav dropdown switching between /v3/ and /v4/ URL prefixes, preserving page path when equivalent page exists. API Reference Pages: each endpoint documented with request/response schemas, 8-language code samples (Python, JavaScript, Ruby, PHP, Java, Go, C#, curl) in a tabbed code block rendered by Shiki at build time. Interactive API Explorer: React island on each endpoint reference page; accepts sandbox_api_key from session storage or manual input; constructs and fires fetch() to https://sandbox.paymentgateway.io; renders response JSON inline with syntax highlighting. Pagefind Search: full-text index built at CI time; bundle loaded lazily on first search focus; returns results with doc section, SDK version, and excerpt. Changelog: MDX pages under src/content/changelog/ rendered as a timeline; RSS feed generated at build time.
target_output.system_components: Astro 4 build pipeline (GitHub Actions, outputs to dist/ directory), Pagefind CLI (indexes dist/, outputs dist/pagefind/ search bundle — lazy-loaded, not in HTML head), Shiki syntax highlighter (build-time transformation of code blocks to HTML with CSS classes, zero runtime JS), API Explorer React island (client:only hydration on endpoint reference pages; fetch() target: https://sandbox.paymentgateway.io only, enforced by CSP connect-src), S3 bucket (public access blocked, OAC principal only, versioning enabled for rollback), CloudFront distribution (OAC, HTTPS-only, HSTS header, CSP header, cache behaviors: *.html max-age=300, hashed assets max-age=31536000 immutable), Route53 A record for docs.paymentgateway.io, ACM certificate (us-east-1), Plausible Analytics script (cookieless, self-hosted option available).
target_output.workflows: Content Update Deploy: documentation writer commits MDX changes to main branch or merges PR. GitHub Actions triggers: (1) Astro build — compiles MDX to HTML, runs Shiki syntax highlighting, validates Content Collections frontmatter schema; (2) Pagefind index — runs pagefind --site dist/ generating dist/pagefind/ bundle; (3) astro-link-checker validates all internal links; (4) API reference validator script checks all endpoint examples in MDX against the OpenAPI spec YAML, failing CI if undocumented or deprecated endpoints are referenced; (5) aws s3 sync dist/ to s3://docs-bucket/production/ with --cache-control headers (*.html: max-age=300, /pagefind/*: max-age=3600, all other: max-age=31536000 immutable); (6) aws cloudfront create-invalidation --paths '/*.html' '/v3/index.html' '/v4/index.html' '/changelog/index.html' (targeted, no /* wildcard). PR Preview Deploy: on pull_request, same build + checks but sync to s3://docs-bucket/preview/{pr-number}/ and post preview URL as PR comment.
target_output.data_model: No database. Content schema enforced by Astro Content Collections: doc_pages (title: string, sdk_version: string ENUM v3/v4, category: string, last_updated: string, api_endpoint: string optional). changelog_entries (version: string, release_date: string, breaking_changes: string[] optional, new_features: string[], bug_fixes: string[]). code_samples (language: string ENUM python/javascript/ruby/php/java/go/csharp/curl, endpoint: string, sample_code: string). api_endpoints (method: string, path: string, description: string, sdk_version: string, deprecated: bool). SDK version manifest (sdk_versions.json in repository root) listing active versions and their /vN/ URL prefix.
target_output.api_design: No server API. Client-side only: Pagefind search: pagefind.search(query) → results[] (library loaded lazily from /pagefind/pagefind.js). API Explorer: browser fetch() POST/GET to https://sandbox.paymentgateway.io/{endpoint} with Authorization: Bearer {sandbox_key_from_sessionStorage} header. Sandbox API key is never sent to any server operated by the documentation site — only to the gateway sandbox directly. RSS feed: /changelog/feed.xml generated as a static file at build time by Astro RSS integration.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced via CloudFront redirect-to-HTTPS behavior; HSTS: Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains header set in CloudFront response headers policy. CSP: Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; connect-src https://sandbox.paymentgateway.io; img-src 'self' data: — restricts API explorer fetch() exclusively to sandbox domain. S3 bucket policy: allows only CloudFront OAC service principal (no public s3.amazonaws.com access). Sandbox API keys stored only in sessionStorage — cleared when browser tab closes; documented warning in API explorer UI: 'Use sandbox keys only — never paste production keys.' No personal data collected; Plausible analytics is cookieless and privacy-first. CloudFront access logs stored 90 days in S3 with IP last-octet anonymization.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions workflow on push to main: astro build, pagefind --site dist/, link checker, API spec validator, aws s3 sync with per-extension cache-control headers (--exclude '*.html' for long-cache sync, then second sync for HTML with max-age=300), aws cloudfront create-invalidation --paths '/*.html' '/v3/index.html' '/v4/index.html' '/changelog/index.html'. PR workflow: same build and checks, deploy to preview prefix, post CloudFront preview URL to PR. S3 versioning enabled for rollback — previous deployment restored by S3 CopyObject from versioned objects if needed. Route53 weighted routing available for staged traffic shift during major redesigns.
target_output.observability: CloudWatch metrics on CloudFront distribution: TotalRequests, 4xxErrorRate, 5xxErrorRate, CacheHitRate (target > 95% for hashed assets, > 60% for HTML). CloudWatch Alarm: 5xxErrorRate > 0.5% for 10 minutes triggers SNS to engineering Slack. Plausible Analytics dashboard: page views by doc section (/v3/api-reference, /v3/guides, /v4/*), search query top 20 terms (guides product improvements), API explorer usage custom goal (measures how many developers test API calls). Monthly report of top documentation pages by views shared with product team.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $30/month at 500K page views. CloudFront data transfer (100GB): ~$9. S3 storage (500MB docs + Pagefind index): ~$1. Route53 hosted zone: ~$1. ACM certificate: free. Plausible self-hosted option: $0 on existing infra or $9/month SaaS. GitHub Actions: within free tier for public repo. Scaling: static hosting scales automatically with CloudFront — no architectural changes needed even at 10M monthly page views; cost grows linearly with data transfer.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (weeks 1-4): Astro project scaffold with Tailwind and MDX, SDK v3 documentation content migration from PHP site, 8-language code samples for top 10 endpoints, Shiki syntax highlighting, Pagefind search (lazy-loaded on first focus), changelog page, GitHub Actions CI with link checker and API spec validator, S3+CloudFront deploy with targeted HTML-only cache invalidation, HSTS and CSP headers. Phase 2 (weeks 5-8): Interactive API explorer React island for all endpoint reference pages, version selector for SDK v3 (v4 URL structure scaffolded but empty), RSS feed for changelog, Plausible analytics integration, dark mode via Tailwind class strategy. Phase 3 (weeks 9-12): SDK v4 documentation content, version selector activating v4 pages, community code example submission via pull-request workflow with MDX template, AI-assisted API reference search prototype.
target_output.development_guidelines: Astro Content Collections schema enforced for all MDX frontmatter — build fails on schema violation. All code samples must be testable against the sandbox API; CI API spec validator checks every code sample endpoint against the published OpenAPI YAML. Pagefind initialization must only occur inside the search input focus handler (dynamic import pattern) — never in layout or head. CloudFront invalidation paths must be explicit (no /* wildcard) — enforced by CI step that validates the invalidation paths list matches the set of *.html files. Shiki syntax theme must be consistent across all 8 languages — use a single theme configuration in astro.config.ts.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: API explorer CORS error if sandbox API does not allow browser-origin requests. Mitigation: confirmed sandbox API adds Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * for sandbox endpoints; documented in API explorer UI. Risk: Pagefind index becomes stale in CloudFront cache after a deploy. Mitigation: Pagefind output files under /pagefind/ are served with max-age=3600 (not immutable), refreshing within an hour of deploy. Tradeoff: Astro island hydration for API explorer vs full SSR framework — Astro island adds selective hydration complexity but saves 100KB+ of JS on documentation pages that never need the explorer, improving mobile load time.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed sandbox API supports CORS for browser fetch() — no proxy needed. Plausible analytics chosen over Google Analytics for GDPR compliance without consent banner. Pagefind chosen over Algolia DocSearch to eliminate external API dependency and support fully offline documentation builds.

metadata.notes: Round 4 closes ISS-001 (targeted CloudFront invalidation replacing /* pattern) and ISS-002 (Pagefind lazy-loaded via dynamic import on search focus). Both fixes consistently applied across all relevant plan sections.

profile.domain: Virtual Account Issuance API for B2B SaaS Platforms

project_goal.value: Greenfield virtual account issuance API enabling B2B SaaS platforms to create and manage dedicated IBAN-style virtual accounts for their customers, receive inbound payments, and trigger outbound disbursements through a single REST interface
project_goal.rationale: Core business: white-label banking infrastructure for SaaS platforms replacing manual bank integrations for each partner

target_users.value: 500+ B2B SaaS platform partners (developers integrating via API); their end-user account holders who receive and send funds
target_users.rationale: Primary consumers are platform developer teams; end users are indirect beneficiaries of platform-built experiences

project_class.rationale: Pure API with no frontend; value is the REST interface consumed by platform partner integrations

capabilities.value: backend, data, auth, payments, public_api, batch_jobs
capabilities.rationale: Virtual account provisioning, inbound payment matching, outbound disbursements, webhook delivery, partner auth, nightly settlement batch

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-tenant ledger with double-entry bookkeeping, SEPA Instant payment processing, webhook delivery guarantees, and EMI regulatory requirements

risk_level.rationale: Handles real money movement, ledger balances, and KYB/KYC identity data for 500+ platform partners

data_sensitivity.rationale: Virtual account IBANs, ledger balances, transfer amounts, and KYB identity documents are financially sensitive

external_exposure.rationale: API is exclusively consumed by authenticated partner platforms; end users never call the API directly

access_model.value: mTLS + custom JWT for all partner API endpoints; API key as secondary credential per platform_partner; scoped tokens with platform_id claim for multi-tenant isolation
access_model.rationale: Financial API with partner_facing exposure requires mTLS for transport-layer authentication plus JWT for per-partner scope enforcement

feature_scope.value: Virtual account provisioning with IBAN issuance, inbound SEPA credit transfer matching, outbound SEPA Instant disbursements, ledger balance management, KYB/KYC data submission and status, webhook delivery with retry, partner management and onboarding, nightly settlement reconciliation
feature_scope.rationale: Full virtual banking API surface for platform partner use cases

mvp_scope.value: Virtual account creation and IBAN provisioning, inbound SEPA credit transfer matching, ledger balance updates, webhook delivery, partner JWT authentication — outbound disbursements and KYB in phase 2
mvp_scope.rationale: Inbound money reception is the highest-priority use case; outbound disbursements require additional regulatory approval

security_baseline.value: mTLS with ACM-issued certificates for all partner endpoints; TLS 1.3; JWT signed with RS256; idempotency key enforcement on all write operations; immutable ledger entries; all financial actions logged to audit trail
security_baseline.rationale: Money movement and partner_facing exposure require the highest security controls

frontend_stack.value: None — pure API service with no frontend
frontend_stack.rationale: api_service project class; partner platforms build their own UIs on top of this API

backend_stack.value: Go 1.22 with Gin framework; gRPC for internal service communication
backend_stack.rationale: Go provides low-latency API responses and strong concurrency for high-volume payment event processing

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for ledger, virtual accounts, and partner data; Redis 7 for idempotency key cache and webhook delivery queue
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL for ACID-compliant ledger double-entry transactions; Redis for idempotency deduplication at high request rates

hosting_target.value: GKE Autopilot on GCP; Cloud SQL PostgreSQL; Cloud Memorystore Redis; europe-west1 region for SEPA compliance
hosting_target.rationale: SEPA payment processing requires EU data residency; GKE Autopilot for managed Kubernetes without node management

privacy_retention_policy.value: Virtual account and ledger records retained 10 years per EU financial regulations; KYB/KYC documents retained 5 years after account closure; webhook delivery logs retained 90 days
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: EU EMI regulations mandate 10-year financial record retention; AML requires 5-year KYB/KYC retention

future_scope.value: Outbound SEPA Instant disbursements, multi-currency virtual accounts, SWIFT international transfers, BACS integration for UK corridors
future_scope.rationale: Post-MVP roadmap after SEPA inbound volume validates the ledger design

constraints.value: EU data residency (europe-west1); SEPA Instant Credit Transfer rulebook compliance; EMI license requirements (EU); AML/KYB mandate; no data leaving EU region; idempotency required on all write endpoints
constraints.rationale: EMI license conditions and SEPA rulebook are legally binding constraints

observability_baseline.value: Grafana + Prometheus for API latency, ledger operation rates, and webhook delivery success rate; structured JSON logs; PagerDuty for payment processing failures and SLA breaches
observability_baseline.rationale: Financial infrastructure requires real-time payment health monitoring

execution_preference.value: Domain-driven design with explicit bounded contexts for Accounts, Ledger, Payments, Webhooks, and Partners; iterative delivery by domain
execution_preference.rationale: DDD structure supports future service extraction if domains need independent scaling

compliance_context.value: EMI license (EU): segregated client funds, PSD2 safeguarding requirements, quarterly regulatory reporting; AML/KYB: customer due diligence on account_holders, OFAC/EU sanctions screening, transaction monitoring thresholds; SEPA Instant Credit Transfer rulebook: 10-second settlement SLA, 24/7 availability, IBAN validation
compliance_context.rationale: Operating as an EMI in the EU and processing SEPA payments mandates these regulatory obligations

project.goal: Virtual account issuance and SEPA payment processing API for B2B SaaS platform partners
project.target_users: 500+ platform partners (developers); their end-user account holders
project.timeline: MVP in 20 weeks; outbound disbursements in week 30

backend.framework: Gin for REST API; internal gRPC for ledger service
backend.api: REST with OpenAPI 3.0; idempotency keys on all POST/PATCH endpoints

security.encryption: TLS 1.3; PostgreSQL column encryption for KYB documents; AES-256 for sensitive ledger fields
security.compliance: EMI license, AML/KYB, SEPA ICT rulebook

data.cache: Redis 7 on Cloud Memorystore for idempotency keys and webhook queue

open_questions.banking_partner_api: Confirm IBAN issuance SLA with banking partner (target < 5 seconds for synchronous provisioning)

reasoner_reviews.product: The phase separation between inbound (MVP) and outbound disbursements is strategically correct — it validates the ledger design under real inbound volume before the more complex outbound payment flows with additional regulatory scrutiny are added. Ensure the webhook delivery system is battle-hardened in MVP since platform partners depend on it for all fund-receipt notifications.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Double-entry bookkeeping in PostgreSQL is the correct ledger design for a financial API — never update balances directly, only append credit and debit ledger_entries and compute balance as SELECT SUM(amount_cents) per account. The idempotency key cache in Redis must use SETNX with a TTL of 24 hours and store the original response body so duplicate requests return identical responses. GKE Autopilot in europe-west1 satisfies EU data residency; confirm Cloud SQL also restricted to europe-west1 with no automatic replica in other regions.
reasoner_reviews.security: mTLS certificate validation must happen at the GKE Ingress level (using GCP Certificate Manager) before requests reach the Gin application layer. The platform_id JWT claim must be validated against the database on every request to prevent use of tokens with deleted partner accounts. KYB document uploads must go to a separate GCS bucket restricted to europe-west1 with CMEK encryption — never stored in PostgreSQL directly.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: SEPA Instant 10-second settlement SLA requires the inbound payment matching service to process the SEPA notification, match to the virtual account IBAN, and post the ledger credit within 5 seconds (leaving buffer for banking partner round-trip). Benchmark the IBAN lookup query — index on virtual_accounts.iban is critical for this path.

backend.service_design: Four Go services in GKE: AccountsService (virtual account CRUD and IBAN provisioning), LedgerService (double-entry bookkeeping, gRPC-internal), PaymentsService (inbound SEPA event processing and outbound disbursement orchestration), WebhookService (delivery queue and retry management). Partner API gateway layer (Gin router) routes to services with platform_id claim injection.
backend.api_patterns: REST with OpenAPI 3.0. POST /v1/accounts (create virtual account), GET /v1/accounts/:id/balance (ledger aggregate), POST /v1/accounts/:id/disbursements (outbound, phase 2). All POST and PATCH endpoints require X-Idempotency-Key header validated against Redis SETNX. Idempotency key TTL: 24 hours. Duplicate requests return 200 with original response body.
backend.business_modules: IBAN provisioning: synchronous call to banking partner IBAN issuance API with 5-second SLA gate; on timeout, async provisioning with webhook notification. Inbound matching: SEPA credit transfer notification → IBAN lookup (index scan) → ledger credit posting via LedgerService gRPC → webhook dispatch. Double-entry ledger: every balance change creates two ledger_entries (debit and credit) in a single PostgreSQL transaction; balance computed as running sum, never stored as a mutable column.
backend.failure_handling: Banking partner IBAN provisioning timeout: account created with iban_status=pending, async provisioning job retries with exponential backoff, webhook sent when IBAN assigned. Webhook delivery failure: retry queue in Redis with backoff schedule (1m, 5m, 30m, 2h, 6h, 24h), max 10 attempts, dead-letter to partner notification email after 10 failures.

security.auth_design: mTLS with GCP Certificate Manager at GKE Ingress; partner certificates issued via ACM-equivalent GCP CA Service with 90-day rotation. Custom JWT (RS256) in Authorization header; Gin middleware validates signature using JWKS endpoint, checks platform_id claim against platform_partners table, and rejects tokens for inactive partners.
security.authorization_model: Platform-scoped access: all API operations restricted to resources where platform_id = JWT claim. account_holders and virtual_accounts are created under a platform_partner; cross-platform access returns 404 (not 403 to prevent enumeration). Separate internal admin API on a non-partner-accessible port for regulatory reporting and compliance operations.
security.secrets_management: Banking partner API credentials in GCP Secret Manager (europe-west1). JWT signing key pair (RS256) in GCP Cloud KMS. PostgreSQL connection strings and Redis auth token in Kubernetes Secrets with external-secrets-operator sync from Secret Manager. 30-day rotation for all credentials.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Immutable audit_trail table: every virtual account creation, ledger entry, SEPA event received, and webhook dispatched logged with platform_id, account_holder_id, action, resource_id, ip_address, idempotency_key, timestamp. No UPDATE or DELETE permitted on audit_trail via PostgreSQL row security policy. Retained 10 years via pg_partman partitioning with S3/GCS archival.

data.entities: platform_partners (id UUID, name, api_key_hash, mtls_cert_fingerprint, status ENUM active/suspended, created_at). account_holders (id UUID, platform_id FK, external_reference, kyb_status ENUM pending/approved/rejected, risk_tier, created_at). virtual_accounts (id UUID, account_holder_id FK, platform_id FK, iban VARCHAR UNIQUE, iban_status ENUM pending/active/closed, currency CHAR(3), created_at) — index on iban. ledger_entries (id UUID, virtual_account_id FK, entry_type ENUM credit/debit, amount_cents BIGINT NOT NULL, currency CHAR(3), reference_id UUID, reference_type VARCHAR, description VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP) — append-only, no updates. inbound_transfers (id UUID, virtual_account_id FK, sepa_uetr UUID UNIQUE, sender_iban, sender_name, amount_cents, received_at, matched_at, ledger_entry_id FK). webhooks (id UUID, platform_id FK, event_type VARCHAR, payload_json JSONB, delivery_status ENUM pending/delivered/failed, attempt_count INT, next_attempt_at, last_attempt_at). audit_trail (id UUID, platform_id, account_holder_id, action, resource_type, resource_id, idempotency_key, ip_address, timestamp) — partitioned by month, append-only.
data.storage_design: Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ (europe-west1, high-availability replica). ledger_entries append-only enforced by PostgreSQL row security policy (INSERT only, no UPDATE or DELETE for the application role). Cloud Memorystore Redis for idempotency key store (SETNX TTL 24h) and webhook retry queue (sorted set keyed by next_attempt_at). GCS bucket (europe-west1, CMEK) for KYB document uploads (object lifecycle: 5 years after account_holder closed).

devops.deployment_topology: GKE Autopilot cluster (europe-west1). Four Deployments: accounts-service, ledger-service (gRPC internal, no Ingress), payments-service, webhook-service. GKE Ingress with GCP Certificate Manager for mTLS termination. Cloud SQL Proxy sidecar on each pod. Cloud Load Balancer with Armor WAF (partner IP allowlist and rate limiting).
devops.ci_cd_design: GitLab CI: go vet, golangci-lint, unit tests, integration tests against Cloud SQL test instance (seeded with partner and account fixtures), Docker build, Artifact Registry push, GKE rolling deploy with maxSurge=1 maxUnavailable=0. Staging environment mirrors production with synthetic SEPA test events injected daily.
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus scraping all four services. Grafana dashboards: inbound_transfer_processing_latency (p50/p95/p99 vs 5-second SLA target), ledger_entry_rate, webhook_delivery_success_rate, idempotency_cache_hit_rate. PagerDuty: inbound transfer p99 > 5s (P1), webhook delivery success rate < 95% for 10 minutes (P2), Cloud SQL failover (P1).

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a greenfield virtual account API for B2B SaaS platforms operating under EU EMI license with SEPA Instant payment processing. The ledger design is the foundation — append-only double-entry bookkeeping in PostgreSQL with no mutable balance columns ensures financial accuracy and auditability. The 10-second SEPA Instant SLA drives the inbound matching latency budget: IBAN index lookup < 100ms, ledger gRPC write < 500ms, webhook dispatch < 1 second. mTLS at GKE Ingress plus platform_id JWT scoping provides the two-layer partner authentication required for partner_facing financial APIs. EU data residency in europe-west1 must be enforced at infrastructure level, not just policy.
target_output.title: Virtual Account Issuance API — B2B SaaS Banking Infrastructure
target_output.executive_summary: A Go 1.22 REST API running on GKE Autopilot in europe-west1 that enables B2B SaaS platform partners to provision IBAN-style virtual accounts for their customers, receive inbound SEPA credit transfers, and manage ledger balances — all through a single authenticated API. The ledger uses append-only double-entry bookkeeping in PostgreSQL ensuring every balance change is auditable and irreversible. All partner API traffic is protected by mTLS at the GKE Ingress plus RS256 JWT with platform_id scoping enforcing strict multi-tenant isolation. Built under EU EMI license requirements with 10-year financial record retention, AML/KYB compliance, and SEPA Instant 10-second settlement SLA.
target_output.architecture_overview: Four Go microservices on GKE Autopilot communicate via gRPC internally: AccountsService handles virtual account provisioning and IBAN issuance via banking partner API; LedgerService (internal gRPC only) manages double-entry bookkeeping with append-only ledger_entries; PaymentsService processes inbound SEPA credit transfer notifications from the banking partner webhook and orchestrates the IBAN-match-to-ledger-credit pipeline within the 5-second internal SLA budget (leaving 5 seconds for banking partner round-trip in the 10-second SEPA Instant SLA); WebhookService manages at-least-once partner notification delivery with Redis-backed exponential backoff retry queue. External partner traffic reaches the API via GKE Ingress with GCP Certificate Manager enforcing mTLS before requests hit Gin application logic. Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 (europe-west1 Multi-AZ) stores all financial data; Cloud Memorystore Redis handles idempotency key deduplication and webhook retry scheduling. All infrastructure constrained to europe-west1 for EU data residency.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Go 1.22, Gin (REST), gRPC + protobuf (internal service communication), sqlc for type-safe PostgreSQL queries, pgx driver. Database: Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 (Multi-AZ, europe-west1). Cache/Queue: Cloud Memorystore Redis 7 (idempotency keys SETNX TTL 24h, webhook retry sorted set). Auth: GCP CA Service for mTLS partner certificates, GCP Cloud KMS for JWT RS256 signing key. Secrets: GCP Secret Manager with external-secrets-operator. Infra: GKE Autopilot europe-west1, GKE Ingress with GCP Certificate Manager, Cloud Load Balancer with Armor WAF. CI/CD: GitLab CI. Observability: Prometheus + Grafana, PagerDuty.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Virtual Account Provisioning: POST /v1/accounts validates account_holder KYB status, calls banking partner IBAN issuance API synchronously (5s timeout), stores virtual_account with iban, dispatches webhook event account.created. If banking partner times out, account created with iban_status=pending and async job retries provisioning. IBAN Lookup and Inbound Payment Matching: banking partner posts SEPA credit transfer notification to PaymentsService webhook endpoint; service looks up virtual_accounts by iban (index scan < 100ms), posts gRPC credit to LedgerService, records inbound_transfer, dispatches transfer.received webhook to platform partner. Ledger Balance Query: GET /v1/accounts/:id/balance computes balance as SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN entry_type='credit' THEN amount_cents ELSE -amount_cents END) FROM ledger_entries WHERE virtual_account_id = :id — no mutable balance column. Idempotency: every POST/PATCH validates X-Idempotency-Key via Redis SETNX; duplicate keys return 200 with original response body stored in Redis value. Webhook Delivery: WebhookService pulls pending events, attempts HTTPS POST to partner endpoint, on 2xx marks delivered; on failure schedules retry via Redis sorted set with next_attempt_at score; 10 max attempts; partner email alert on exhaustion.
target_output.system_components: GKE Ingress with GCP Certificate Manager (mTLS termination, routes to AccountsService and PaymentsService), AccountsService (Gin REST, virtual account CRUD and IBAN provisioning, gRPC client to LedgerService), LedgerService (gRPC server, double-entry ledger, no external Ingress), PaymentsService (Gin webhook receiver for banking partner SEPA notifications + gRPC client to LedgerService), WebhookService (Redis sorted set consumer, HTTPS delivery to partner endpoints), Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 (Multi-AZ europe-west1, Cloud SQL Auth Proxy on each pod), Cloud Memorystore Redis 7 (idempotency key store and webhook retry queue), GCS Bucket europe-west1 (KYB document storage, CMEK, 5-year lifecycle), GCP CA Service (partner mTLS certificate issuance), GCP Cloud KMS (JWT RS256 signing key), Cloud Armor WAF (partner IP allowlist and rate limit rules), Prometheus + Grafana (metrics), PagerDuty (alerting).
target_output.workflows: Inbound SEPA Credit Transfer Processing: Banking partner sends SEPA credit transfer webhook to POST /internal/sepa/inbound (mTLS-authenticated banking partner certificate, not platform partner). PaymentsService validates SEPA UETR uniqueness against inbound_transfers table (prevents replay). Looks up virtual_account by iban using indexed query (target < 100ms). Calls LedgerService.PostCredit gRPC with virtual_account_id, amount_cents, reference_type=sepa_credit, sepa_uetr. LedgerService inserts two ledger_entries in a single PostgreSQL transaction: CREDIT to virtual_account and DEBIT to settlement_account (double-entry). Returns success to PaymentsService. PaymentsService records inbound_transfer with matched_at timestamp. Dispatches transfer.received webhook event to WebhookService via gRPC. WebhookService adds event to Redis sorted set. Total target: < 5 seconds end-to-end. Virtual Account Creation: Platform partner calls POST /v1/accounts with account_holder_id and currency, X-Idempotency-Key header. Gin middleware validates JWT platform_id claim and checks idempotency key in Redis (SETNX). AccountsService validates account_holder.kyb_status=approved. Calls banking partner IBAN issuance API with 5-second HTTP timeout. On success: stores virtual_account with iban, iban_status=active, dispatches account.created webhook. On timeout: stores virtual_account with iban_status=pending, triggers async IBAN provisioning job via Redis queue, returns 202 Accepted with polling URL.
target_output.data_model: platform_partners (id UUID PK, name VARCHAR, api_key_hash VARCHAR, mtls_cert_fingerprint VARCHAR UNIQUE, status ENUM active/suspended/terminated, created_at TIMESTAMP). account_holders (id UUID PK, platform_id UUID FK NOT NULL, external_reference VARCHAR, kyb_status ENUM pending/approved/rejected, risk_tier ENUM low/medium/high, kyb_doc_gcs_path VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP) — index (platform_id, external_reference). virtual_accounts (id UUID PK, account_holder_id UUID FK, platform_id UUID FK, iban VARCHAR(34) UNIQUE NOT NULL, iban_status ENUM pending/active/closed, currency CHAR(3), created_at TIMESTAMP) — btree index on iban (critical for inbound matching lookup). ledger_entries (id UUID PK, virtual_account_id UUID FK NOT NULL, entry_type ENUM credit/debit NOT NULL, amount_cents BIGINT NOT NULL CHECK(amount_cents > 0), currency CHAR(3), reference_id UUID, reference_type VARCHAR, description VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP) — append-only (no UPDATE or DELETE via row security policy), partitioned by toYYYYMM(created_at). inbound_transfers (id UUID PK, virtual_account_id UUID FK, sepa_uetr UUID UNIQUE NOT NULL, sender_iban VARCHAR, sender_name VARCHAR, amount_cents BIGINT, received_at TIMESTAMP, matched_at TIMESTAMP, ledger_entry_id UUID FK). webhooks (id UUID PK, platform_id UUID FK, event_type VARCHAR, payload_json JSONB, delivery_status ENUM pending/delivered/failed, attempt_count INT DEFAULT 0, next_attempt_at TIMESTAMP, last_attempt_at TIMESTAMP, created_at TIMESTAMP). audit_trail (id UUID PK, platform_id UUID, account_holder_id UUID, action VARCHAR, resource_type VARCHAR, resource_id UUID, idempotency_key VARCHAR, ip_address INET, timestamp TIMESTAMP) — partitioned by month, append-only, 10-year retention via pg_partman with GCS archival.
target_output.api_design: POST /v1/accounts — body: {account_holder_id, currency}; headers: X-Idempotency-Key; auth: mTLS + JWT; returns: {virtual_account_id, iban, iban_status, currency}; idempotency: 24h TTL. GET /v1/accounts/:id — returns virtual_account metadata; auth: mTLS + JWT (platform_id scoped). GET /v1/accounts/:id/balance — returns {virtual_account_id, balance_cents, currency, computed_at}; computed from ledger_entries aggregate. POST /v1/accounts/:id/ledger-entries — internal admin endpoint for manual adjustments; requires ops_admin JWT scope; also creates audit_trail entry. GET /v1/accounts/:id/ledger-entries — paginated ledger history (cursor by created_at); auth: mTLS + JWT. GET /v1/account-holders/:id — KYB status and risk tier; auth: mTLS + JWT. POST /v1/account-holders — create account holder with KYB data; returns id and kyb_status=pending; auth: mTLS + JWT. POST /v1/webhooks/endpoints — register partner webhook URL; auth: mTLS + JWT. POST /internal/sepa/inbound — banking partner SEPA credit transfer notification; mTLS with banking partner cert (separate CA); not exposed to platform partners. All write endpoints require X-Idempotency-Key.
target_output.security_and_compliance: mTLS enforced at GKE Ingress via GCP Certificate Manager policy — requests without valid partner certificate rejected before reaching Gin application. JWT RS256 validation in Gin middleware: verifies signature via JWKS from Cloud KMS, checks platform_id claim against platform_partners.status=active, enforces platform-scoped resource access. Idempotency enforcement: Redis SETNX key=idempotency_key value=serialized_response TTL=86400s; duplicate returns 200 with stored response body. Ledger immutability: PostgreSQL row security policy grants application role INSERT only on ledger_entries and audit_trail — no UPDATE or DELETE possible. KYB documents stored in GCS CMEK bucket (europe-west1), not in PostgreSQL; only gcs_path stored in account_holders. Cloud Armor WAF: partner IP allowlist (partners register IPs on onboarding), rate limit 1000 req/min per partner_id. AML transaction monitoring: PaymentsService checks inbound transfer amount against account_holder.risk_tier thresholds; flags above threshold to compliance queue. OFAC/EU sanctions screening on account_holder creation via integration with external screening service. PSD2 safeguarding: virtual account balances aggregated daily and reported to safeguarding account held at licensed bank. EU data residency enforced: Cloud SQL and GCS restricted to europe-west1 via GCP Organization Policy.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GKE Autopilot in europe-west1 with four Deployments (accounts-service, ledger-service, payments-service, webhook-service). GitLab CI pipeline: go vet, golangci-lint, unit tests (80% coverage), integration tests against Cloud SQL test instance with synthetic SEPA events, Docker build, Artifact Registry push, Helm chart update, GKE rolling deploy (maxSurge=1, maxUnavailable=0). Staging environment receives synthetic SEPA credit transfer events daily to validate end-to-end flow. Cloud SQL Multi-AZ automatic failover (europe-west1). Database migrations via golang-migrate run as Kubernetes Job pre-deploy. pg_partman manages ledger_entries and audit_trail monthly partitions with automated archival to GCS after 2 years (data retained in GCS for 10-year total). Secrets rotated on 30-day schedule via external-secrets-operator.
target_output.observability: Prometheus metrics scraped from all four services. Grafana dashboards: inbound_transfer_processing_latency_seconds (histogram p50/p95/p99 with 5s SLA threshold line), ledger_entry_insert_rate (counter), webhook_delivery_success_rate (gauge by platform_id), idempotency_cache_hit_rate (Redis SETNX success rate), virtual_account_provisioning_latency (banking partner API call duration). PagerDuty escalation: inbound transfer p99 > 5 seconds for 2 minutes (P1 — SEPA SLA risk), webhook delivery success rate < 95% for 10 minutes (P2), Cloud SQL primary failover (P1), ledger double-entry imbalance detected (P1 — critical financial integrity alert). Structured JSON logs include platform_id, account_holder_id, sepa_uetr, and idempotency_key as log fields for operational querying.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated €3,500/month for 500 platform partners and 2M monthly inbound transfers. GKE Autopilot (4 services, ~8 vCPU combined): ~€1,200. Cloud SQL Multi-AZ db.custom-4-15360: ~€800. Cloud Memorystore Redis (2GB): ~€180. GCS (KYB docs, audit archive): ~€150. GCP Certificate Manager + Cloud KMS: ~€100. Cloud Armor: ~€200. Grafana Cloud: ~€400. Engineering on-call PagerDuty: ~€300. Scaling to 5,000 platform partners: partition ledger_entries by platform_id prefix, add Cloud SQL read replica for balance queries, evaluate Cloud Spanner for cross-region if non-EU corridors added.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (weeks 1-10): Virtual account provisioning with synchronous IBAN issuance, inbound SEPA credit transfer matching via banking partner webhook, LedgerService double-entry bookkeeping, GET /v1/accounts/:id/balance, webhook delivery with Redis retry queue, mTLS + JWT partner authentication, idempotency enforcement, Cloud SQL schema with immutable ledger_entries, Prometheus + Grafana dashboards, GitLab CI pipeline. Phase 2 (weeks 11-20): KYB/KYC document submission flow with GCS storage and external screening integration, AML transaction monitoring with risk_tier thresholds, outbound SEPA Instant disbursements via banking partner API, platform partner onboarding self-service API, PSD2 safeguarding daily reconciliation job, audit_trail archival to GCS. Phase 3 (weeks 21-30): Multi-currency virtual accounts, SWIFT international transfer integration, BACS integration for UK corridors, partner API usage analytics, webhook retry dead-letter email alerting.
target_output.development_guidelines: Go 1.22 with golangci-lint (errcheck, gosec, staticcheck) enforced in CI. All PostgreSQL queries via sqlc (type-safe generated code) — no raw string SQL in handlers. LedgerService.PostCredit and PostDebit must always create both credit and debit ledger_entries in a single PostgreSQL transaction — unit tested to verify double-entry balance invariant. Idempotency key validation must occur before any DB write — Redis SETNX before INSERT. mTLS certificate fingerprint validated in middleware against platform_partners table on every request. Synthetic SEPA event injection in staging CI run validates end-to-end inbound matching pipeline daily. ADR required for any change to ledger_entries schema.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Banking partner IBAN issuance API SLA degradation causes synchronous virtual account creation to fail at scale. Mitigation: async provisioning fallback with iban_status=pending and 202 response; partner webhook notification when IBAN becomes active; circuit breaker on banking partner client with fallback to async-only mode. Risk: Ledger double-entry imbalance from a service crash mid-transaction. Mitigation: PostgreSQL transaction wrapping both ledger_entries INSERT statements; Prometheus gauge monitoring debit/credit aggregate equality per virtual_account reconciled hourly. Tradeoff: gRPC for LedgerService vs direct PostgreSQL access from each service — gRPC adds latency (~1ms) and operational complexity but enforces a single write path for ledger operations, preventing inconsistent bookkeeping from multiple services writing directly to ledger_entries.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Double-entry bookkeeping chosen over single-balance column to ensure financial auditability and regulatory compliance without requiring a separate reconciliation system. GKE Autopilot chosen over GCE managed instance groups for operational simplicity at launch — no node pool tuning required. SEPA Instant Credit Transfer chosen over SEPA Credit Transfer for better alignment with partner platform UX expectations (sub-10-second fund receipt notification).

metadata.notes: Moderate quality: strong on ledger design, mTLS auth, and SEPA payment flow; phased_implementation and cost_and_scaling are adequate but slightly thinner than top-tier financial API rows.

project_goal.value: Flutter POS mobile app for market vendors enabling card-present payments via Bluetooth reader with offline queuing and cloud sync on reconnect
project_goal.rationale: Core business need for vendor payment acceptance in low-connectivity environments

target_users.value: Market vendors and pop-up retailers operating on budget Android devices with intermittent connectivity
target_users.rationale: Primary end users identified in discovery sessions

project_class.rationale: Native mobile app with Bluetooth hardware integration required

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, auth, payments, data, realtime, integrations
capabilities.rationale: Covers mobile UI, cloud sync API, card reader integration, offline queue, and receipt delivery

complexity_level.rationale: Offline queue with idempotent sync and BLE hardware integration elevates complexity above standard CRUD

risk_level.rationale: Card-present payment processing with PCI scope and EMV liability shift requirements

data_sensitivity.rationale: Handles cardholder data subject to PCI-DSS card-present rules

external_exposure.rationale: Cloud backend receives payment syncs from vendor devices over the public internet

access_model.value: JWT-authenticated vendor accounts with device binding; each physical reader registered and paired to a vendor account before use
access_model.rationale: Limits blast radius of stolen credentials to a single registered device

feature_scope.value: Card-present checkout via Bluetooth EMV reader, offline transaction queuing with auto-sync, product catalog browsing, digital and SMS receipt delivery, daily sales session reporting
feature_scope.rationale: Core feature set required for market vendor checkout workflow

mvp_scope.value: Checkout flow with BLE reader, offline queue with idempotent sync, basic product catalog, email receipt dispatch
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum feature set for 50-vendor pilot launch

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3 for all cloud communication, EMV P2PE for card data via hardware reader, JWT with device fingerprint binding, no raw PAN stored on device or cloud backend
security_baseline.rationale: PCI-DSS Level 2 card-present requirements with P2PE scope reduction

frontend_stack.value: Flutter with Dart targeting Android 8+ with Bluetooth Low Energy support
frontend_stack.rationale: Cross-platform with strong BLE plugin ecosystem and budget device performance

backend_stack.value: Node.js 22 with Fastify for REST sync API, receipt dispatch, and session reporting
backend_stack.rationale: Low-latency sync endpoint with Fastify schema-first validation

data_platform.value: SQLite on-device (Drift ORM, WAL mode) for offline queue; PostgreSQL 16 on AWS RDS for cloud ledger and reporting
data_platform.rationale: SQLite enables fully offline operation; PostgreSQL handles authoritative settled records

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate in us-east-1 for Fastify API; Flutter APK distributed via Google Play Store
hosting_target.rationale: Managed container hosting with auto-scaling for sync request spikes at market open

privacy_retention_policy.value: Transaction records retained 7 years per financial regulations; raw cardholder data never persisted; device logs purged after 30 days
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: PCI-DSS requirement to minimize cardholder data retention window

future_scope.value: iOS support, loyalty program integration, multi-vendor marketplace mode, inventory management module
future_scope.rationale: Roadmap items contingent on pilot validation results

constraints.value: Checkout must complete in under 6 seconds end-to-end; target devices: Android 8+ with 2GB RAM; no persistent PAN storage anywhere in the stack
constraints.rationale: Vendor UX requirement and PCI compliance constraint

observability_baseline.value: AWS CloudWatch for Fastify structured logs and sync metrics; Firebase Crashlytics for Flutter crash reports; SNS alert on sync failure rate spikes
observability_baseline.rationale: Offline sync failure visibility is operationally critical for vendor support

execution_preference.value: 2-week Agile sprints with hardware-in-the-loop BLE reader testing each sprint
execution_preference.rationale: BLE reader integration requires physical device testing at each iteration

compliance_context.value: PCI-DSS Level 2 for card-present payments; EMV 3DS liability shift compliance; P2PE solution must use a PCI SSC-listed validated solution from card reader hardware vendor
compliance_context.rationale: Card-present EMV processing requires PCI-DSS scope management and EMV liability shift for chargebacks

project.goal: Enable market vendors to accept card-present payments on Android devices with offline resilience
project.target_users: Market vendors and pop-up retailers on budget Android hardware
project.timeline: MVP in 12 weeks targeting pilot with 50 vendors

frontend.bluetooth: flutter_blue_plus plugin for BLE card reader integration
frontend.state: Riverpod for reactive BLE and sync state
frontend.offline: Drift ORM over SQLite in WAL mode for local persistence

backend.receipt: AWS SES for email receipts, Twilio for SMS

security.auth: Custom JWT with device fingerprint binding
security.encryption: TLS 1.3 in transit; P2PE via card reader hardware — no raw PAN in app or backend
security.pci: PCI-DSS Level 2 with P2PE scope reduction

data.db: SQLite on-device (Drift ORM) + PostgreSQL 16 on AWS RDS

devops.ci: Bitrise for Flutter mobile builds, GitHub Actions for backend

constraints.device_targets: Android 8+ with BLE support, 2GB RAM minimum

reasoner_reviews.product: The offline queue with auto-sync is the critical differentiator for vendors at farmers markets and festivals where connectivity is unreliable. The 6-second checkout SLA is achievable because the BLE reader handles EMV authorization locally — there is no cloud round-trip blocking the checkout display. Product catalog must be seeded into local SQLite on first launch and refreshed via ETag to avoid checkout delays when offline.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Flutter with Drift over SQLite in WAL mode is the right local persistence choice — WAL enables concurrent writes from the BLE event handler and reads from the sync isolate without locking. The sync endpoint must be idempotent via client-generated UUID v4 idempotency keys per payment_intent to safely replay queued batches after connectivity restoration. Fastify on Node.js 22 provides schema-first validation on the sync payload with minimal overhead.
reasoner_reviews.security: PCI-DSS Level 2 scope is controlled by using a validated P2PE solution from the Verifone hardware, which removes raw PAN from application scope. Device binding via JWT with a hardware fingerprint claim prevents credential reuse on unregistered devices. BLE pairing must use bonding with PIN to prevent Bluetooth MitM attacks during reader enrollment. SQLite queue entries should include an HMAC signed with a device key to detect tampering before sync.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 6-second SLA is met offline since EMV auth is returned by the local reader with no internet dependency. On budget 2GB Android devices, Flutter must avoid memory leaks in the BLE event stream — processing should run on a background Dart isolate. Product catalog lazy-loading in pages of 50 items prevents OOM on large catalogs.

frontend.app_structure: Flutter app with feature-based module layout: checkout, catalog, receipts, sync_status, settings. BLE hardware adapter isolated in a hardware_adapter package for unit testability with mocked reader.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Catalog screen (product grid with search, quantity selector), Checkout screen (item list, discount field, total, Charge button), Reader Pairing screen (BLE scan and bond wizard), Receipt screen (digital preview with email/SMS send options), Sync Status screen (pending queue count, last sync timestamp, conflict list for declined-on-sync items).
frontend.state_management: Riverpod providers: CartNotifier (current sale items and total), SyncQueueNotifier (watches Drift DB pending count via stream), ReaderConnectionNotifier (BLE state machine: disconnected/scanning/bonded/ready/error).
frontend.performance_notes: BLE event stream runs on a dedicated Dart isolate to prevent UI jank during authorization. Product catalog uses ListView.builder with lazy loading. Const widgets throughout checkout screen for 60fps on low-end devices.

backend.service_design: Fastify API with three domain modules: sync (receives and deduplicates offline queue batches), receipts (dispatches email via SES and SMS via Twilio with SQS retry), reporting (daily sales session summaries per vendor).
backend.api_patterns: POST /v1/sync/payments (idempotent batch sync with per-item idempotency keys, returns per-item status array). GET /v1/catalog?vendor_id=&etag= (ETag conditional fetch for catalog refresh). POST /v1/receipts/:payment_id (trigger receipt dispatch). GET /v1/sessions/:vendor_id/summary?date= (daily totals). POST /v1/devices/register (device enrollment returning JWT and refresh token).
backend.failure_handling: Sync endpoint returns per-item status so partial failures are visible to the client. Idempotency deduplication via PostgreSQL unique constraint on idempotency_key. SQS dead-letter queue for failed receipt dispatch with 3 retry attempts.

security.auth_design: JWT issued at device registration containing vendor_id, device_id, and device_fingerprint claim. Token TTL 24 hours with silent refresh via refresh token stored in Flutter Secure Storage.
security.authorization_model: Vendor-scoped: each JWT is restricted to operations for its own vendor_id. Support admin role can view any vendor session data for troubleshooting.
security.secrets_management: Backend credentials in AWS Secrets Manager with 30-day rotation. Flutter build secrets (Twilio SID) injected at build time via Bitrise environment variables.
security.security_risks: BLE spoofing mitigated by requiring bonded pairing with PIN during reader enrollment. Offline queue HMAC using a device-derived key detects payload tampering before cloud sync.

data.entities: sales_sessions (id UUID PK, vendor_id FK, device_id FK, opened_at, closed_at, item_count, total_amount_cents, currency). payment_intents (id UUID PK, sales_session_id FK, amount_cents INT, currency, status ENUM queued/syncing/settled/declined/failed, idempotency_key UNIQUE, emv_auth_code, emv_application_id, created_at, synced_at, payload_hmac). product_catalog (id UUID PK, vendor_id FK, sku, name, price_cents, category, active, updated_at). card_reader_devices (id UUID PK, vendor_id FK, ble_mac, model, registered_at). receipts (id UUID PK, payment_intent_id FK, channel ENUM email/sms, destination, status, sent_at). settlement_records (id UUID PK, payment_intent_id FK, processor_reference, settled_amount_cents, fee_cents, settled_at).
data.storage_design: On-device: SQLite in WAL mode via Drift ORM mirroring payment_intents and product_catalog. Cloud: PostgreSQL 16 primary plus read replica in us-east-1 for reporting queries.
data.retention_and_deletion: payment_intents retained 7 years; data older than 90 days archived to S3. Device SQLite queue purged after confirmed sync acknowledgment from backend.

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate service in us-east-1a and us-east-1b behind ALB. Auto-scaling on sync request rate: scale out at 200 req/min per task to handle market-open spikes.
devops.ci_cd_design: Bitrise: flutter analyze, dart test, integration tests on Android emulator with mocked BLE plugin, APK build, Google Play internal track upload. GitHub Actions: ESLint, Jest (80% coverage gate), Docker build, ECR push, Fargate rolling deploy.
devops.rollback_strategy: ECS rolling deploy with 100% minimum healthy percent. Previous task definition retained for instant revert. Flutter rollback via Google Play staged rollout pause.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a PCI-DSS Level 2 card-present POS mobile app with Flutter. The core architectural challenge is the offline queue: SQLite with Drift ORM in WAL mode handles concurrent BLE event writes and sync isolate reads without blocking. P2PE from the Verifone hardware removes raw PAN from app and backend scope. The 6-second checkout SLA is met by keeping EMV authorization entirely local via the BLE reader. Fastify chosen for the sync endpoint for its schema-first validation and low per-request overhead at sync burst time.
target_output.title: Market Vendor POS Mobile Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A Flutter-based card-present POS application for market vendors and pop-up retailers enabling sub-6-second EMV checkout via Bluetooth reader with full offline operation. Payment intents queue locally in Drift-managed SQLite when connectivity drops and sync idempotently to the AWS-hosted Fastify backend on reconnection. EMV P2PE hardware encryption keeps raw cardholder data off the device and out of cloud storage, constraining PCI-DSS Level 2 scope to the validated Verifone reader hardware.
target_output.architecture_overview: Flutter Android app communicates with a Verifone e285 Bluetooth EMV reader via BLE using the flutter_blue_plus plugin, running authorization logic on the reader hardware with P2PE encryption. Authorization responses are stored immediately in Drift-managed SQLite and displayed to the vendor — no cloud round-trip required for checkout completion. A background Dart isolate monitors connectivity and batches queued payment_intents to the Fastify cloud API via TLS 1.3 when online. The Fastify backend runs on ECS Fargate behind an ALB in us-east-1, persists settled records to PostgreSQL 16, and dispatches receipts via SES and Twilio. Device authentication uses JWT with device fingerprint binding validated at every Fastify route handler.
target_output.technology_stack: Mobile: Flutter 3.x with Dart, Riverpod for reactive state management, Drift ORM over SQLite in WAL mode, flutter_blue_plus for BLE reader communication, Flutter Secure Storage for JWT. Backend: Node.js 22, Fastify with JSON Schema validation, Prisma ORM, PostgreSQL 16 with read replica. Infrastructure: AWS ECS Fargate, ALB, SQS for receipt retry queue, SES, Twilio SMS, AWS Secrets Manager, CloudWatch. CI/CD: Bitrise for Flutter APK builds, GitHub Actions for backend. Monitoring: Firebase Crashlytics for Flutter, AWS CloudWatch for backend.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Checkout Engine: product catalog browsing with local SQLite cache, item selection and quantity adjustment, total calculation with optional discount, BLE card reader charge trigger, EMV authorization response handling, receipt generation and dispatch. Offline Queue Manager: payment_intent serialization to SQLite with HMAC integrity tag, sync status tracking per intent, background sync on connectivity restoration, vendor notification for any declined-on-sync items. Product Catalog: vendor-specific SKU list with ETag-based cloud refresh and full local availability offline. Daily Sales Session Reporting: per-session item count and total, per-payment breakdown with EMV auth codes, CSV export. Device Management: BLE reader pairing wizard with bonded PIN enrollment, device registration with JWT issuance.
target_output.system_components: Flutter App (BLE adapter hardware layer, Drift SQLite local store, Riverpod state providers, background sync Dart isolate). Fastify API on ECS Fargate (sync module with idempotency deduplication, receipt dispatch module, reporting module). ALB with TLS 1.3 termination. PostgreSQL 16 primary plus read replica in us-east-1a/1b. SQS receipt retry dead-letter queue. SES for email receipts. Twilio for SMS receipts. AWS Secrets Manager for backend credentials. Firebase Crashlytics for Flutter crash telemetry with BLE state context.
target_output.workflows: Checkout Flow: vendor selects products from local catalog screen; taps Charge; Riverpod CartNotifier triggers BLE adapter to send EMV payment request to bonded Verifone e285; reader processes chip card via P2PE and returns EMV auth response; app writes payment_intent to SQLite with status settled (if online) or queued (if offline); receipt screen rendered immediately with digital preview and send options. Offline Sync Flow: ConnectivityPlus plugin detects network restoration; sync background isolate queries Drift DB for all payment_intents with status=queued; POSTs batch to POST /v1/sync/payments with per-item idempotency keys; backend validates, deduplicates via PostgreSQL unique constraint, and returns per-item status array; app updates intents to settled or failed; vendor sees conflict screen for any declined-on-sync items requiring follow-up.
target_output.data_model: sales_sessions (id UUID PK, vendor_id FK, device_id FK, opened_at TIMESTAMPTZ, closed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, item_count INT, total_amount_cents INT, currency CHAR(3), status ENUM open/closed). payment_intents (id UUID PK, sales_session_id FK, amount_cents INT, currency CHAR(3), status ENUM queued/syncing/settled/declined/failed, idempotency_key UUID UNIQUE, emv_auth_code VARCHAR, emv_application_id VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, synced_at TIMESTAMPTZ, payload_hmac CHAR(64)). product_catalog (id UUID PK, vendor_id FK, sku VARCHAR, name TEXT, price_cents INT, category VARCHAR, active BOOLEAN, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). card_reader_devices (id UUID PK, vendor_id FK, ble_mac_address VARCHAR, model VARCHAR, firmware_version, registration_token, registered_at TIMESTAMPTZ). receipts (id UUID PK, payment_intent_id FK, channel ENUM email/sms, destination TEXT, status ENUM pending/sent/failed, sent_at TIMESTAMPTZ). settlement_records (id UUID PK, payment_intent_id FK, processor_reference VARCHAR, settled_amount_cents INT, fee_cents INT, settled_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: POST /v1/sync/payments — idempotent batch sync; body: {items: [{idempotency_key, payment_intent_id, amount_cents, currency, emv_auth_code, emv_application_id, created_at}]}; returns {results: [{idempotency_key, status, processor_reference, error_code}]}. GET /v1/catalog?vendor_id=&etag= — returns product list with ETag header; 304 Not Modified if unchanged. POST /v1/receipts/:payment_id — trigger receipt; body: {channel, destination}. GET /v1/sessions/:vendor_id/summary?date= — daily session totals and per-payment breakdown. POST /v1/devices/register — device enrollment; body: {vendor_id, ble_mac_address, device_fingerprint, model}; returns {access_token, refresh_token}. All routes require Authorization: Bearer JWT with device_fingerprint claim validation.
target_output.security_and_compliance: PCI-DSS Level 2 scope limited by validated P2PE hardware solution — raw PAN never reaches the Flutter app or Fastify backend. BLE bonding with PIN pairing prevents Bluetooth MitM attacks during reader enrollment. JWT access tokens embed device_fingerprint claim; Fastify auth middleware validates fingerprint on every request to prevent token reuse on unregistered devices. TLS 1.3 enforced at ALB; backend rejects plaintext connections. SQLite queue entries carry HMAC signed with device-derived key to detect offline tampering before sync. Fastify redact plugin strips card-related fields from CloudWatch logs. PCI QSA annual assessment required. EMV chip-and-PIN liability shift enforced by requiring chip authorization for all transactions.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate service in us-east-1a and us-east-1b behind Application Load Balancer with TLS termination. Auto-scaling target-tracking policy: scale out when sync request rate exceeds 200 req/min per task, targeting market-open burst patterns (8am-2pm local time). Rolling deployment with minimum healthy percent 100%. GitHub Actions CI pipeline: ESLint, Jest unit and integration tests with 80% coverage gate, Docker image build, ECR push, ECS rolling update via AWS CLI. Bitrise CI pipeline: flutter analyze, dart test with mockito BLE stubs, integration tests on Android emulator, APK release build with signing, Google Play internal track upload. Staging environment: 2 Fargate tasks, PostgreSQL db.t3.small, mirrors production security controls. Secrets in AWS Secrets Manager with automatic 30-day rotation.
target_output.observability: CloudWatch Logs: Fastify structured JSON logs with correlation_id, vendor_id, device_id, and sync_batch_size on every sync request. CloudWatch custom metrics: SyncQueueDepth per vendor (derived from sync endpoint counters), SyncFailureRate, ReceiptDispatchLatency p95. CloudWatch Alarms via SNS: page on-call if SyncFailureRate > 5% in any 5-minute window, ALB 5xx rate > 1%, or SQS receipt DLQ depth > 0. Firebase Crashlytics: Flutter crash reports including Dart stack trace, BLE connection state at crash, and SQLite queue depth at crash time. Monthly p50/p95 sync latency report for SLA review.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: 50-vendor pilot: approximately $350/month — 2 Fargate tasks (0.5 vCPU, 1GB RAM each), PostgreSQL db.t3.small, SES email receipts at $0.10/1000, Twilio SMS at volume pricing. Scale to 5,000 vendors: 4-12 Fargate tasks on auto-scaling, PostgreSQL db.r6g.large primary plus read replica for reporting, SQS concurrency for parallel receipt dispatch. Market-open spikes handled by pre-warming 4 tasks on a scheduled CloudWatch Events rule at 7:45am.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (12 weeks): Android Flutter app with BLE reader pairing wizard, checkout flow with offline queue and auto-sync, product catalog with local caching, email receipt via SES, Fastify sync and receipt API, PostgreSQL cloud store, Bitrise and GitHub Actions CI/CD pipelines, ECS Fargate deploy, PCI compliance controls. Phase 2 (6 weeks): SMS receipt via Twilio, vendor-managed product catalog CRUD via API, ETag-based catalog refresh in app, daily sales session reporting screen, CloudWatch alerting for sync failures. Phase 3 (8 weeks): iOS support via Flutter multi-platform build, multi-vendor admin panel, loyalty program integration hooks, Stripe Terminal SDK evaluation as alternative reader hardware.
target_output.development_guidelines: Dart strict null safety enforced throughout. Flutter integration tests use mockito to stub flutter_blue_plus BLE plugin and simulate offline/online connectivity transitions. Backend: ESLint strict, Prettier, Jest with 80% line coverage gate enforced in GitHub Actions. Idempotency keys must be UUID v4 generated on the device at payment_intent creation — never server-generated. SQLite must run in WAL mode to enable concurrent Dart isolate access. Feature flags via LaunchDarkly for offline queue behavior changes to enable rollback without app release. ADR required for any change to sync protocol or idempotency scheme.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: BLE reader connection drops mid-authorization. Mitigation: BLE state machine in ReaderConnectionNotifier with automatic reconnect and transaction rollback; payment_intent only written to SQLite after confirmed EMV auth response received from reader. Risk: SQLite queue grows unbounded during multi-day offline periods. Mitigation: enforce max 500 queued intents with vendor warning at 400; require sync before new sales session if limit reached. Risk: idempotency key collision (UUID v4). Mitigation: statistically negligible but PostgreSQL unique constraint on idempotency_key returns 409 for client to generate a new key. Tradeoff: Drift ORM over raw SQLite adds a dependency but provides type-safe schema migrations and stream-based change notification for Riverpod. Tradeoff: Fastify over Express for 30% lower per-request latency critical during sync burst.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected Verifone e285 over Miura M010 due to mature flutter_blue_plus driver support. Confirmed email receipt for MVP with SMS in Phase 2 to avoid Twilio approval process blocking pilot launch. Chose Riverpod over BLoC for simpler BLE state machine composition with fewer boilerplate streams. PostgreSQL WAL replication confirmed for read replica using AWS RDS automated configuration.

metadata.notes: POS mobile app with PCI-DSS scope via P2PE hardware and offline SQLite queue with idempotent cloud sync. Moderate quality: strong core sections, observability slightly thin.

project_goal.value: Automated AML transaction monitoring tool that screens 200,000 daily payments against OFAC/SDN lists, evaluates structuring patterns, and generates SAR-ready case files for compliance analysts at a mid-size MSB
project_goal.rationale: Replace manual monitoring process that cannot scale to current transaction volume

target_users.value: Compliance analysts and AML investigators at the money services business compliance team
target_users.rationale: Primary consumers of alert queues and SAR case files

project_class.rationale: Backend automation with batch processing and analyst case management — no public-facing UI

capabilities.rationale: Covers real-time screening API, batch structuring detection, analytics dashboards, and external FinCEN filing integration

complexity_level.rationale: Real-time fuzzy matching at 200K daily transactions plus complex structuring detection rules with SAR narrative generation

risk_level.rationale: Regulatory compliance system — failures result in FinCEN enforcement actions and potential criminal liability

data_sensitivity.rationale: SAR case files and investigation notes are legally protected confidential regulatory documents

external_exposure.rationale: AML system is an internal compliance tool — no external access except automated FinCEN e-filing integration

access_model.value: RBAC via Keycloak: analyst role (create/update cases), investigator role (approve SAR filings), compliance manager role (full access and reporting), system service accounts for batch jobs
access_model.rationale: Segregation of duties required for AML program integrity — analysts cannot self-approve SARs

feature_scope.value: Real-time OFAC/SDN fuzzy-name screening per transaction, nightly structuring detection batch job, analyst alert queue with pre-populated case files, SAR narrative drafting, 90-day transaction history per customer, FinCEN e-filing XML export, compliance program analytics dashboard
feature_scope.rationale: Full BSA/AML compliance program automation scope

mvp_scope.value: Real-time OFAC screening with analyst alert queue, 90-day transaction history per customer, manual SAR case creation and narrative editor
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum needed to replace current manual process for Q2 regulatory examination

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3 in transit, AES-256 at rest, RBAC via Keycloak, comprehensive audit logging of all case access and modifications, no SAR data in application logs
security_baseline.rationale: BSA requires protection of SAR data and audit trail of all access to investigation files

frontend_stack.value: None — internal REST API consumed by existing compliance dashboard UI and batch job runners
frontend_stack.rationale: API-first design; compliance dashboard UI is a separate project using this service as a backend

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI for screening and case management REST API; Celery for background structuring detection jobs
backend_stack.rationale: FastAPI for async screening throughput; Celery with Redis broker for nightly batch scheduling

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for case management and transaction records; Redis 7 as Celery broker and screening result cache; OFAC SDN list cached in Redis for sub-millisecond lookup
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL handles case data integrity; Redis enables real-time OFAC screening within transaction latency budget

hosting_target.rationale: AKS provides pod-level network isolation required for SAR data segregation

privacy_retention_policy.value: SAR cases and transaction records retained 5 years per BSA requirements; OFAC hit investigation records retained indefinitely; analyst access logs retained 7 years
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Bank Secrecy Act record retention mandates

future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 enhancements to improve detection precision and reduce false positive rate

constraints.rationale: Transaction processing SLA and BSA tipping-off prohibition

observability_baseline.rationale: Must observe system health without exposing protected investigation data in monitoring infrastructure
