previous_plan.title: Corporate Expense Mobile App Architecture v1
previous_plan.executive_summary: React Native expense app with Django DRF backend on ECS Fargate. AWS Textract for receipt OCR, GPT-4o via Bedrock for policy violation explanations, Okta SAML SSO, and Celery-based async processing.
previous_plan.workflows: Receipt Capture: employee photographs receipt, image uploaded to S3 presigned URL, Celery OCR task invokes Textract AnalyzeExpense, extracted fields populate expense draft. Approval: out-of-policy expenses enter manager queue with GPT-4o-generated explanation.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Okta SAML SSO, TLS 1.3, AES-256 at rest. GDPR erasure endpoint deletes PostgreSQL records. S3 receipt images not addressed in erasure.
previous_plan.data_model: expense_reports, receipts with ocr fields, spend_categories, approval_workflows, policy_rules, cost_centers.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 revision addressing two high-severity issues: ISS-001 (OCR confidence fallback) and ISS-002 (GDPR S3 cascade). The fix for ISS-001 requires adding a confidence threshold gate in ReceiptOCRService that routes sub-threshold receipts to a manual review state with pre-populated editable fields. ISS-002 requires adding S3 object deletion to the GDPR erasure endpoint and documenting it in privacy controls. This is intentionally a flawed plan — while both issues are addressed in the fix_report, the data_model section incorrectly reverts to generic Users/Orders terminology for one entity instead of domain-specific names.

fix_report.action_taken: Added OCR confidence threshold gate in ReceiptOCRService: Textract confidence below 0.85 triggers manual_review status on the receipt record; the mobile app renders an editable form pre-populated with extracted fields and a 'Low confidence — please verify' banner. Updated workflows section to describe both the auto-extraction path and the manual review fallback path.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Sub-threshold receipts no longer silently pass with incorrect data; cardholders review and confirm extracted values before submission.

fix_report.action_taken: Updated GDPR erasure endpoint to call s3.delete_object for each receipt s3_key associated with the employee before deleting PostgreSQL records. Added S3 cascade step to privacy controls section and documented the erasure audit log entry. EU employee records tagged with gdpr_subject flag to support targeted erasure jobs.
fix_report.expected_outcome: GDPR erasure requests completely remove both structured data and receipt image files within the 30-day response window.

target_output.title: Corporate Expense Mobile App Architecture v2
target_output.executive_summary: A React Native mobile expense management app for enterprise cardholders built with Python 3.12/Django DRF on AWS ECS Fargate. AWS Textract extracts receipt data via OCR with a confidence threshold gate routing uncertain extractions to manual review; GPT-4o via Bedrock generates plain-language policy violation explanations for manager approvals. Okta SAML SSO with RBAC enforces role-based access for cardholders, managers, and finance admins. GDPR erasure now cascades to S3 receipt images alongside PostgreSQL records.
target_output.architecture_overview: React Native mobile client authenticating via Okta OIDC communicates with a Django DRF API on ECS Fargate over TLS 1.3. Receipt images are uploaded directly to S3 via presigned URLs, triggering Celery OCR tasks that invoke AWS Textract AnalyzeExpense. Results below the 0.85 confidence threshold route to a manual review queue; above-threshold extractions proceed to the PolicyEngine which evaluates spend against company policy rules. Out-of-policy expenses enter the manager approval queue enriched with GPT-4o-generated policy violation explanations. GDPR erasure endpoint cascades deletion to both PostgreSQL records and S3 receipt images.
target_output.technology_stack: Mobile: React Native with TypeScript, Redux Toolkit, RTK Query, React Navigation v6, Expo Notifications, Okta OIDC SDK. Backend: Python 3.12, Django 5, DRF, Celery 5 with Redis broker, drf-spectacular for OpenAPI. AI/OCR: AWS Textract AnalyzeExpense, AWS Bedrock GPT-4o. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate (API and Celery worker task definitions), RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ, Redis 7, S3 with SSE-S3, Okta SAML/OIDC, Datadog APM.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Receipt Capture with camera/gallery picker uploading to S3 presigned URL. OCR Extraction via Textract with 0.85 confidence gate: auto-path populates expense draft directly; manual-review path renders editable pre-populated form with verification banner. Policy Engine evaluating extracted category and amount against spend_categories and policy_rules. Manager Approval Queue with GPT-4o policy violation explanation and one-tap approve/reject. Expense Report generation with SAP-compatible CSV export. GDPR Erasure endpoint cascading to PostgreSQL and S3. Spend Analytics by category and cost center.
target_output.system_components: React Native mobile app with Expo Notifications and Okta OIDC. Django DRF API on ECS Fargate (API worker pool). Celery workers on separate ECS task definition: ocr_processing queue and notification_dispatch queue. ReceiptOCRService calling Textract AnalyzeExpense with confidence threshold gate. PolicyEngine evaluating against JSON-configured policy_rules. ApprovalRouter querying Okta Groups API for manager resolution. GPT-4o via Bedrock for violation explanation generation. PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ. Redis 7 for Celery broker. S3 private bucket for receipt images with presigned URL access.
target_output.workflows: Receipt OCR with Fallback: Cardholder photographs receipt, app uploads to S3 presigned URL, Celery ocr_processing task calls Textract AnalyzeExpense; if confidence_score >= 0.85 the extracted merchant, amount, date, and tax code auto-populate the expense draft and advance to PolicyEngine; if confidence_score < 0.85, receipt.status set to manual_review, cardholder receives in-app alert to verify extracted fields before submission. Policy Violation and Approval: PolicyEngine checks extracted category against policy_rules; violations trigger Celery notification task which calls Bedrock GPT-4o to generate a manager-readable explanation of the specific rule violated; notification sent to manager via push and email with approve/reject deeplink. GDPR Erasure: finance_admin or cardholder submits erasure request; endpoint queries receipts table for all s3_keys associated with employee, calls s3.delete_object for each, then deletes PostgreSQL records in cascade order, logs erasure event to audit_log with timestamp and requestor.
target_output.data_model: expense_reports (id UUID, cardholder_id FK, status ENUM draft/submitted/approved/rejected/exported, cost_center_id FK, total_amount NUMERIC(10,2), submitted_at). receipts (id UUID, expense_report_id FK, s3_key, ocr_status ENUM pending/completed/manual_review/failed, extracted_merchant, extracted_amount NUMERIC(10,2), extracted_date DATE, confidence_score FLOAT, gdpr_subject BOOLEAN, uploaded_at). spend_categories (id UUID, name, parent_id, policy_limit NUMERIC(10,2), policy_currency CHAR(3)). approval_workflows (id UUID, expense_report_id FK, approver_id FK, action ENUM pending/approved/rejected, comment TEXT, actioned_at). policy_rules (id UUID, category_id FK, rule_type ENUM amount_limit/category_block, threshold_amount, enforcement ENUM warn/block). orders (id, user_id, total) — NOTE: this generic entity was inadvertently included and does not belong to this domain.
target_output.api_design: RESTful at /api/v1. POST /receipts/upload-url (get presigned S3 URL). POST /receipts/:id/ocr-review (submit manual corrections for low-confidence receipt). POST /expenses (create draft from receipt). GET /expenses?status=&date_from=&date_to= (list with filters). POST /expenses/:id/submit (trigger PolicyEngine). GET /approvals (manager queue). PATCH /approvals/:id (approve or reject with comment). GET /exports/expense-reports?format=sap_csv (SAP export). DELETE /employees/:id/gdpr-erasure (cascade delete). Auth: Okta JWT validated via DRF TokenAuthentication middleware.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Okta SAML SSO with MFA enforced at identity provider level. DRF permission classes enforce RBAC for all endpoints. S3 presigned URLs with 15-minute expiry for receipt upload and download — no public S3 URLs at any time. GDPR erasure cascade: endpoint deletes S3 receipt images via s3.delete_object before removing PostgreSQL records, logs erasure to append-only compliance_audit_log with requestor_id, timestamp, and record_count. EU cardholder records tagged gdpr_subject=true for targeted erasure scoping. IRS 7-year retention enforced via S3 Object Expiry policy on non-GDPR receipts.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions pipeline (replacing Jenkins): ruff lint, mypy type check, pytest with 80% coverage gate, Docker build and ECR push, ECS rolling deployment with health check gate. Jenkins runs in parallel during transition; cutover after 2 consecutive successful GitHub Actions deployments. ECS Fargate: API worker task definition (min 2 tasks, auto-scale 2-8 on CPU 70%), Celery worker task definition with separate OCR queue (min 1 task, auto-scale 1-4 on queue depth). React Native: Expo EAS Build, distributed via MDM. Environments: dev, staging (with Textract sandbox), production. RDS Multi-AZ, Redis ElastiCache, Secrets Manager rotation 30 days.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM tracing Django API requests and Celery task execution. Custom metrics: ocr_processing_time_p99 (Textract latency), ocr_confidence_below_threshold_rate, approval_queue_depth, policy_violation_rate by category, gdpr_erasure_requests_completed. PagerDuty alerts: OCR Celery queue depth > 500, Textract error rate > 5%, API error rate > 1%, RDS failover.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: ~$2,200/month at 5,000 employees: ECS API tasks $400, Celery workers $200, RDS Multi-AZ $350, Redis $80, S3 $50, Textract $300 at 10k receipts/day, Bedrock GPT-4o $150 for violation explanations, ALB and networking $100. Textract is the primary variable cost — scales linearly with receipt volume. Celery OCR worker auto-scaling on queue depth controls burst capacity.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): Receipt capture with Textract OCR, 0.85 confidence gate with manual review fallback, PolicyEngine with configurable policy_rules, single-level manager approval queue, Okta SSO, GDPR erasure with S3 cascade, GitHub Actions pipeline. Phase 2 (6 weeks): GPT-4o violation explanations, SAP CSV export, multi-level approval chains, spend analytics dashboard, Jenkins pipeline decommission. Phase 3 (6 weeks): Visa/Mastercard corporate card feed integration, AI-powered spend anomaly detection, SAP Concur migration API.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python: ruff linting, mypy strict type checking, 80% pytest coverage for all service layer code. React Native: ESLint with TypeScript rules, Detox E2E tests for receipt capture and approval flows. No direct S3 presigned URL generation in Django views — centralized in ReceiptStorageService. All Celery tasks idempotent with deduplication keys. GDPR erasure tested in staging with synthetic EU employee data before production deployment.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Textract AnalyzeExpense accuracy varies by receipt format — structured receipts from major retailers score well but handwritten or thermal receipts degrade below threshold frequently. Mitigation: confidence threshold tunable per deployment via environment variable; monitor manual_review rate and adjust. Risk: GPT-4o Bedrock latency adds 1-2 seconds to approval notification delivery. Mitigation: violation explanation is generated async via Celery after policy decision, not blocking the submission path. Tradeoff: Django DRF monolith over microservices for faster delivery, with domain app boundaries enabling future service extraction.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: OCR confidence threshold set to 0.85 based on Textract documentation for AnalyzeExpense accuracy. S3 presigned URL expiry set to 15 minutes. Jenkins runs in parallel with GitHub Actions for 2 sprints before cutover. GDPR erasure window set to 30 days maximum response time.

project.target_users: SaaS founders and integration engineers

open_questions.pricing_calc: Should the pricing calculator show blended rate or interchange-plus breakdowns?

reasoner_reviews.product: The pricing calculator is the highest-conversion element for a payments developer audience — SaaS founders need to model their cost at projected volume before committing to a waitlist. Ensure the calculator handles both flat-rate and interchange-plus pricing models.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Next.js 14 static export on Vercel is the right choice. Avoid any API routes or SSR — the constraint is clear. The pricing calculator runs entirely client-side in JavaScript. Contentlayer for MDX quickstart content enables non-engineers to update code samples via pull requests.
reasoner_reviews.security: No backend means minimal attack surface. CSP header must block inline scripts except for the Plausible analytics script. GDPR cookie consent banner needed for EU visitors who trigger analytics tracking.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 2-week delivery is achievable for the MVP scope. Lighthouse 95+ requires image optimization via Next.js Image component and no render-blocking scripts. Core Web Vitals pass is critical for organic SEO.

frontend.app_structure: Next.js 14 static export with app/ directory. Pages: / (landing), /pricing, /quickstart, /changelog. Components colocated by feature: hero/, pricing-calculator/, code-samples/, waitlist-form/.
frontend.pages_and_flows: / contains Hero with animated API request/response demo (TypeAnimation), PricingCalculator component with volume slider, CodeSamples tabbed between JavaScript and Python using Shiki syntax highlighting, WaitlistForm Airtable embed, Testimonials grid. /quickstart renders MDX content from Contentlayer with sidebar navigation. /changelog lists sdk_versions from MDX files.
frontend.performance_notes: Next.js Image for all hero and testimonial images with WebP conversion. Font preloading for Inter. Shiki syntax highlighting runs at build time — no client-side bundle cost. Plausible script loaded with defer. Lighthouse 95+ target enforced in CI via Lighthouse CI action.
frontend.accessibility_notes: Pricing calculator slider uses native range input with aria-valuetext and live region for screen reader announcements of computed price. Code samples include language labels. GDPR cookie banner focus-trapped on first render for EU visitors.

devops.deployment_topology: Vercel Edge Network for global static delivery. GitHub repository connected to Vercel for automatic deployments. Preview deployments on every branch push. Production deployment on merge to main.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: TypeScript type-check, ESLint, Next.js build with static export validation, Lighthouse CI check (threshold 95 mobile), then trigger Vercel production deployment via Vercel CLI or automatic Vercel GitHub integration.
devops.observability_stack: Plausible Analytics script embedded for page views, waitlist form conversion events. Vercel Analytics for LCP/FID/CLS Web Vitals. Vercel deployment notifications to Slack channel.
devops.cost_controls: Vercel Pro plan sufficient at expected traffic. Plausible Analytics fixed monthly fee with no per-event cost. Zero server-side compute cost.

Contentlayer MDX approach for quickstart guides enables content team self-service
concerns: GDPR cookie consent requirement acknowledged but no implementation documented
Pricing calculator described for flat-rate only; interchange-plus model missing
recommendations: Implement GDPR consent banner with conditional Plausible script loading using a small consent library like vanilla-cookieconsent
Add interchange-plus tab to pricing calculator showing blended rate from card mix inputs

previous_plan.executive_summary: Next.js 14 static export on Vercel with Plausible Analytics. Waitlist form via Airtable embed. Pricing calculator with volume slider.

target_output.thinking_summary: This is a Round 2 revision for a static landing page addressing two medium issues: GDPR consent banner implementation and pricing calculator interchange-plus mode. The fixes are straightforward — add vanilla-cookieconsent for conditional Plausible loading and extend the calculator component with an interchange-plus tab. No architectural changes needed; this is purely an implementation specification gap being closed.

fix_report.action_taken: Specified GDPR cookie consent implementation using vanilla-cookieconsent (3KB gzipped). Plausible Analytics script conditionally loaded only after user accepts analytics cookies. EU visitor detection via Vercel Edge geolocation header to show banner only to EU traffic. Cookie preference stored in localStorage. Added to security_and_compliance and functional_feature_map sections.
fix_report.expected_outcome: EU visitors see GDPR consent banner before analytics fires; non-EU visitors experience no banner friction; Plausible only receives consented data.

fix_report.action_taken: Extended PricingCalculator component to support two tabs: Flat Rate (percentage + per-transaction fee applied to volume slider) and Interchange Plus (inputs for average interchange rate, processor markup, and card mix percentage to compute blended effective rate). Both modes output monthly cost estimate and cost-per-transaction. Updated functional_feature_map.
fix_report.expected_outcome: SaaS founders can model both pricing structures; interchange-plus tab differentiates the platform for cost-conscious developers.

target_output.title: Embedded Payments API Landing Page Architecture v2
target_output.executive_summary: A Next.js 14 static landing page for a B2B embedded payments API platform, deployed on Vercel Edge with zero server-side runtime. Features an animated API demo, dual-mode pricing calculator (flat-rate and interchange-plus), tabbed JavaScript/Python integration quickstart, Airtable waitlist form, and privacy-first Plausible Analytics with GDPR-compliant conditional loading. Achieves Lighthouse 95+ via build-time syntax highlighting and Next.js image optimization.
target_output.architecture_overview: Fully static Next.js 14 app exported via next export and deployed to Vercel Edge Network — no API routes, no SSR, no backend infrastructure. All interactivity (pricing calculator, tab switching, animated demo) runs client-side in the browser. Content for quickstart guides is processed at build time via Contentlayer parsing MDX files into typed JSON. Waitlist submissions are handled by Airtable's embedded form iframe — no form data touches the site infrastructure. Plausible Analytics fires only after GDPR consent is granted, using vanilla-cookieconsent for conditional script injection. Vercel Edge geolocation headers trigger the consent banner for EU visitors only.
target_output.technology_stack: Next.js 14 with static export, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v3. Contentlayer for MDX quickstart content with type-safe page props. Shiki for build-time syntax highlighting (JavaScript, Python, curl). TypeAnimation for hero API request/response animation. vanilla-cookieconsent for GDPR consent management. Plausible Analytics (self-hosted script). Vercel Analytics for Web Vitals. Airtable embedded form for waitlist. GitHub Actions for CI with Lighthouse CI plugin. Vercel for deployment.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Hero Section with TypeAnimation cycling through API request/response pairs demonstrating payment initiation and webhook delivery. PricingCalculator with two tabs: Flat Rate (volume slider computing monthly cost at configurable % + per-transaction fee) and Interchange Plus (card mix percentage, average interchange rate, processor markup inputs computing blended effective rate and monthly cost). CodeSamples component with language tabs for JavaScript and Python showing SDK initialization, payment intent creation, and webhook handling — syntax highlighted at build time via Shiki. WaitlistForm Airtable iframe embed with company_name, email, and monthly_transaction_volume fields. GDPR ConsentBanner using vanilla-cookieconsent, shown only to EU visitors via Vercel Edge geolocation, blocking Plausible script until consent granted. Testimonials grid with company logo and quote. Changelog feed rendering sdk_versions MDX entries.
target_output.system_components: Next.js 14 static export with app/ directory and page-level metadata for SEO. PricingCalculator client component with flat-rate and interchange-plus tabs. Contentlayer schema defining QuickstartDoc and ChangelogEntry types from MDX source. vanilla-cookieconsent initialized in root layout with Plausible script conditionally injected on analytics consent. Vercel Edge Network for global delivery. GitHub Actions CI pipeline with Lighthouse CI enforcing 95+ mobile threshold. Vercel preview deployments for each PR branch.
target_output.workflows: Content Update: Engineer or marketer commits MDX changes to /content/quickstart/ or /content/changelog/; GitHub Actions runs TypeScript check, ESLint, and next build; Lighthouse CI validates 95+ mobile score; on merge to main, Vercel auto-deploys and invalidates CDN cache globally within 30 seconds. Waitlist Signup: Visitor fills Airtable embedded form; Airtable automation sends welcome email and posts to sales Slack channel; no data touches site infrastructure. GDPR Consent: EU visitor detected via x-vercel-ip-country header in Next.js middleware; ConsentBanner renders with accept/decline options; on accept, vanilla-cookieconsent stores preference in localStorage and injects Plausible script tag; on decline, Plausible never loads for that session.
target_output.data_model: No database or server-side data storage. Content modeled as Contentlayer types: QuickstartDoc (slug, title, language, order, body MDX), ChangelogEntry (version, date, summary, body MDX). Pricing calculator state is transient React component state — volume, fee_percentage, per_transaction_fee, interchange_rate, processor_markup, card_mix_percentage — computed client-side with no persistence. Cookie consent preference stored in localStorage under cc_cookie key by vanilla-cookieconsent.
target_output.api_design: No API routes. External integrations: Airtable embedded form (iframe, no API calls from site code). Plausible Analytics event API called client-side via plausible() function for custom events: waitlist_form_view, pricing_calculator_interaction, code_sample_tab_switch. Vercel Analytics automatic Web Vitals reporting via @vercel/analytics package.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced via Vercel HTTPS-only setting with HSTS header. Content-Security-Policy header configured in next.config.js: default-src 'self', script-src 'self' 'nonce-{nonce}' https://plausible.io (loaded only after consent), frame-src https://airtable.com for waitlist iframe. No inline scripts. GDPR: EU visitors shown vanilla-cookieconsent banner before Plausible loads; analytics preference stored in localStorage; no cookies set before consent. CAN-SPAM: Airtable automation emails include unsubscribe link. No server-side data collection on site infrastructure.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Vercel GitHub integration for automatic deployments. Branch pushes create Vercel preview URLs shared in PR description. GitHub Actions on PR: TypeScript type-check (tsc --noEmit), ESLint, next build, Lighthouse CI action checking mobile Performance >= 95, Accessibility >= 90, SEO >= 95. Merge to main triggers Vercel production deployment with global edge propagation. Vercel Analytics dashboard tracks LCP, FID, CLS in production. No server maintenance — fully managed static hosting.
target_output.observability: Plausible Analytics tracking: page views by path, waitlist_form_view event, pricing_calculator_interaction with mode parameter (flat_rate/interchange_plus). Vercel Analytics: LCP, FID, CLS per page and geography. Vercel deployment dashboard showing build times and error rates. Slack webhook notification on production deployment success or failure. No server-side monitoring required.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Vercel Pro plan at $20/month supports expected pre-launch traffic with unlimited preview deployments. Plausible Analytics at $9/month for up to 10k page views. Airtable Free tier sufficient for waitlist volume. Total ~$30/month with zero per-request infrastructure cost. Vercel Edge Network auto-scales to any traffic spike — no capacity planning needed for a static site.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (2 weeks): Hero section with API demo animation, flat-rate pricing calculator, JavaScript code samples, Airtable waitlist form, Plausible Analytics, GDPR consent banner, GitHub Actions CI with Lighthouse gate, Vercel deployment. Phase 2 (1 week): Interchange-plus pricing calculator tab, Python code samples, testimonials grid, Contentlayer quickstart MDX setup. Phase 3 post-launch: Changelog feed, blog section, interactive sandbox link, additional language code samples.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode. ESLint with next/core-web-vitals ruleset. All images use Next.js Image component with explicit width/height to prevent CLS. No client-side JavaScript for content that can be built at compile time — use Contentlayer and Shiki. Pricing calculator logic in a pure function with unit tests. Plausible event names follow snake_case convention. PR previews shared with product team for review before merge.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Airtable iframe load time impacting CLS and Lighthouse score. Mitigation: lazy-load Airtable iframe below the fold with IntersectionObserver. Risk: vanilla-cookieconsent version updates breaking CSP nonce setup. Mitigation: pin major version and test CSP header compatibility in CI. Tradeoff: static export over SSR means no server-side personalization — acceptable for a pre-launch marketing page with no authenticated state.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: GDPR consent implemented via vanilla-cookieconsent with EU-only detection via Vercel Edge geolocation — no IP geolocation library needed. Pricing calculator to support both flat-rate and interchange-plus tabs — interchange-plus inputs: average interchange rate, processor markup, card mix. Waitlist data stored in Airtable — no infrastructure storage needed.

metadata.notes: Embedded payments API landing page revision fixing GDPR consent and interchange-plus pricing calculator. Static-only architecture correctly maintained throughout.

project_goal.value: Automated PCI-DSS evidence collection and reporting tool that periodically scans network segmentation configs, log retention policies, and access control lists across the cardholder data environment and compiles results into pre-formatted QSA questionnaire responses
project_goal.rationale: Reduce quarterly audit preparation time for a Level 1 merchant from weeks to hours

target_users.value: Security and compliance teams at a Level 1 merchant preparing for PCI-DSS QSA audits
target_users.rationale: Primary users are the compliance analysts running quarterly audit evidence collection

project_class.rationale: Scheduled scan and reporting workflow with no interactive UI — batch automation pattern

capabilities.rationale: AWS Config/IAM integrations, scheduled scan jobs, PostgreSQL evidence storage, PDF report generation

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-service AWS integrations, PCI requirement mapping logic, Step Functions orchestration

risk_level.rationale: Tool itself is in PCI scope; read access to CDE network configs and IAM policies

data_sensitivity.rationale: Evidence artifacts include network diagrams, firewall rule exports, IAM policy docs — confidential compliance data

external_exposure.rationale: Tool runs within the AWS environment; no public-facing endpoints

access_model.value: Cognito RBAC with compliance_reader, compliance_analyst, and admin roles; Lambda execution role with least-privilege IAM for AWS Config and IAM read access
access_model.rationale: Tool must operate on least-privilege principle; human access via Cognito JWT-protected API

feature_scope.value: Network segmentation scans via AWS Config rules, firewall rule export and analysis, IAM policy access control review, log retention verification, evidence artifact storage, PCI requirement mapping, QSA questionnaire report generation, scheduled scan execution, deviation alerting
feature_scope.rationale: Full PCI evidence collection lifecycle from scan to report

mvp_scope.value: AWS Config rule compliance scans for CDE segmentation, IAM policy review for CDE access, evidence artifact storage in S3, basic PCI requirement mapping for SAQ D, CSV deviation report
mvp_scope.rationale: Core evidence collection for highest-priority PCI requirements

security_baseline.value: All Lambda execution roles with least-privilege IAM; evidence S3 bucket with SSE-KMS and no public access; API endpoints protected by Cognito JWT; all scan activity logged to CloudTrail
security_baseline.rationale: Tool in PCI scope requires strict access controls and audit logging

frontend_stack.value: None — automation tool with no UI; reports exported as PDF and CSV
frontend_stack.rationale: No browser interface needed; reports consumed by compliance team via S3 download or email

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI for control plane API; Lambda functions for scan workers
backend_stack.rationale: FastAPI for the report query API; Lambda for stateless scan tasks invoked by Step Functions

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS for scan results and evidence metadata; S3 for evidence artifact storage
data_platform.rationale: Relational model suits scan result history and requirement mappings; S3 for raw artifacts

hosting_target.value: AWS Lambda plus Step Functions for scan orchestration; API on Lambda via API Gateway
hosting_target.rationale: Serverless suits intermittent scan workloads; no always-on infrastructure cost

privacy_retention_policy.value: PCI evidence artifacts retained 1 year minimum (PCI DSS Requirement 10.7); audit logs retained 1 year online, 3 years archive
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: PCI-DSS 4.0 retention requirements for audit evidence and logs

future_scope.value: Continuous compliance monitoring with real-time deviation alerting, Jira integration for remediation ticket creation, multi-cloud support for Azure CDE components
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 roadmap for continuous compliance posture management

constraints.value: Lambda functions must not exceed 15-minute timeout for any scan worker; no persistent compute during non-scan periods; evidence S3 bucket must be in same region as CDE
constraints.rationale: Serverless execution limits and data residency for PCI scope boundary

observability_baseline.value: AWS CloudWatch for Lambda metrics and Step Functions execution logs; CloudTrail for all API and evidence access; SNS email alert for scan failures or critical deviations
observability_baseline.rationale: CloudWatch and CloudTrail are native to the AWS environment; SNS for immediate deviation notification

execution_preference.value: Automated quarterly scans via EventBridge Scheduler; on-demand scan triggerable via API for pre-audit runs
execution_preference.rationale: Quarterly schedule aligns with PCI audit cadence; on-demand for ad-hoc evidence collection

compliance_context.value: PCI-DSS 4.0 Level 1 compliance; the tool itself is within PCI scope and its access controls, logging, and change management must be documented as part of the CDE boundary
compliance_context.rationale: Level 1 merchant QSA audit requirement; tool access controls are audit evidence

project.goal: Automate PCI-DSS evidence collection and QSA report generation for Level 1 merchant quarterly audits
project.target_users: Security and compliance analysts
project.timeline: Q3 delivery before next QSA audit

backend.framework: FastAPI for control plane API; Lambda for scan workers
backend.orchestration: AWS Step Functions for scan workflow

open_questions.firewall_export: Will firewall rule export be via AWS Network Firewall API or manual upload?

reasoner_reviews.product: The mapping of scan findings to specific PCI-DSS requirement numbers is the core value-add — without this mapping, the output is just raw findings that the compliance team still has to manually categorize. The requirement mapping logic must be configurable to handle PCI-DSS 4.0 vs 3.2.1 distinction during the transition period.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Step Functions for scan orchestration is well-suited — it handles retry logic, parallel scan branches per CDE component, and provides a visual execution history that doubles as audit evidence of scan execution. Lambda workers per scan type (Config rules, IAM review, log retention check) are the right granularity. FastAPI on Lambda via Mangum adapter works well for the control plane API.
reasoner_reviews.security: Tool is in PCI scope. Lambda execution roles must be scoped to read-only permissions on Config, IAM, CloudWatch Logs — no write access to production CDE resources. Evidence S3 bucket must have object-level logging enabled in CloudTrail. All human access to scan results must go through the Cognito-authenticated API, never direct S3 access.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Lambda 15-minute timeout is adequate for IAM policy review and Config rule scans. PDF report generation via WeasyPrint may approach timeout limits for large CDE environments — consider async report generation via Step Functions with SNS notification when complete.

backend.service_design: FastAPI control plane deployed on Lambda via Mangum adapter, exposed through API Gateway. Step Functions state machine orchestrating parallel scan workers: ConfigComplianceLambda, IamPolicyReviewLambda, LogRetentionCheckLambda, NetworkSegmentationLambda. ReportGeneratorLambda assembles evidence into PDF/CSV after all scan workers complete.
backend.api_patterns: FastAPI REST API: POST /scans (trigger on-demand scan run), GET /scans (list scan history), GET /scans/:id/results (scan findings), GET /scans/:id/report (download PDF or CSV), GET /deviations (open audit findings), GET /requirements/:pci_req_id/evidence (evidence by PCI requirement).
backend.business_modules: PciRequirementMapper maps scan findings to PCI-DSS 4.0 requirement IDs and sub-requirements. ComplianceBaselineLoader reads expected baseline configs from S3 JSON files. DeviationDetector compares current scan results against baseline. ReportBuilder renders QSA questionnaire responses using Jinja2 templates with WeasyPrint PDF export.
backend.failure_handling: Step Functions retries each scan Lambda up to 3 times with exponential backoff. Failed scan workers mark cde_components record as scan_failed and SNS alert fires. Partial scan results are persisted — a complete scan failure does not discard partially collected evidence.

security.auth_design: Cognito user pool with RBAC. API Gateway Lambda authorizer validates Cognito JWT. Lambda execution roles: ScanWorkerRole (Config:Get*, IAM:Get*, IAM:List*, Logs:FilterLogEvents, s3:PutObject on evidence bucket only), ControlPlaneRole (s3:GetObject on evidence bucket, rds access).
security.authorization_model: compliance_reader: GET scan results and reports. compliance_analyst: trigger scans and access all evidence. admin: manage CDE component registry and baseline configs.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: CloudTrail data events enabled on evidence S3 bucket logging all GetObject, PutObject, DeleteObject calls. API Gateway access logs with user identity and source IP. Step Functions execution history retained 90 days. All Lambda function logs in CloudWatch with 1-year retention.
security.secrets_management: No secrets stored in Lambda environment variables — RDS credentials in Secrets Manager with automatic rotation. Evidence bucket KMS key managed by KMS with key policy restricting usage to scan execution roles.

data.entities: cde_components (id UUID, name, component_type ENUM ec2/rds/firewall/lambda, aws_resource_id, in_scope BOOLEAN, last_scanned_at). scan_runs (id UUID, triggered_by ENUM scheduled/on_demand, status ENUM running/completed/partial_failure/failed, started_at, completed_at, pci_version). control_checks (id UUID, scan_run_id FK, cde_component_id FK, pci_requirement_id VARCHAR(10), check_type, result ENUM pass/fail/not_applicable, evidence_s3_key, checked_at). audit_findings (id UUID, control_check_id FK, severity ENUM critical/high/medium/low, description, expected_value, actual_value, status ENUM open/remediated, opened_at). qsa_questionnaires (id UUID, scan_run_id FK, pci_version, report_s3_key, generated_at). remediation_items (id UUID, audit_finding_id FK, owner, due_date, status, notes).
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS Single-AZ (dev/staging) and Multi-AZ (production) with SSE enabled. S3 evidence bucket with SSE-KMS, versioning enabled, object-level CloudTrail logging, and lifecycle policy archiving objects older than 1 year to Glacier.
data.retention_and_deletion: Control check records and evidence artifacts retained 3 years (1 year online, 2 years Glacier). QSA report PDFs retained 3 years. Audit findings retained until remediated plus 1 year.

devops.deployment_topology: Lambda functions packaged as Python 3.12 zip with dependencies. Step Functions state machine defined in ASL. API Gateway v2 HTTP API in front of FastAPI Lambda. All resources deployed via Terraform in GitLab CI pipeline.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitLab CI: ruff lint, mypy type check, pytest with mocked AWS SDK calls (moto), Terraform plan in staging, manual approval gate for production Terraform apply. Lambda zip packages built with pip and uploaded to S3 deployment bucket.
devops.observability_stack: CloudWatch Metrics for Lambda invocation count, error rate, and duration. Step Functions execution success/failure metrics. SNS email alert for scan failure and for critical audit_findings created. CloudWatch Dashboard showing last scan run status, open deviation count, and days since last successful scan.
devops.rollback_strategy: Lambda function versions pinned in Terraform. Previous version alias available for immediate rollback. Terraform state in S3 with DynamoDB lock.

target_output.thinking_summary: Building a PCI-DSS evidence automation tool as a serverless AWS architecture. The key design decisions are Step Functions for orchestrating parallel scan workers (each scan type is a separate Lambda), Mangum to run FastAPI on Lambda for the control plane API, and strict IAM least-privilege for scan execution roles since the tool itself is in PCI scope. ReportBuilder uses Jinja2 templates per PCI-DSS requirement to generate pre-formatted QSA responses — this is the core differentiating logic.
target_output.title: PCI-DSS Evidence Collection Automation Tool Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A serverless AWS automation tool for PCI-DSS Level 1 evidence collection, built with Python 3.12 FastAPI on Lambda (via Mangum) for the control plane and AWS Step Functions orchestrating parallel scan workers across the cardholder data environment. Scans AWS Config rule compliance, IAM access control lists, log retention policies, and network segmentation configurations, mapping findings to PCI-DSS 4.0 requirement IDs and generating pre-formatted QSA questionnaire responses as PDF and CSV. All evidence artifacts stored in S3 with SSE-KMS; the tool itself operates under documented CDE scope.
target_output.architecture_overview: Serverless architecture with three layers: a Cognito JWT-authenticated FastAPI control plane on Lambda via Mangum behind API Gateway for human-triggered scans and report downloads; an AWS Step Functions state machine orchestrating parallel Lambda scan workers (ConfigComplianceLambda, IamPolicyReviewLambda, LogRetentionCheckLambda, NetworkSegmentationLambda); and PostgreSQL 16 on RDS for scan result metadata with S3 for raw evidence artifacts. EventBridge Scheduler triggers quarterly scans automatically; the control plane API supports on-demand runs for pre-audit preparation. All Lambda execution roles are least-privilege read-only against CDE resources. ReportGeneratorLambda maps findings to PCI-DSS 4.0 requirement IDs via PciRequirementMapper and renders PDF QSA responses using Jinja2 and WeasyPrint.
target_output.technology_stack: Runtime: Python 3.12. Control plane: FastAPI with Mangum Lambda adapter, API Gateway v2 HTTP API. Orchestration: AWS Step Functions (Express Workflow). Scan workers: Lambda functions per scan type. PDF generation: Jinja2 templates with WeasyPrint. Infrastructure: RDS PostgreSQL 16, S3 with SSE-KMS, Cognito user pools, API Gateway, EventBridge Scheduler, SNS, CloudTrail, CloudWatch. IaC: Terraform. CI/CD: GitLab CI. AWS SDK: boto3 for Config, IAM, Logs API calls.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Scheduled Scan Execution triggered by EventBridge Scheduler quarterly and ad-hoc via API. ConfigComplianceLambda evaluating AWS Config managed rules against CDE component list and baseline. IamPolicyReviewLambda enumerating IAM policies, roles, and access keys with CDE access, flagging excessive permissions. LogRetentionCheckLambda verifying CloudWatch Log Groups for CDE resources meet PCI Requirement 10.7 retention minimums. NetworkSegmentationLambda reviewing Security Group and Network Firewall rules for CDE isolation. PciRequirementMapper tagging each finding to a specific PCI-DSS 4.0 requirement ID (e.g., Req 7.2.1, Req 10.7.1). DeviationDetector comparing findings against compliance baseline. QSA Report Generator producing PDF with pre-populated SAQ D responses and CSV deviation summary.
target_output.system_components: EventBridge Scheduler rule (quarterly cron, UTC) invoking Step Functions state machine. Step Functions Express Workflow with parallel branches for Config, IAM, LogRetention, and NetworkSegmentation scan workers, then a sequential ReportGenerator step. FastAPI control plane on Lambda via Mangum with API Gateway v2. PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ in production for scan metadata. S3 evidence bucket with SSE-KMS, versioning, and lifecycle to Glacier at 12 months. ComplianceBaselineLoader reading expected states from S3 JSON config files. ReportBuilder with Jinja2 QSA templates per PCI-DSS 4.0 requirement section. SNS topic for scan completion and critical deviation notifications.
target_output.workflows: Quarterly Automated Scan: EventBridge Scheduler triggers Step Functions at 02:00 UTC on first day of each quarter. State machine fans out to 4 parallel scan Lambda workers, each writing findings to control_checks and evidence artifacts to S3. After all workers complete, ReportGeneratorLambda runs PciRequirementMapper against all findings, renders Jinja2 QSA template per requirement section, produces PDF via WeasyPrint and CSV deviation list, stores both to S3, updates qsa_questionnaires record, and fires SNS notification with report download URL to compliance team. Pre-Audit On-Demand Run: Compliance analyst calls POST /scans via Cognito-authenticated API, triggering same Step Functions workflow with on_demand trigger type. Progress visible via GET /scans/:id status polling.
target_output.data_model: cde_components (id UUID, name, component_type ENUM ec2/rds/firewall/lambda, aws_resource_id VARCHAR(255), in_scope BOOLEAN, last_scanned_at TIMESTAMPTZ). scan_runs (id UUID, triggered_by ENUM scheduled/on_demand, status ENUM running/completed/partial_failure/failed, pci_version VARCHAR(10), started_at, completed_at). control_checks (id UUID, scan_run_id FK, cde_component_id FK, pci_requirement_id VARCHAR(10), check_type VARCHAR(100), result ENUM pass/fail/not_applicable, evidence_s3_key, checked_at). audit_findings (id UUID, control_check_id FK, severity ENUM critical/high/medium/low, description, expected_value, actual_value, status ENUM open/remediated, opened_at). qsa_questionnaires (id UUID, scan_run_id FK, pci_version, report_s3_key, csv_s3_key, generated_at). remediation_items (id UUID, audit_finding_id FK, owner, due_date DATE, status ENUM open/in_progress/closed, notes).
target_output.api_design: FastAPI on Lambda via Mangum. POST /scans (trigger on-demand scan, returns scan_run_id). GET /scans (list history, paginated). GET /scans/:id (status and summary). GET /scans/:id/results (all control_checks with findings). GET /scans/:id/report?format=pdf|csv (presigned S3 download URL). GET /deviations?status=open (all open audit_findings across runs). GET /requirements/:pci_req_id/evidence (evidence artifacts for a specific PCI requirement). GET /cde-components (registry). PUT /cde-components/:id (update in-scope flag). Auth: API Gateway Lambda authorizer validating Cognito JWT. All endpoints require compliance_reader role minimum.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Tool in PCI CDE scope — access controls and change management are themselves audit evidence. ScanWorkerRole IAM policy: Config:GetComplianceDetailsByConfigRule, Config:DescribeConfigRules, IAM:GetPolicy, IAM:ListAttachedRolePolicies, IAM:ListUsers, logs:FilterLogEvents, ec2:DescribeSecurityGroups, network-firewall:DescribeFirewallPolicy — no write permissions to any CDE resource. Evidence S3 bucket: SSE-KMS with dedicated KMS key, block all public access, object-level CloudTrail logging for GetObject and PutObject. Cognito user pool with password policy and MFA optional at admin discretion. CloudTrail trail with S3 data events covering evidence bucket. API Gateway access logs retained 1 year. Step Functions execution history 90 days.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitLab CI pipeline: ruff lint, mypy type check, pytest with moto for AWS SDK mocking (80% coverage gate on scanner modules), Terraform plan against staging, manual approval gate for production Terraform apply. Lambda packages built as Python 3.12 zip with dependencies via pip install -t. Lambda function versions pinned in Terraform with previous version alias for rollback. EventBridge Scheduler enabled/disabled via Terraform variable for maintenance windows. Environments: dev (single-AZ RDS, mock scan data), staging (multi-AZ RDS, sandboxed AWS account), production (multi-AZ RDS, live CDE read access). Terraform state in S3 with DynamoDB lock.
target_output.observability: CloudWatch custom dashboard: last_scan_run_status, open_deviations_count, critical_findings_count, days_since_last_successful_scan. Lambda metrics: invocation count, error rate, duration (p99). Step Functions: execution success rate, execution duration. SNS email alerts: scan failure (Step Functions execution FAILED), critical severity audit_finding created, report generation timeout (Lambda duration > 10 minutes). CloudTrail anomaly detection for unexpected CDE resource access by scan roles.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: ~$150/month for quarterly scan cadence: Lambda invocations at quarterly scale are near-zero cost. RDS PostgreSQL db.t3.small Multi-AZ ~$80, S3 storage ~$20, Step Functions Express Workflows ~$10, API Gateway ~$10, CloudWatch ~$20. On-demand pre-audit runs add minor Lambda cost. WeasyPrint PDF generation is CPU-intensive — consider 3008MB Lambda memory for report generator to stay within 15-minute timeout for large CDE environments.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): CDE component registry, ConfigComplianceLambda for CDE segmentation rules, IamPolicyReviewLambda, evidence S3 storage, PciRequirementMapper for SAQ D requirements, CSV deviation report, Cognito auth, Terraform IaC, EventBridge quarterly schedule, GitLab CI pipeline. Phase 2 (4 weeks): LogRetentionCheckLambda, NetworkSegmentationLambda via Security Group rules, PDF QSA report generation with Jinja2/WeasyPrint, SNS notifications, DeviationDetector with baseline comparison. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Remediation tracking via remediation_items, Jira webhook integration for ticket creation, compliance posture dashboard via CloudWatch, AWS Network Firewall rule analysis.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python ruff linting and mypy strict type checking enforced in GitLab CI. All scanner Lambda functions must have unit tests with moto mocking AWS SDK calls — no live AWS calls in tests. PciRequirementMapper logic unit-tested against all 12 PCI-DSS 4.0 requirement sections. Terraform modules for Lambda, Step Functions, and S3 in separate reusable modules. No hardcoded AWS resource IDs — all configuration via SSM Parameter Store or environment variables. Evidence artifact S3 keys follow pattern: {scan_run_id}/{cde_component_id}/{check_type}/{timestamp}.json.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: WeasyPrint PDF generation timing out for CDE environments with 100+ components. Mitigation: chunk report generation into per-requirement-section Lambda invocations and assemble via Step Functions. Risk: AWS Config rule coverage gaps for custom CDE components not tracked by managed rules. Mitigation: ComplianceBaselineLoader supports custom check definitions for non-Config-native resources. Tradeoff: FastAPI on Lambda over a persistent container — cold start latency is acceptable for infrequent on-demand scans. Tradeoff: RDS over DynamoDB for structured query support on scan history and requirement-level evidence lookup.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Network firewall rule export will use AWS Network Firewall DescribeFirewallPolicy API — no manual upload required. PDF format confirmed via WeasyPrint with Jinja2 templates; report generator Lambda allocated 3008MB to handle large CDE environments. Baseline configs stored in S3 JSON files editable by admin role, not hardcoded in Lambda.

metadata.notes: PCI-DSS evidence collection automation with Step Functions orchestration, Lambda scan workers, and QSA report generation. Tool itself in CDE scope.

project_goal.value: Research prototype exploring dynamic routing of payment transactions across multiple acquirers to minimize interchange and processing fees using reinforcement learning to learn optimal routing policies from historical acceptance rates and cost data
project_goal.rationale: High-volume e-commerce merchant processing 50M transactions/year needs cost optimization research foundation

target_users.value: Payments engineering and data science team at a high-volume e-commerce merchant
target_users.rationale: Internal research team evaluating whether RL routing warrants production development

project_class.rationale: Experimental system for policy learning and evaluation; not production payment infrastructure

capabilities.rationale: RL training pipeline, feature engineering, batch evaluation, simulation analytics

complexity_level.rationale: RL environment simulation, feature engineering at transaction scale, policy evaluation framework

risk_level.rationale: Internal research with anonymized data; no live payment routing; limited blast radius

data_sensitivity.rationale: Anonymized and tokenized transaction data still contains BIN ranges, amounts, and merchant categories — financial data

external_exposure.rationale: Internal research prototype; no external API surface

access_model.value: Custom JWT with researcher and admin roles; GKE workload identity for service-to-service auth
access_model.rationale: Internal tool; lightweight auth sufficient

feature_scope.value: Historical transaction replay environment, RL agent training (PPO and Q-learning variants), feature engineering pipeline from BIN and transaction attributes, acquirer simulation with acceptance rate and fee models, policy evaluation against holdout dataset, projected savings and acceptance rate delta reporting
feature_scope.rationale: Full research cycle from data preparation to policy evaluation

mvp_scope.value: Historical replay environment with 12 months of tokenized transactions, PPO agent training, per-acquirer cost model, basic evaluation report with savings projection
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum to demonstrate proof of concept and quantify business case

security_baseline.value: All datasets tokenized before ingestion; no raw card data; GKE namespace isolation; TLS 1.3 for all internal service communication
security_baseline.rationale: Even anonymized financial data requires access controls and transport encryption

frontend_stack.value: None — no browser UI; results consumed via Jupyter notebooks and exported evaluation reports
frontend_stack.rationale: Research prototype outputs notebooks and CSV/JSON reports, not a web UI

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI for internal experiment management API; Ray RLlib for distributed RL training
backend_stack.rationale: FastAPI for experiment tracking REST API; Ray RLlib provides PPO and Q-learning implementations with GKE autoscaling support

data_platform.value: ClickHouse for transaction event storage and feature queries; Redis 7 for online feature cache during training rollouts
data_platform.rationale: ClickHouse handles high-cardinality aggregations (BIN velocity, merchant category acceptance rates) efficiently; Redis for low-latency feature lookup during simulation steps

hosting_target.value: GKE Autopilot on GCP
hosting_target.rationale: GKE Autopilot auto-scales Ray worker nodes for training jobs without manual node pool management

privacy_retention_policy.value: All datasets tokenized at ingestion; no retention of raw transaction identifiers; research data retained 1 year then deleted; no GDPR subject data in tokenized datasets
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Internal research exemption applies when data is fully tokenized; 1-year research data lifecycle

future_scope.value: Shadow mode deployment alongside production routing engine, online policy updates from live acceptance signals, multi-objective routing (acceptance rate vs cost vs latency)
future_scope.rationale: Production deployment roadmap contingent on prototype proving business case

constraints.value: No live card data or production payment infrastructure access; all training on tokenized historical datasets; prototype must produce Go/No-Go recommendation report with statistical confidence intervals
constraints.rationale: Safety constraint for research prototype; business case rigor requirement

observability_baseline.value: Grafana with Prometheus for training job metrics; Ray Dashboard for distributed job monitoring; experiment results logged to MLflow tracking server
observability_baseline.rationale: Standard research toolchain for experiment tracking and infrastructure monitoring

execution_preference.value: Iterative research cycles of 2 weeks each; experiment results reviewed by team before next cycle
execution_preference.rationale: Research iteration cadence with team review gates

compliance_context.value: Internal research using tokenized financial data; datasets anonymized per internal data governance policy; no PCI scope since no card data present
compliance_context.rationale: Tokenized data falls outside PCI scope under internal data governance framework

llm_integration.value: LLM used for automated experiment report generation — GPT-4o via Vertex AI summarizes training run metrics, policy performance comparisons, and statistical test results into a natural-language Go/No-Go recommendation memo for engineering leadership
llm_integration.rationale: Research team produces many experiment runs; LLM-generated summaries reduce reporting burden and standardize output format

project.goal: Evaluate RL-based acquirer routing for interchange cost reduction via historical simulation
project.target_users: Payments engineering and data science research team

backend.framework: FastAPI for experiment API; Ray RLlib for RL training
backend.simulation: Custom Gym environment wrapping ClickHouse replay

security.data_protection: All datasets tokenized; no raw card data

devops.environments: dev and research (no staging/prod distinction for prototype)

open_questions.agent_architecture: Should we compare PPO vs DQN vs contextual bandit for this routing problem?

reasoner_reviews.product: The prototype must deliver a clear business case artifact — projected annual savings in dollars and acceptance rate impact with confidence intervals. Without this, engineering leadership cannot make a Go/No-Go decision. The LLM-generated summary should be reviewed by the research lead before distribution.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Ray RLlib on GKE Autopilot is the right choice — the autoscaling eliminates manual node pool management for variable-duration training jobs. The custom Gym environment wrapping ClickHouse replay needs careful design to avoid data leakage between training and evaluation splits. ClickHouse materialized views for precomputed BIN-level acceptance rates will dramatically reduce feature query latency during training rollouts.
reasoner_reviews.security: The tokenization pipeline must run before any data enters the GKE cluster. Raw transaction exports from the production system should land in a GCS staging bucket with restricted access, and the tokenizer job runs first before writing to the ClickHouse training cluster. Prototype should never have network access to production payment infrastructure.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Statistical confidence intervals for the Go/No-Go report are non-trivial — specify bootstrap confidence intervals over 100 evaluation rollouts on the holdout set, not just a single point estimate.

backend.service_design: FastAPI experiment management API tracking training runs, hyperparameter configs, and evaluation results. Ray RLlib cluster (head node + auto-scaled worker pods on GKE) for distributed PPO and DQN training. Custom AcquirerRoutingEnv Gym environment that replays ClickHouse transactions, calls the routing policy for each step, and returns reward as fee_saved minus acceptance_penalty.
backend.api_patterns: FastAPI REST: POST /experiments (launch training run with config), GET /experiments/:id (status and metrics), GET /experiments/:id/evaluation (holdout evaluation results), POST /experiments/:id/report (trigger LLM summary generation), GET /experiments/:id/report (download summary).
backend.business_modules: AcquirerRoutingEnv Gym environment with per-step reward function: reward = (baseline_fee - routed_fee) - acceptance_penalty_weight * (1 - acceptance_probability). FeatureEngineer computing BIN-level velocity counters and merchant category acceptance rates from ClickHouse. PolicyEvaluator replaying holdout dataset through frozen policy and computing savings_delta, acceptance_rate_delta, and 95% bootstrap confidence intervals. ExperimentReportGenerator calling GPT-4o via Vertex AI to generate Go/No-Go memo.
backend.llm_integration_design: GPT-4o via Vertex AI called by ExperimentReportGenerator with structured prompt containing: experiment config, training convergence metrics, evaluation savings_delta and acceptance_rate_delta with confidence intervals, comparison against baseline static routing. Output is a 1-2 page natural language memo with recommendation and caveats. Human review by research lead required before distribution.

data.entities: routing_decisions (id UUID, experiment_id, transaction_token, selected_acquirer_id, action_probs JSON, reward FLOAT, episode_step INT, logged_at). acquirer_connections (id UUID, acquirer_name, processing_currency, active BOOLEAN). interchange_rates (id UUID, card_network, card_type, merchant_category_code VARCHAR(4), rate_bps INT, effective_date DATE). acceptance_outcomes (id UUID, transaction_token, acquirer_id, outcome ENUM accepted/declined/timeout, response_code VARCHAR(4), logged_at). cost_models (id UUID, acquirer_id, base_fee_bps, per_transaction_fee_cents, effective_date). rl_episodes (id UUID, experiment_id, episode_num, total_reward FLOAT, steps INT, completed_at). fee_simulations (id UUID, experiment_id, evaluation_dataset_id, savings_delta_annual USD NUMERIC(12,2), acceptance_rate_delta FLOAT, confidence_interval_lower, confidence_interval_upper, bootstrap_samples INT, generated_at).
data.storage_design: ClickHouse cluster on GKE: transactions table with BIN, amount_cents, merchant_category_code, acquirer_selected, acceptance_outcome, fee_bps. Materialized views: mv_bin_acceptance_rates (BIN-level 7-day rolling acceptance rates per acquirer) and mv_merchant_category_fees (average fee by MCC and acquirer). Redis 7 for online feature cache: BIN velocity counters and mv_bin_acceptance_rates keyed by bin_prefix.
data.analytics_events: training_step_reward, episode_completed, evaluation_run_completed, savings_projection_computed logged to MLflow tracking server with run_id linking to experiment record.

devops.deployment_topology: GKE Autopilot cluster. Ray head node as GKE Deployment. Ray worker nodes as auto-scaled GKE pods — scale from 0 to 20 workers during training. FastAPI experiment API as GKE Deployment with 1-2 replicas. MLflow tracking server as GKE Deployment with GCS artifact store.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: ruff lint, mypy type check, pytest for FeatureEngineer and PolicyEvaluator unit tests, Docker build and push to GCR. No automated deployment of model artifacts — researchers trigger training runs manually via FastAPI. Jupyter notebooks version-controlled in repo.
devops.observability_stack: Grafana with Prometheus: Ray cluster utilization, training throughput (steps/second), ClickHouse query latency, Redis cache hit rate. Ray Dashboard for job-level worker utilization and error logs. MLflow tracking: per-experiment loss curves, reward history, evaluation metrics. Grafana alert on Ray worker OOM errors.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 4 is a focused fix for ISS-003 — the LLM-generated Go/No-Go report being distributed to Slack without a mandatory human review gate. The fix adds a report_status state machine (pending_review/approved/distributed) to the fee_simulations record and requires a POST /experiments/:id/report/approve action by a user with the researcher role before the Slack webhook fires. ISS-001 (data leakage) and ISS-002 (confidence intervals) were resolved in Round 3 and should be preserved unchanged.

fix_report.action_taken: Added report_status ENUM (pending_review/approved/distributed) to fee_simulations table. ExperimentReportGenerator sets status to pending_review and notifies research lead via email (not Slack) with a review link. Research lead must call POST /experiments/:id/report/approve (requires researcher role) to transition to approved. Only after approved does the system POST the memo to the #payments-research Slack channel and set status to distributed. FastAPI endpoint returns 403 if Slack publish attempted while status != approved.
fix_report.expected_outcome: GPT-4o Go/No-Go memos require explicit human approval before reaching engineering leadership; reduces risk of distributing incorrect RL evaluation conclusions.

target_output.title: RL Payment Routing Research Prototype Architecture v4
target_output.executive_summary: A Python 3.12 research prototype on GKE Autopilot exploring reinforcement learning for acquirer routing cost optimization. Ray RLlib trains PPO and DQN agents in a custom AcquirerRoutingEnv that replays 12 months of tokenized ClickHouse transactions, applying per-acquirer cost and acceptance rate models. PolicyEvaluator produces 95% bootstrap confidence intervals over holdout data for statistically credible savings projections. GPT-4o via Vertex AI generates Go/No-Go recommendation memos that require explicit research lead approval before distribution — enforced via a report_status state machine.
target_output.architecture_overview: GKE Autopilot cluster hosting: Ray head node and auto-scaled worker pods for distributed RL training; FastAPI experiment management API; MLflow tracking server with GCS artifact store. Training pipeline: FeatureEngineer queries ClickHouse materialized views for BIN-level acceptance rates and merchant category fee distributions, populating Redis 7 online feature cache; AcquirerRoutingEnv Gym environment replays ClickHouse transactions with holdout_flag=false, returning step rewards based on fee savings minus acceptance penalty. Evaluation: PolicyEvaluator freezes trained policy and replays holdout_flag=true partition, computing savings_delta and acceptance_rate_delta with 1000-sample bootstrap CI. Reporting: ExperimentReportGenerator calls GPT-4o via Vertex AI, stores result with report_status=pending_review, notifies research lead by email; research lead approves via API before Slack publish.
target_output.technology_stack: Python 3.12, FastAPI, Ray RLlib 2.x (PPO, DQN), OpenAI Gym (custom AcquirerRoutingEnv), numpy/scipy for bootstrap CI, MLflow for experiment tracking. Data: ClickHouse (transaction replay + materialized views), Redis 7 (online feature cache). LLM: GPT-4o via Vertex AI (report generation). Infrastructure: GKE Autopilot, GCS for MLflow artifacts and tokenized dataset staging, Grafana + Prometheus, GitHub Actions CI. Auth: custom JWT with researcher and admin roles, GKE Workload Identity for service accounts.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Tokenization Pipeline ingesting raw transaction exports from GCS staging, applying token replacement for PAN/card identifiers, setting holdout_flag on 20% sample at ingestion time. Feature Engineering computing BIN-level 7-day rolling acceptance rates and MCC-level fee distributions as ClickHouse materialized views. AcquirerRoutingEnv Gym environment with configurable reward weight (acceptance_penalty_weight) and per-step reward = (baseline_fee - selected_fee) - weight * (1 - acceptance_prob). Ray RLlib training runs for PPO and DQN variants with hyperparameter sweep support. PolicyEvaluator running frozen policy over holdout partition and computing annual savings projection with 95% bootstrap CI (1000 samples). ExperimentReportGenerator calling Vertex AI GPT-4o with structured metrics context. Report Review Workflow with pending_review/approved/distributed states and research lead approval gate. Grafana training dashboard.
target_output.system_components: GKE Autopilot cluster (us-central1). Ray cluster: head Deployment + auto-scaled worker Pods (0-20, CPU-optimized). FastAPI experiment API Deployment (2 replicas, ClusterIP). MLflow tracking server Deployment with GCS backend. ClickHouse StatefulSet (2 shards, 3 replicas) with transactions table and mv_bin_acceptance_rates, mv_merchant_category_fees materialized views. Redis 7 Deployment for online feature cache. FeatureEngineer batch job populating Redis from ClickHouse on training job start. AcquirerRoutingEnv Gym wrapper using ClickHouse Python client for episode replay. PolicyEvaluator service with scipy.stats bootstrap sampling. ExperimentReportGenerator calling Vertex AI SDK. Prometheus + Grafana stack.
target_output.workflows: Training Run: Researcher calls POST /experiments with PPO config and acquirer list. API creates experiment record, triggers Ray RLlib job on GKE workers. FeatureEngineer pre-populates Redis cache from ClickHouse mv_bin_acceptance_rates. AcquirerRoutingEnv replays holdout_flag=false transactions, calls Redis for BIN acceptance rates each step, returns reward. Ray logs episode rewards to MLflow. On convergence, training job marks experiment status=completed. Evaluation and Report: Researcher calls POST /experiments/:id/evaluation. PolicyEvaluator replays holdout_flag=true partition through frozen policy, runs 1000-sample bootstrap over savings_delta, writes fee_simulations record with CI bounds and report_status=pending_review. ExperimentReportGenerator calls Vertex AI GPT-4o with structured prompt, stores memo in fee_simulations.llm_memo_gcs_key, sends email notification to research lead with review URL. Research lead calls POST /experiments/:id/report/approve (researcher role required), API transitions report_status to approved. Automated job detects approved status and posts memo excerpt to #payments-research Slack channel, sets report_status=distributed.
target_output.data_model: routing_decisions (id UUID, experiment_id FK, transaction_token, selected_acquirer_id, action_probs JSON, reward FLOAT, episode_step INT, logged_at). acquirer_connections (id UUID, acquirer_name, processing_currency, active BOOLEAN, api_endpoint_mock). interchange_rates (id UUID, card_network, card_type, merchant_category_code VARCHAR(4), rate_bps INT, effective_date DATE). acceptance_outcomes (id UUID, transaction_token, acquirer_id, outcome ENUM accepted/declined/timeout, response_code, logged_at). cost_models (id UUID, acquirer_id, base_fee_bps INT, per_transaction_fee_cents INT, effective_date DATE). rl_episodes (id UUID, experiment_id FK, episode_num INT, total_reward FLOAT, steps INT, policy_entropy FLOAT, completed_at). fee_simulations (id UUID, experiment_id FK, evaluation_dataset_id, savings_delta_annual_usd NUMERIC(12,2), acceptance_rate_delta FLOAT, ci_lower FLOAT, ci_upper FLOAT, bootstrap_samples INT, llm_memo_gcs_key, report_status ENUM pending_review/approved/distributed, reviewed_by, reviewed_at, generated_at).
target_output.api_design: FastAPI REST. POST /experiments (launch training run with RLlib config JSON). GET /experiments/:id (status, episode count, latest reward). POST /experiments/:id/evaluation (trigger PolicyEvaluator on holdout partition). GET /experiments/:id/evaluation (savings_delta, acceptance_rate_delta, CI bounds, bootstrap_samples). POST /experiments/:id/report (trigger GPT-4o memo generation, sets pending_review). GET /experiments/:id/report (report text and status). POST /experiments/:id/report/approve (researcher role required, transitions to approved). GET /acquirer-connections (list active acquirers). GET /interchange-rates?card_network=&mcc= (fee lookup). Auth: custom JWT middleware, researcher and admin roles enforced per endpoint.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Tokenization pipeline runs before any data enters GKE — raw exports land in restricted GCS staging bucket (tokenizer-service-account only), tokenizer job replaces PAN data with 256-bit HMAC tokens, writes to ClickHouse training cluster. No raw card identifiers in GKE cluster at any time. GKE Workload Identity for service account binding — no static credentials in pod specs. GKE namespace isolation: research namespace has no egress to production payment infrastructure. TLS 1.3 for all inter-service communication. report_status gate enforced at FastAPI middleware: POST to Slack webhook rejected with HTTP 403 if fee_simulations.report_status != approved. Audit log of report approvals written to GCS.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions: ruff lint, mypy type check, pytest for FeatureEngineer, PolicyEvaluator, and AcquirerRoutingEnv unit tests (mocked ClickHouse responses), Docker build and push to GCR. No automated CD — researchers deploy training jobs manually via FastAPI. GKE Autopilot auto-scales Ray workers based on pending Ray tasks. ClickHouse backups to GCS daily. MLflow GCS artifact store backed up via GCS versioning. Research data lifecycle: 1-year TTL enforced via GCS Object Lifecycle policy on tokenized dataset bucket.
target_output.observability: Grafana dashboard: Ray cluster CPU/GPU utilization, training throughput (steps/second), episode_completed rate, ClickHouse query p99, Redis cache hit rate. MLflow Tracking: per-experiment reward curves, policy_entropy over training, evaluation metrics (savings_delta, CI bounds). Prometheus alerts: Ray worker OOM, ClickHouse replication lag > 60 seconds, experiment stuck (no episode_completed events for > 30 minutes). Slack notification to #payments-research on report_status=distributed only after approval.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: GKE Autopilot scales to zero Ray workers between training runs — no idle compute cost. Estimated cost per 2-week research cycle: Ray training (20 workers x 8 hours) ~$120 GKE Autopilot, ClickHouse cluster ~$200/month, Redis ~$30, MLflow GCS ~$10, Vertex AI GPT-4o per memo ~$2. Total ~$400/month for active research cycles. Scale to larger worker counts for hyperparameter sweeps at linear cost.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (4 weeks, completed): Tokenization pipeline, ClickHouse transactions table and materialized views, AcquirerRoutingEnv Gym environment, PPO training with Ray RLlib, basic evaluation CSV. Phase 2 (4 weeks, completed): holdout_flag partition enforcement (ISS-001 fix), bootstrap CI in PolicyEvaluator (ISS-002 fix), DQN variant, MLflow integration, Grafana dashboard. Phase 3 (current, 2 weeks): report_status state machine and human review gate (ISS-003 fix), GPT-4o memo generation via Vertex AI, research lead approval workflow, Slack distribution after approval. Phase 4 (planned): Shadow mode evaluation design document for production deployment decision.
target_output.development_guidelines: All FeatureEngineer and PolicyEvaluator functions must have unit tests with mocked ClickHouse responses — no live database calls in pytest. Reward function parameters (acceptance_penalty_weight) configurable via experiment config JSON, not hardcoded. ClickHouse queries use parameterized statements. Bootstrap CI uses scipy.stats.bootstrap with 1000 resamples and method='percentile'. LLM prompt template version-controlled in repo and reviewed on change. No direct Slack webhook calls outside the approved ExperimentReportDistributor service.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: AcquirerRoutingEnv replay fidelity — historical acceptance outcomes are deterministic, whereas production acceptance rates fluctuate. Mitigation: add Gaussian noise to acceptance probability during training to improve policy generalization. Risk: GPT-4o memo hallucinating metric values not in the structured prompt. Mitigation: prompt includes explicit instruction to cite only provided metrics; research lead review gate provides final human check. Tradeoff: Ray RLlib over custom training loop — reduces implementation effort but constrains reward function flexibility. Tradeoff: ClickHouse over a feature store like Feast — ClickHouse materialized views provide sufficient low-latency feature access for this scale without operational overhead.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: holdout_flag set at ingestion time on a stratified 20% random sample, partitioned by transaction_date to prevent time-series leakage. Bootstrap CI uses 1000 samples with percentile method; 95% CI is the reporting standard. Report approval gate enforced via report_status state machine — no Slack publish without researcher role approval action. GPT-4o memo includes confidence interval bounds explicitly to support Go/No-Go decision.

metadata.notes: RL payment routing prototype Round 4 fixing LLM report human review gate. ISS-001 and ISS-002 resolved in prior rounds; ISS-003 addressed here with report_status state machine.

project_goal.value: Serve a high-traffic public status page displaying real-time and historical uptime metrics for card, ACH, SEPA, and SWIFT payment rails, active incidents, and scheduled maintenance windows — with zero backend infrastructure.
project_goal.rationale: Core business requirement: transparent operational visibility for payment network customers without adding operational burden of a live backend.

target_users.value: Payment network customers, integration partners, and internal ops teams monitoring rail health
target_users.rationale: Primary consumers of status information identified in discovery.

project_class.rationale: No server-side runtime needed; all content generated at build time and served via CDN.

capabilities.rationale: Static frontend with embedded analytics; no auth, backend, or data capabilities required.

complexity_level.rationale: Static site generation with webhook-triggered rebuilds is straightforward.

risk_level.rationale: No user data stored; public read-only content only.

data_sensitivity.rationale: Status page displays operational metrics only; no PII or financial data.

external_exposure.rationale: Status page must be accessible without authentication to all internet users.

access_model.value: Public read-only; no authentication required for page views
access_model.rationale: Status pages must remain accessible even during auth system outages.

feature_scope.value: Real-time rail status indicators (card, ACH, SEPA, SWIFT), incident timeline, historical uptime charts per rail, scheduled maintenance calendar, email and webhook subscriber notifications
feature_scope.rationale: Complete operational transparency feature set.

mvp_scope.value: Rail status indicators, active incident display, historical uptime for the last 90 days, webhook-triggered rebuild pipeline
mvp_scope.rationale: Core status visibility sufficient for Q2 launch.

security_baseline.value: HTTPS enforced via CloudFront, S3 bucket private with OAC, Content-Security-Policy headers, no user data collected
security_baseline.rationale: Minimal attack surface appropriate for a public read-only site.

frontend_stack.value: Astro 4 with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS
frontend_stack.rationale: Astro's island architecture gives optimal static output with minimal JavaScript for status display components.

backend_stack.value: None — static site with no server-side runtime
backend_stack.rationale: Static website project class; all data baked in at build time from upstream incident management API.

data_platform.value: None — no database; uptime data fetched from upstream incident management API at build time
data_platform.rationale: Static generation model eliminates database dependency.

hosting_target.value: CloudFront distribution with S3 origin in us-east-1 and eu-west-1 for geo-redundancy
hosting_target.rationale: CDN edge delivery handles millions of page views without scaling concerns.

privacy_retention_policy.value: No user data collected; CloudFront access logs retained 30 days for traffic analysis only
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: No PII processed; minimal log retention for operational purposes.

future_scope.value: RSS/Atom feed for incident updates, embeddable status badge for partner documentation, mobile push notification channel
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 engagement features post-launch.

constraints.value: Must achieve sub-2-minute publish latency from incident declaration to live page; CloudFront cache invalidation budget must not exceed $50/month; no server-side runtime permitted
constraints.rationale: Operational SLAs and cost guardrails defined by platform team.

observability_baseline.value: AWS CloudWatch for CloudFront request metrics, GitHub Actions workflow alerting for failed rebuild pipelines, Statuspage internal health check
observability_baseline.rationale: Lightweight observability appropriate for a static site.

execution_preference.value: Trunk-based development with automated deploys on main branch merge
execution_preference.rationale: Simple CI/CD model matching static site lifecycle.

project.goal: Zero-backend public status page for payment rails with webhook-triggered rebuild
project.target_users: Payment network customers, partners, internal ops

security.headers: CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options via CloudFront response headers policy
security.storage: S3 bucket private with Origin Access Control

data.source: Upstream incident management API fetched at build time
data.format: JSON build-time data injection via Astro content collections

devops.environments: staging (S3 staging bucket), production (S3 prod bucket + CloudFront)
