target_output.data_model: authorization_events (event_id UUID, network_transaction_id VARCHAR(36) INDEX, event_type ENUM authorization, pan_token VARCHAR(64), amount_cents BIGINT, currency_code CHAR(3), issuer_bank_id VARCHAR(20), merchant_id VARCHAR(20), mcc SMALLINT, approval_code CHAR(6), response_code CHAR(2), message_timestamp TIMESTAMP, received_at TIMESTAMP, schema_version SMALLINT, aws_region CHAR(10)). clearing_files (event_id UUID, network_transaction_id FK, clearing_amount_cents BIGINT, interchange_amount_cents BIGINT, settlement_date DATE, clearing_timestamp TIMESTAMP, issuer_bank_id VARCHAR(20)). settlement_records (batch_id UUID, settlement_date DATE, issuer_bank_id, net_amount_cents BIGINT, transaction_count INT, file_reference). issuer_connections (bank_id VARCHAR(20) PK, bank_name, aws_region ENUM us-east-1/eu-west-1, connection_status ENUM connected/reconnecting/degraded, last_heartbeat TIMESTAMP, messages_today BIGINT, cert_expiry DATE). bank_region_assignments (bank_id VARCHAR(20) PK, aws_region ENUM us-east-1/eu-west-1, msks_bootstrap_servers, effective_from DATE). schema_versions (version SMALLINT, bank_id VARCHAR(20), field_mapping_json JSONB, effective_from DATE, PK (version, bank_id)). processing_checkpoints (job_id VARCHAR, checkpoint_id BIGINT, checkpoint_path VARCHAR, checkpoint_timestamp TIMESTAMP, state_size_bytes BIGINT).
target_output.api_design: Internal gRPC: TokenizationService.TokenizePan(PanTokenizeRequest{pan, correlation_id}) → PanTokenizeResponse{pan_token, vault_reference}. Admin HTTP (internal): GET /v1/banks/{bank_id}/status → {connection_status, last_heartbeat, messages_today, cert_expiry_days}. GET /v1/consumers/{group_id}/lag → {topic, partition, lag}[] — used by Datadog check. POST /v1/banks/{bank_id}/region-assignment → update bank_region_assignments (admin only, audit-logged). GET /v1/flink/jobs → Flink REST API proxy for job status and checkpoint state.
target_output.security_and_compliance: PCI CDE: ISO 8583 receivers and CloudHSM in dedicated PCI VPC subnet with no egress except HSM cluster port 2223 and MSK port 9094. PAN never leaves CDE subnet in plaintext. Kafka topics contain only pan_token (never PAN). mTLS: bank TCP connections validated against bank CA bundle from ACM PCA; CRL checked on connection establishment; certificate expiry alerts 30 days before. Kafka ACLs: per consumer group, enforced via Kafka ACL resource patterns. EU residency enforcement: NLB target group routes EU bank_id prefixes to eu-west-1 receiver instances; bank_region_assignments DynamoDB table serves as authoritative routing registry; integration test asserts no EU bank event is produced to us-east-1 Kafka. Annual PCI QSA audit evidence: CloudTrail for MSK topic production, bank_region_assignments audit log in DynamoDB Streams → CloudWatch Logs.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: us-east-1: MSK 3-broker (kafka.r6g.2xlarge, unclean.leader.election.enable=false), 8 x ISO 8583 receiver EC2 c6i.4xlarge in PCI VPC private subnet behind NLB, Flink NormalizationJob + AuthClearingJoinFlinkJob on EMR 7.x on EKS (node groups: c6i.4xlarge for NormalizationJob, r6i.4xlarge with NVMe for AuthClearingJoinJob). eu-west-1: mirror MSK cluster, 4 x ISO 8583 receiver instances, Flink NormalizationJob (EU events only). Jenkins pipeline: unit tests (JUnit5 + Flink MiniCluster test harness) → integration tests (EmbeddedKafka + Testcontainers CloudHSM mock) → Docker build + ECR push → deploy to staging EKS namespace. Flink job deployment: submit via Flink REST API with savepoint from current version; canary route 10% partition subset to new job version; monitor normalization_error_rate; promote or rollback within 30-minute window. ISO 8583 receiver: blue-green EC2 deploy with NLB connection draining (300s) to avoid dropping bank connections mid-stream.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM: Java traces for ISO8583ReceiverService (span: decode, tokenize, kafka_produce), NormalizationFlinkJob operator traces. Grafana / Prometheus: Flink job metrics (events_per_second_per_bank, normalization_latency_p50/p95/p99, checkpoint_duration_ms, checkpoint_size_bytes, RocksDB_state_size_bytes, auth_clearing_match_rate, late_event_drop_rate). MSK CloudWatch: BytesInPerSec per broker, UnderReplicatedPartitions (alert >0), ConsumerGroupLag per consumer group (alert if analytics-consumer-group lag >10K). Custom Datadog alerts: tokenization_timeout_rate >0.01% → P2, unmatched_auth_rate >0.1% → P2, RocksDB_state_size_bytes >400GB → warning, >480GB → P1, EU bank event produced to us-east-1 topic → P1 immediate.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated monthly: MSK us-east-1 (3 x r6g.2xlarge brokers) ~$2800, MSK eu-west-1 ~$1400, EC2 receivers (12 instances c6i.4xlarge) ~$6K, Flink EKS nodes (24 x r6i.4xlarge) ~$28K, ClickHouse (3 x i4i.4xlarge) ~$8K, ElastiCache Redis ~$800, CloudHSM (2 HSMs) ~$3K, S3 + data transfer ~$2K. Total ~$52K/month vs. $80K budget. At 2x network growth (400 banks), scale MSK to 6 brokers and Flink parallelism to 128; incremental cost ~$15K/month. S3 Intelligent-Tiering for raw event archives after 90 days.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 — Core Ingestion and Normalization (months 1-6): ISO 8583 Netty receiver with mTLS for 50 pilot banks, CloudHSM tokenization, Kafka exactly-once producer, NormalizationFlinkJob, bank_region_assignments routing (ISS-001 resolved), canonical-events topic delivery to analytics consumer. ClickHouse ingestion for analytics hot storage. Phase 2 — Auth-Clearing Join and Reconciliation (months 7-12): AuthClearingJoinFlinkJob with analytically sized RocksDB state (ISS-002 resolved), matched-transactions and reconciliation-exceptions topics, full 200+ bank onboarding, Flink canary deployment process for production jobs. Phase 3 — Scale and Resilience (months 13-18): eu-west-1 full deployment with EU bank routing enforcement, Flink savepoint compatibility test suite, burst capacity auto-scaling, PCI QSA audit pass, legacy event router decommission.
target_output.development_guidelines: Java 21 with strict null-safety via @NonNull annotations. JUnit5 for unit tests; Flink MiniCluster test harness for operator unit tests. Integration tests use EmbeddedKafka (kafka-streams-test-utils) and Testcontainers for ClickHouse and Redis. CI gate: zero new Kafka topics without documented consumer contract in schema registry. Bank field mapping rules stored as YAML in git, loaded into schema_versions at deploy time; mapping changes require data engineering review. RocksDB state backend configuration (state.backend.rocksdb.localdir, state.checkpoints.dir) version-controlled in Flink job Helm values, not hardcoded. All ISO 8583 field parsing logic in unit-tested modules separate from Netty channel handler.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Flink savepoint incompatibility during job version upgrade causes cold restart from Kafka earliest offset, reprocessing 72 hours of events. Mitigation: Avro schema backward compatibility enforced, savepoint restore tested in staging with production-scale state dump before each deployment. Risk: CloudHSM node failure causes tokenization latency spike exceeding 100ms P99. Mitigation: 2-HSM cluster for HA; HSM connection pool pre-warmed to 50 sessions per receiver instance; circuit breaker halts Kafka produce if tokenization latency P99 >80ms. Tradeoff: per-region MSK clusters vs. MirrorMaker replication — per-region chosen to enforce data residency without relying on MirrorMaker filtering logic. Tradeoff: Flink interval join vs. custom windowed aggregation — interval join chosen for exactly-once guarantees and built-in late-arrival handling.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: EU residency routing: bank_region_assignments DynamoDB table with Redis cache selected over IP-range-based routing for explicit auditability. RocksDB state: 500GB NVMe EBS gp3 per task manager confirmed via capacity analysis. Schema registry: Redis-backed version cache with DynamoDB source of truth chosen over Confluent Schema Registry to avoid additional managed service dependency.

metadata.notes: Round 2 high-scale data pipeline; resolves ISS-001 (EU data residency routing via bank_region_assignments) and ISS-002 (RocksDB state analytically sized for burst) for card network ISO 8583 streaming pipeline.

profile.domain: Tier-1 Bank Digital Wallet Mobile App

project_goal.value: Digital wallet mobile app for a tier-1 bank's 20 million retail customers supporting stored-value top-ups, peer-to-peer transfers, QR code payments at merchants, and bill split requests — with sub-200ms transaction response time.
project_goal.rationale: Strategic digital banking initiative to reduce card transaction fees and increase app engagement.

target_users.value: 20 million retail banking customers, merchants accepting QR payments, internal fraud analysts
target_users.rationale: Primary end users defined in product discovery and merchant pilot program.

project_class.rationale: Native iOS and Android apps with Go backend; mobile-first user interaction model.

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, auth, payments, data, realtime, analytics, integrations
capabilities.rationale: Full wallet capability set including native apps, payment processing, biometric auth, real-time push, and accounting system integrations.

complexity_level.rationale: 20M active users, sub-200ms transaction SLA, atomic wallet debit/credit operations, real-time bill split settlement.

risk_level.rationale: PCI-DSS Level 1, central bank stored-value license, AML monitoring obligations.

data_sensitivity.rationale: Wallet balances, transaction history, linked bank accounts, and payment credentials are financial data.

external_exposure.rationale: Mobile app accessed by retail customers over public internet; API gateway is internet-facing.

access_model.value: Custom JWT (15-min expiry) + biometric re-authentication for transactions above $100; device binding via hardware-backed keystore; refresh token rotation with device fingerprint validation
access_model.rationale: PCI-DSS and central bank license require strong authentication with step-up for high-value transactions.

feature_scope.value: Wallet top-up from linked bank account, P2P transfer to contacts or phone number, QR code generation and scanning for merchant payments, bill split requests with real-time share settlement tracking, transaction history, spending limits, push notifications
feature_scope.rationale: Core wallet feature set for launch.

mvp_scope.value: Wallet top-up, P2P transfer, QR merchant payment, transaction history. Bill split and advanced analytics in phase 2.
mvp_scope.rationale: Core payment flows validated before secondary features.

security_baseline.value: PCI-DSS Level 1 CDE, TLS 1.3 mobile-to-API, certificate pinning on iOS and Android, hardware-backed key storage for device keys, biometric auth for transactions, AML transaction monitoring integration
security_baseline.rationale: Central bank license and PCI obligations define security baseline.

frontend_stack.value: Swift 5.9 (iOS 17+), Kotlin 1.9 with Jetpack Compose (Android 10+)
frontend_stack.rationale: Native apps required for hardware-backed keystore access (iOS Secure Enclave, Android StrongBox) for payment auth.

backend_stack.value: Go 1.22 with gRPC and REST, transaction service with distributed ledger pattern
backend_stack.rationale: Go chosen for low-latency transaction processing; gRPC for mobile-to-backend and service-to-service.

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 (primary financial ledger), Redis 7 (wallet balance cache and idempotency keys), GCP Pub/Sub for real-time event distribution
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL ACID transactions for ledger integrity; Redis for sub-millisecond balance reads.

hosting_target.value: GKE Autopilot in asia-south1 (primary) and asia-east1 (DR), Cloud Spanner for multi-region ledger
hosting_target.rationale: GKE Autopilot reduces node management; Cloud Spanner for globally consistent multi-region ledger if scale demands.

privacy_retention_policy.value: Transaction records 7 years per financial regulation; wallet balance snapshots 2 years; device registration logs 1 year; biometric templates never stored server-side
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Central bank data retention requirements; biometric templates processed on-device only.

future_scope.value: Merchant analytics dashboard, loyalty points integration, cross-border remittance, credit product upsell within wallet app
future_scope.rationale: Phase 3 monetization features.

constraints.value: Sub-200ms P99 transaction response end-to-end; 20M active users with 500K concurrent sessions at peak; zero downtime during deployments; central bank mandated AML screening within 5 seconds of transaction authorization
constraints.rationale: SLA commitments and regulatory timeline obligations.

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM for Go services, Firebase Analytics for mobile event tracking, GCP Cloud Monitoring for infrastructure, custom transaction latency histograms per payment type
observability_baseline.rationale: Datadog corporate standard; Firebase for mobile funnel analytics.

execution_preference.value: Canary deployments for backend services; staged App Store/Play Store rollouts starting at 1% of users
execution_preference.rationale: 20M user base requires gradual rollout to contain regression impact.

compliance_context.value: PCI-DSS Level 1: CDE segmentation, annual QSA, tokenization. Central bank stored-value license: daily float reporting, transaction limit enforcement. AML: real-time transaction monitoring via Actimize integration, 5-second screening SLA, suspicious activity report filing. GDPR/data localisation: all customer data in-country.
compliance_context.rationale: PCI Level 1 and central bank license obligations trigger compliance context.

project.goal: Native digital wallet app for 20M retail bank customers with sub-200ms transactions
project.target_users: Retail customers, QR merchants, fraud analysts

frontend.ios: Swift 5.9, Combine, AVFoundation for QR scanner
frontend.android: Kotlin 1.9, Jetpack Compose, CameraX for QR scanner
frontend.auth: iOS Secure Enclave + FaceID/TouchID; Android StrongBox + BiometricPrompt

security.pci: CDE segmentation, no card data stored in wallet backend

data.ledger: PostgreSQL 16 with advisory locks for atomic debits
data.cache: Redis 7 cluster for wallet balance reads and idempotency keys
data.events: GCP Pub/Sub for payment events to analytics and fraud

reasoner_reviews.product: The QR payment flow is the highest-value MVP feature for merchant adoption. The sub-200ms P99 requirement is the primary engineering constraint — it must drive all latency budget allocation decisions. Bill split real-time settlement tracking requires WebSocket or Firebase Realtime connection; this dependency should be surfaced in the architecture.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The wallet debit/credit operation must be a single PostgreSQL transaction with advisory locking on the wallet row to prevent race conditions at 500K concurrent sessions. Redis caches the wallet balance for reads (invalidated on every write) to avoid hitting PostgreSQL on every balance display. The QR payment flow latency budget: JWT validation ~2ms, Redis balance check ~1ms, PostgreSQL advisory lock + debit/credit ~15ms, AML async pre-screen ~3ms, Pub/Sub event ~5ms, gRPC response ~2ms = ~28ms P95. The 200ms P99 budget has ample headroom.
reasoner_reviews.security: Certificate pinning must be implemented with backup pins for rotation without forced app update. The biometric step-up for transactions over $100 must use the hardware-backed key (Secure Enclave / StrongBox) to sign the transaction authorization — the server verifies the signature using the registered device public key. AML screening must be synchronous-or-timeout: if Actimize does not respond within 5s, the transaction must be held pending review, not approved.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 500K concurrent sessions require Redis cluster mode with at least 3 shards for idempotency key storage. PostgreSQL must be configured with connection pooling (PgBouncer, max_pool_size=200) to handle connection surge without exhausting PostgreSQL max_connections. GKE Autopilot auto-scales pods; ensure HPA is configured on custom transaction_rate metric, not just CPU.

frontend.app_structure: Modular iOS: WalletKit framework (balance, transactions), PayKit framework (QR, P2P, top-up), AuthKit framework (biometric, device registration). Android: multi-module Gradle project mirroring iOS framework boundaries.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Home (balance, recent transactions), Send Money (contact/phone lookup, amount, biometric confirm), QR Scanner (camera, decode, amount confirm, biometric step-up), Top-Up (linked account selection, amount, bank OAuth), Bill Split (group creation, amount entry, share assignment, real-time status), Transaction History (filterable list, detail view).
frontend.state_management: iOS: Combine publishers for wallet balance stream; Swift concurrency for async payment flows. Android: StateFlow in ViewModel layer; Coroutines for payment service calls. Both platforms: optimistic UI update on payment submission, rollback on error.
frontend.frontend_security_notes: Certificate pinning: iOS via TrustKit with backup pin; Android via OkHttp CertificatePinner with 2 backup pins. JWT stored in iOS Keychain (kSecAttrAccessibleWhenUnlockedThisDeviceOnly); Android EncryptedSharedPreferences. Biometric: iOS LocalAuthentication with LAPolicy deviceOwnerAuthenticationWithBiometrics; Android BiometricManager.canAuthenticate(BIOMETRIC_STRONG).
frontend.frontend_risks: Risk: certificate pin expiry forces forced app update if backup pins not configured. Mitigation: 2 backup pins always active, pin rotation tested in staging 30 days before production rotation. Risk: QR scanner camera permission denial causes payment flow failure. Mitigation: graceful fallback to manual payment link entry.

backend.service_design: WalletService (Go): manages wallet balances in PostgreSQL, Redis balance cache, handles top-up and debit/credit operations with advisory locking. TransactionService (Go): records transaction history, idempotency key management in Redis. QRPaymentService (Go): generates time-limited QR payloads (JWT, 5-min expiry), processes QR scans and initiates WalletService debit/credit. BillSplitService (Go): creates bill split sessions, tracks participant share payments, publishes real-time status updates via Pub/Sub to Firebase Realtime Database for mobile websocket delivery.
backend.api_patterns: gRPC for mobile-to-backend (WalletService, QRPaymentService). REST (Gin) for third-party integrations (bank top-up OAuth callback, Actimize AML webhook). Idempotency: all mutation RPCs accept idempotency-key header stored in Redis with 24h TTL; duplicate requests return cached response.
backend.failure_handling: Wallet debit failure (insufficient funds): immediate gRPC error FAILED_PRECONDITION with balance. Advisory lock timeout (50ms): retry once, then return RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED. Actimize AML timeout (5s): transaction status set to PENDING_AML_REVIEW, user notified via push, analyst review queue populated. Bank top-up failure: idempotent retry with exponential backoff; compensating transaction if partial debit occurred.
backend.scaling_notes: WalletService: horizontal scaling to 50 pods on GKE Autopilot; HPA on transaction_rate_per_second custom metric. PgBouncer connection pooler (transaction mode) deployed as DaemonSet, max_pool_size=200 per node. Redis cluster: 6 shards (3 masters + 3 replicas) for balance cache and idempotency keys.

security.auth_design: Device registration: app generates ECDSA P-256 key pair in hardware enclave on first launch, uploads public key to DeviceRegistry service. Transaction auth: app signs transaction payload hash with device private key; server verifies signature using registered public key. JWT: 15-min expiry, RS256, signed by internal key rotation service. Biometric step-up: triggers on transactions > $100; re-signs challenge with device key after biometric verification.
security.authorization_model: Customer: access own wallet only (wallet_id == jwt.sub). Merchant: receive QR payment credits only (no balance read access). Fraud analyst: read-only access to transaction history and AML flags via internal admin API. No cross-customer data access permitted.
security.abuse_prevention: Rate limiting: 10 payment attempts per minute per device_id (Redis sliding window). Velocity checks: >5 P2P transfers to new recipients in 24h triggers AML flag. QR payload replay prevention: QR JWT has 5-min expiry and single-use nonce stored in Redis.
security.security_risks: Risk: device key compromise if device is rooted/jailbroken. Mitigation: SafetyNet/Play Integrity attestation check on Android before device registration; iOS jailbreak detection via file system checks. Risk: AML timeout causes fraudulent transactions to slip through pending review. Mitigation: PENDING_AML_REVIEW transactions are held (not settled) until analyst clears them; maximum 5-second hold.

data.entities: wallets (id UUID PK, customer_id UUID UNIQUE, balance_cents BIGINT, currency_code CHAR(3), status ENUM active/frozen/closed, version BIGINT for OCC, updated_at TIMESTAMP). top_up_transactions (id UUID PK, wallet_id FK, linked_account_id FK, amount_cents BIGINT, bank_reference, status ENUM pending/completed/failed, initiated_at, completed_at). p2p_transfers (id UUID PK, sender_wallet_id FK, recipient_wallet_id FK, amount_cents BIGINT, note VARCHAR(140), idempotency_key UUID UNIQUE, status ENUM pending/completed/failed, initiated_at, completed_at). qr_payment_sessions (id UUID PK, merchant_wallet_id FK, qr_payload_jwt TEXT, amount_cents BIGINT, nonce UUID UNIQUE, expires_at TIMESTAMP, status ENUM open/completed/expired). bill_split_requests (id UUID PK, initiator_wallet_id FK, total_amount_cents BIGINT, description VARCHAR(140), created_at). spending_limits (wallet_id FK, limit_type ENUM daily/per_transaction, amount_cents BIGINT, effective_from DATE). linked_bank_accounts (id UUID PK, wallet_id FK, bank_id, masked_account_number, oauth_token_encrypted, verified_at TIMESTAMP).
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16: wallets and all transaction tables. Advisory lock on wallets.id for debit/credit atomicity. Redis 7 cluster: wallet balance cache (wallet:{id}:balance, invalidate on every write), idempotency keys (idempotency:{key}, 24h TTL), QR nonces (qr_nonce:{nonce}, 5-min TTL). GCP Pub/Sub: payment-events topic (analytics, fraud consumers), bill-split-events topic (Firebase Realtime Database bridge for mobile).
data.data_risks: Risk: Redis balance cache stale if write to PostgreSQL succeeds but Redis invalidation fails. Mitigation: Write-through pattern with Redis DEL after successful PostgreSQL commit; on cache miss, read from PostgreSQL and re-populate. Risk: PostgreSQL advisory lock contention on popular merchant wallet during QR payment burst. Mitigation: separate merchant wallet shard by wallet_id hash prefix; PgBouncer transaction mode limits connection hold time.

devops.deployment_topology: GKE Autopilot asia-south1: WalletService (50 pods), TransactionService (30 pods), QRPaymentService (20 pods), BillSplitService (10 pods), PgBouncer DaemonSet. Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 (primary asia-south1 + read replica asia-east1). Redis cluster 6 shards ElastiCache equivalent on GCP Memorystore. asia-east1: DR standby with 15-min RTO.
devops.ci_cd_design: CircleCI: Go unit tests + race detector → integration tests (Testcontainers PostgreSQL + Redis) → docker build + GCR push → deploy to staging GKE with Helm → Datadog synthetic test suite → canary deploy production (1% pods) → automated rollback if p99_latency_ms > 200. iOS/Android: Fastlane builds → TestFlight/Firebase App Distribution for staging → staged App Store/Play Store rollout at 1%, 5%, 20%, 100%.
devops.devops_risks: Risk: GKE Autopilot scale-out latency during sudden demand spike (e.g., payday top-up surge). Mitigation: pre-scale WalletService pods to minimum 20 from 06:00-10:00 local time on 1st and 15th of month via KEDA cron scaler. Risk: Cloud SQL failover to read replica promotes stale data without advisory lock state. Mitigation: all write paths retry on connection error with idempotency key; no advisory lock state survives failover.

ISS-001.title: AML timeout path approves transactions without screening completion
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 architecture specified AML screening via Actimize with a 5-second SLA but the failure path on timeout was not fully specified. The transaction must be held in PENDING_AML_REVIEW status on Actimize timeout, not approved. Round 1 plan was ambiguous about whether timeout results in approval or hold.

history.note: Identified in round 1 audit: AML timeout handling described as fire-and-forget; central bank regulation requires hold-not-approve on screening timeout.

ISS-002.title: QR payment nonce replay prevention not implemented at persistence layer
ISS-002.detail: QR payloads have a 5-minute JWT expiry but no single-use enforcement at the server. The same QR code could be scanned and paid multiple times within the 5-minute window if the nonce is not checked and stored in Redis with atomic SET NX before transaction processing.

history.note: Identified in round 1 audit: QR JWT expiry specified but nonce single-use enforcement mechanism not described.

focus_issues.title: AML timeout path approves transactions without screening completion
focus_issues.detail: Actimize timeout must result in PENDING_AML_REVIEW hold, not automatic approval. Round 1 was ambiguous.

focus_issues.title: QR payment nonce replay prevention not implemented at persistence layer
focus_issues.detail: QR nonce single-use check (Redis SET NX) not specified before transaction processing begins.

latest_recommendations: Explicitly specify AML timeout behavior: on Actimize non-response after 5s, set transaction.status=PENDING_AML_REVIEW, return 202 Accepted to mobile with status polling URL, notify fraud analyst queue, do not settle wallet debit until AML clearance received.
Add Redis SET NX nonce check as the first step in QR payment processing before any wallet operation begins; if SET NX returns 0 (nonce already used), return ALREADY_PROCESSED error immediately.

convergence_state.reason: Two high-severity security issues unresolved; AML timeout and QR replay are both regulatory and fraud risks.

previous_audits.summary: Solid high-scale wallet architecture with correct PostgreSQL advisory locking and Redis balance cache pattern. Two high-severity security gaps: AML timeout handling ambiguous, QR nonce replay unaddressed.
strengths: PostgreSQL advisory lock for atomic wallet debit/credit is correct pattern for 500K concurrent sessions
Redis idempotency key pattern for P2P transfers is well-specified
Certificate pinning with backup pins correctly addresses rotation risk
concerns: AML Actimize timeout path not fully specified — regulatory risk if timeout results in approval
QR JWT expiry alone insufficient; server-side nonce single-use check not described
blocking_issues: ISS-001: AML timeout could allow unscreened transactions to settle
ISS-002: QR nonce replay could allow double payment within 5-minute JWT validity window
recommendations: Define explicit state machine for transaction status: INITIATED → AML_SCREENING → (AML_CLEARED → SETTLING → COMPLETED) or (AML_TIMEOUT → PENDING_AML_REVIEW → [analyst_clear → SETTLING] or [analyst_reject → FAILED]).
Implement Redis SET NX on qr_nonce:{nonce} as atomic gate before beginning any QR payment flow; TTL = QR JWT expiry + 60s buffer.

issue_updates.title: AML timeout path undefined
issue_updates.detail: Round 1 described Actimize integration but did not specify the 5-second timeout state transition.

issue_updates.title: QR nonce replay prevention missing
issue_updates.detail: 5-minute QR JWT expiry is necessary but not sufficient; server-side nonce check not specified.

previous_plan.title: Digital Wallet Architecture v1
previous_plan.executive_summary: Native Swift/Kotlin wallet app with Go gRPC backend on GKE Autopilot, PostgreSQL 16 ledger with advisory locking for atomicity, Redis balance cache, and Actimize AML integration for 20M retail banking customers.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Mobile apps communicate via gRPC to Go microservices on GKE. PostgreSQL advisory locks prevent concurrent wallet debit race conditions. Redis caches wallet balances and stores idempotency keys. Actimize integration provides AML screening.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: mTLS inter-service, JWT 15-min expiry, biometric step-up for transactions >$100, certificate pinning with backup pins, PCI CDE segmentation, Actimize AML integration.
previous_plan.workflows: QR Payment: app scans QR, confirms amount, biometric step-up, WalletService debit/credit atomic PostgreSQL transaction, AML screening, Pub/Sub event, response within 200ms.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 revision targeting two high-severity security issues: ISS-001 (AML timeout must hold transaction, not approve) and ISS-002 (QR nonce single-use enforcement via Redis SET NX). Both require changes to QRPaymentService and TransactionService flow logic. The AML fix requires a formal transaction state machine (INITIATED → AML_SCREENING → AML_CLEARED/PENDING_AML_REVIEW → SETTLING → COMPLETED/FAILED/REJECTED). The QR nonce fix requires Redis SET NX as the atomic first gate before any wallet operation, with TTL = qr_payload expiry + 60s. These are security-critical changes that also touch the mobile app's handling of 202 Accepted responses for held transactions.

fix_report.action_taken: Defined formal transaction status state machine: INITIATED → AML_SCREENING (Actimize call dispatched) → [AML_CLEARED → SETTLING → COMPLETED] or [AML_TIMEOUT (5s elapsed) → PENDING_AML_REVIEW (wallet debit held, 202 returned to mobile with poll URL)] or [AML_REJECTED → FAILED (wallet debit reversed)]. On AML_TIMEOUT, the PostgreSQL transaction is rolled back (debit not committed), transaction record status set to PENDING_AML_REVIEW, fraud analyst queue notified via Pub/Sub, mobile client polls /v1/transactions/{id}/status. Analyst clears → SETTLING path resumes. Added state machine diagram to security_and_compliance and workflows sections.
fix_report.expected_outcome: No unscreened transaction can settle. AML timeout results in a held transaction requiring explicit analyst clearance, satisfying central bank stored-value license AML obligation.

fix_report.action_taken: Added Redis SET NX as the first server-side operation in QRPaymentService.ProcessQrPayment: SETNX qr_nonce:{nonce_from_jwt} 1 EX {qr_jwt_expiry + 60s}. If SET NX returns 0 (nonce already claimed), immediately return gRPC ALREADY_EXISTS error. Only if SET NX returns 1 does the service proceed to JWT signature verification, balance check, and wallet debit. This makes nonce consumption atomic and prevents any race condition between concurrent scans of the same QR code. nonce stored in qr_payment_sessions table on session creation. Added integration test: concurrent scan of same QR code by two devices results in exactly one successful payment.
fix_report.expected_outcome: A merchant QR code can be paid exactly once within its 5-minute validity window. Double-scan race conditions are prevented by the Redis atomic gate, eliminating double-debit risk for the merchant and customer.

target_output.title: Digital Wallet Architecture v2
target_output.executive_summary: Native Swift/Kotlin digital wallet app for 20 million retail bank customers backed by Go gRPC microservices on GKE Autopilot with PostgreSQL 16 advisory-locked ledger, Redis balance cache, and hardened payment security controls. Round 2 resolves AML timeout hold behavior (ISS-001) via explicit transaction state machine and QR payment double-scan prevention (ISS-002) via Redis atomic nonce gate — both critical for PCI-DSS Level 1 and central bank stored-value license compliance.
target_output.architecture_overview: Native iOS (Swift 5.9) and Android (Kotlin 1.9 / Jetpack Compose) apps communicate over TLS 1.3 with gRPC certificate pinning to Go microservices on GKE Autopilot in asia-south1. WalletService manages wallet balances using PostgreSQL 16 advisory locks for atomic debit/credit operations; Redis 7 cluster caches balances for sub-millisecond reads. QRPaymentService implements a Redis atomic nonce gate (SET NX) as the first operation before any wallet transaction, preventing QR code double-spend. All payment transactions pass through a formal AML state machine: INITIATED → AML_SCREENING (Actimize, 5s timeout) → AML_CLEARED → SETTLING → COMPLETED, or PENDING_AML_REVIEW on timeout requiring analyst clearance before settlement. GCP Pub/Sub distributes payment events to analytics and fraud consumers. 20M user scale handled via GKE Autopilot horizontal pod autoscaling on custom transaction_rate metric.
target_output.technology_stack: Mobile: Swift 5.9 with Combine + AVFoundation (iOS), Kotlin 1.9 with Jetpack Compose + CameraX (Android). Auth: iOS Secure Enclave ECDSA P-256, Android StrongBox ECDSA P-256, BiometricPrompt. Backend: Go 1.22 with gRPC (ConnectRPC), Gin for REST callbacks. Data: PostgreSQL 16 (Cloud SQL, advisory locks), Redis 7 cluster (Memorystore, 6 shards). Messaging: GCP Pub/Sub (payment-events, bill-split-events, aml-review-queue topics). Hosting: GKE Autopilot asia-south1 + DR asia-east1. AML: Actimize REST API integration. CI/CD: CircleCI. Monitoring: Datadog APM, Firebase Analytics, GCP Cloud Monitoring.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Wallet Dashboard: real-time balance from Redis cache, 10 most recent transactions, spending meter against daily limit. Top-Up: linked bank account OAuth flow, amount selection, bank transfer initiation, webhook confirmation updates transaction to COMPLETED. P2P Transfer: contact/phone lookup, amount + note entry, biometric step-up (>$100), AML screening, atomic PostgreSQL debit/credit, push notification to recipient. QR Merchant Payment: camera scan via AVFoundation/CameraX, JWT decode and expiry check, Redis SET NX nonce gate, merchant name and amount display, biometric confirmation, atomic wallet debit + merchant credit, AML screening, sub-200ms response. Bill Split: group and amount entry, per-participant share assignment, real-time settlement status via Firebase Realtime Database stream, initiator notification when all shares paid. AML Review Queue: internal analyst UI showing PENDING_AML_REVIEW transactions with customer history, clear/reject actions.
target_output.system_components: WalletService (Go, 50 GKE pods): balance read (Redis) and write (PostgreSQL advisory lock), debit/credit operations, spending limit enforcement. QRPaymentService (Go, 20 pods): QR JWT generation (5-min HMAC-signed payload with nonce), QR scan processing with Redis SET NX nonce gate, WalletService debit/credit orchestration. TransactionService (Go, 30 pods): transaction record creation, status state machine management (INITIATED/AML_SCREENING/AML_CLEARED/PENDING_AML_REVIEW/SETTLING/COMPLETED/FAILED), idempotency key store. AMLService (Go, 10 pods): Actimize REST integration with 5-second timeout, state transition to PENDING_AML_REVIEW on timeout, Pub/Sub publish to aml-review-queue. BillSplitService (Go, 10 pods): split session management, Firebase Realtime Database bridge. NotificationService (Go, 10 pods): FCM/APNs push for payment events. PgBouncer (DaemonSet, transaction mode): PostgreSQL connection pooling. Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16: primary asia-south1 + read replica asia-east1. Memorystore Redis 7 cluster: 6 shards for balance cache and nonce/idempotency keys.
target_output.workflows: QR Merchant Payment Flow (target <200ms P99): Customer scans merchant QR code → app decodes JWT (HMAC-SHA256, 5-min expiry) → extracts nonce, amount, merchant_wallet_id → gRPC call to QRPaymentService.ProcessQrPayment → Step 1: Redis SETNX qr_nonce:{nonce} 1 EX 360 (returns 0 if already used → ALREADY_EXISTS error) → Step 2: verify JWT signature → Step 3: fetch balance from Redis (sub-1ms) → Step 4: display confirmation screen (~50ms round-trip) → customer biometric confirm → Step 5: WalletService.DebitCredit PostgreSQL advisory lock transaction (customer debit + merchant credit in single transaction) → Step 6: AMLService async screen with 5s timeout → if cleared: TransactionService status → COMPLETED, Pub/Sub event, gRPC COMPLETED response → app shows success (total ~120ms P95). AML Timeout Hold Flow: AMLService.ScreenTransaction call to Actimize → no response in 5s → TransactionService status → PENDING_AML_REVIEW → PostgreSQL debit/credit rolled back → 202 Accepted returned to mobile with poll_url /v1/transactions/{id}/status and estimated_review_time → Pub/Sub publish to aml-review-queue → fraud analyst reviews in internal dashboard → analyst CLEAR → TransactionService status → AML_CLEARED → SETTLING → WalletService reprocesses debit/credit → COMPLETED → push notification to customer. P2P Transfer Flow: sender selects recipient by phone number → phone number hash lookup in customer registry → amount + note entry → biometric step-up if >$100 → TransactionService.InitiateTransfer (idempotency key from device) → same AML state machine as above → on COMPLETED, FCM/APNs push to recipient app with deep link to transaction detail.
target_output.data_model: wallets (id UUID PK, customer_id UUID UNIQUE, balance_cents BIGINT NOT NULL CHECK(balance_cents >= 0), currency_code CHAR(3), status ENUM active/frozen/closed, version BIGINT, updated_at TIMESTAMP). transactions (id UUID PK, type ENUM top_up/p2p_transfer/qr_payment/bill_split_share, debit_wallet_id UUID FK, credit_wallet_id UUID FK, amount_cents BIGINT, idempotency_key UUID UNIQUE, status ENUM initiated/aml_screening/aml_cleared/pending_aml_review/settling/completed/failed, aml_reference VARCHAR(64), aml_screened_at TIMESTAMP, created_at TIMESTAMP, completed_at TIMESTAMP). qr_payment_sessions (id UUID PK, merchant_wallet_id UUID FK, nonce UUID UNIQUE NOT NULL, amount_cents BIGINT, expires_at TIMESTAMP, transaction_id UUID FK NULLABLE, status ENUM open/completed/expired). top_up_transactions (id UUID PK, wallet_id FK, linked_account_id FK, amount_cents BIGINT, bank_reference, status ENUM pending/completed/failed, initiated_at, completed_at). bill_split_requests (id UUID PK, initiator_wallet_id FK, total_amount_cents BIGINT, description VARCHAR(140), created_at). bill_split_shares (id UUID PK, request_id FK, participant_wallet_id FK, share_amount_cents BIGINT, transaction_id FK NULLABLE, paid_at TIMESTAMP NULLABLE). spending_limits (wallet_id FK PK, limit_type ENUM daily/per_transaction, amount_cents BIGINT). linked_bank_accounts (id UUID PK, wallet_id FK, bank_id, masked_account_number, oauth_token_encrypted, verified_at).
target_output.api_design: gRPC (ConnectRPC): WalletService.GetBalance(wallet_id) → {balance_cents, currency, updated_at}. WalletService.GetTransactionHistory(wallet_id, limit, cursor) → paginated transaction list. QRPaymentService.GenerateQrPayload(merchant_wallet_id, amount_cents) → {qr_jwt, nonce, expires_at}. QRPaymentService.ProcessQrPayment(qr_jwt, customer_wallet_id, idempotency_key) → {transaction_id, status, completed_at} or {transaction_id, status: pending_aml_review, poll_url} (202). TransactionService.GetTransactionStatus(transaction_id) → {status, aml_reference, completed_at} — for polling held transactions. REST (Gin): POST /v1/topup/callback (bank OAuth/webhook). GET /v1/transactions/{id}/status (mobile poll for PENDING_AML_REVIEW transactions). Internal: POST /v1/aml/review/{transaction_id}/clear|reject (fraud analyst action, JWT admin scope).
target_output.security_and_compliance: PCI CDE: WalletService and PostgreSQL in dedicated GKE namespace with NetworkPolicy allowing no egress to public internet; all outbound via internal service mesh. AML State Machine: INITIATED → AML_SCREENING (Actimize REST call, 5s timeout) → [AML_CLEARED → SETTLING → COMPLETED] | [AML_TIMEOUT → PENDING_AML_REVIEW, debit rolled back] | [AML_REJECTED → FAILED, debit rolled back]. No transaction settles without explicit AML_CLEARED status transition. QR Nonce Gate: Redis SETNX qr_nonce:{nonce} as first operation in ProcessQrPayment; concurrent scans return ALREADY_EXISTS. Certificate pinning: iOS TrustKit with 2 backup pins; Android OkHttp CertificatePinner with 2 backup pins; rotation tested in staging 30 days before production rotation. Biometric: ECDSA P-256 device key signing of transaction payload hash; server verifies against registered device public key in DeviceRegistry. Central bank compliance: daily float balance report generated by TransactionService aggregation job; transaction limits enforced in WalletService.DebitCredit before advisory lock.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: CircleCI: Go unit tests (race detector enabled) → integration tests (Testcontainers PostgreSQL 16 + Redis cluster) → docker build → GCR push → Helm deploy to staging GKE → Datadog synthetic transactions (top-up, QR payment, P2P) must pass → canary deploy production (1% pods via Argo Rollouts) → automated rollback if transaction_p99_latency_ms > 200 or error_rate > 0.1% → promote to 100% after 30-minute canary window. iOS: Fastlane gym build → TestFlight (staging) → staged App Store rollout 1% → 5% → 20% → 100% with Datadog crash rate gate per stage. Android: Fastlane supply → Firebase App Distribution (staging) → staged Play Store rollout. Cloud SQL: primary asia-south1, read replica asia-east1, automated failover with 15-min RTO. PgBouncer transaction mode DaemonSet: max_pool_size=200 per node, max_client_conn=5000.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM: Go service traces with span tags payment_type, wallet_id hash, aml_status. Custom Datadog metrics: transaction_p99_latency_ms by payment_type (alert >180ms warning, >200ms critical), aml_timeout_rate (alert >0.1%), qr_nonce_replay_attempts_rate (alert >0.01% — indicator of systematic replay attack), wallet_debit_failure_rate. Firebase Analytics: payment_initiated, payment_completed, payment_failed, qr_scan_success events for mobile funnel. GCP Cloud Monitoring: Cloud SQL connections, Redis memory utilization (alert >80%), GKE pod restart rate. PagerDuty escalation: P1 on transaction_p99_latency_ms >200ms for 2 consecutive minutes, P1 on aml_timeout_rate >1% (regulatory risk), P2 on redis_nonce_cache_hit_miss ratio anomaly.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: GKE Autopilot: ~$12K/month for 130 pods across services at steady state. Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 (db-custom-32-131072 primary + replica): ~$8K/month. Memorystore Redis 6 shards: ~$2K/month. GCP Pub/Sub: ~$200/month at 50M events/day. Cloud Load Balancing: ~$500/month. Datadog APM (200 hosts equivalent): ~$5K/month. Total ~$28K/month at 20M active users. At 3x growth (60M users): scale GKE pods to 390, Cloud SQL to db-custom-64-262144 (~+$8K), Redis to 12 shards (~+$2K); total ~$40K/month. GKE Autopilot pre-scale cron: 1st/15th of month 06:00-10:00 local (payday top-up surge), minimum WalletService pods = 80.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 — Core Wallet MVP (months 1-6): wallet top-up with bank OAuth, P2P transfer with AML state machine (ISS-001 resolved), PostgreSQL advisory lock debit/credit, Redis balance cache, JWT + biometric auth, device registration with hardware-backed keys, certificate pinning. Phase 2 — QR Payments and Bill Split (months 7-10): QR code generation and processing with Redis nonce gate (ISS-002 resolved), merchant wallet onboarding, bill split with Firebase Realtime Database real-time status, biometric step-up for >$100 transactions. Phase 3 — Analytics and Compliance (months 11-15): spending analytics and budget view, daily float reporting to central bank, AML analyst dashboard, staged App Store/Play Store rollout to full 20M user base, PCI QSA audit pass.
target_output.development_guidelines: Go: golangci-lint with errcheck, staticcheck, gosec. Race detector enabled in CI tests. Unit tests for all state machine transitions in TransactionService. Integration tests for concurrent PostgreSQL advisory lock contention using Testcontainers. iOS: SwiftLint + SwiftFormat. XCTest for payment flow unit tests; XCUITest for QR scan and biometric flow UI tests on physical device farm. Android: ktlint + detekt. JUnit5 + Espresso. Both platforms: snapshot tests for payment confirmation screens. No wallet balance or transaction data logged in plaintext — all log fields with financial content masked.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Redis Memorystore failover causes balance cache miss storm hitting PostgreSQL. Mitigation: cache aside pattern with circuit breaker; if Redis unavailable, fall back to PostgreSQL reads with connection pool limit; alert on Redis failover. Risk: PostgreSQL advisory lock timeout (50ms) under extreme concurrency for popular merchant wallet (e.g., large QR payment campaign). Mitigation: shard merchant wallets by wallet_id hash prefix; separate PgBouncer pools for merchant wallet IDs. Tradeoff: Cloud SQL PostgreSQL over Cloud Spanner — PostgreSQL chosen for advisory lock semantics and team familiarity; Cloud Spanner specified in hosting_target for future multi-region scale path. Tradeoff: Firebase Realtime Database for bill split status vs. WebSocket — Firebase chosen to avoid managing WebSocket connection state at 500K concurrent sessions.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: AML timeout behavior confirmed: hold (PENDING_AML_REVIEW) not approve — central bank mandate (ISS-001 resolved). QR nonce: Redis SET NX selected over PostgreSQL INSERT for sub-millisecond atomic check (ISS-002 resolved). Biometric step-up threshold confirmed at $100 per security team. Bill split real-time updates via Firebase Realtime Database confirmed over WebSocket for operational simplicity.

metadata.notes: Round 2 high-scale mobile wallet; resolves ISS-001 (AML timeout hold via state machine) and ISS-002 (QR nonce Redis SET NX gate) for tier-1 bank digital wallet with 20M users.

project_goal.value: Hardened payment link platform for law firms, consultants, and accountants to generate branded one-time payment links for client invoices, accept credit cards via Stripe-hosted fields (card data never touches practitioner servers), and automatically sync paid invoices to QuickBooks or Xero via OAuth.
project_goal.rationale: Professional services firms need PCI-compliant card acceptance without the complexity of full PCI compliance — Stripe payment links with server-side signature achieve SAQ A scope.

target_users.value: Professional services practitioners (lawyers, accountants, consultants) as platform users; their clients as payment link recipients
target_users.rationale: Dual user personas: practitioners manage invoices and links; clients pay via link without creating an account.

project_class.rationale: Practitioner dashboard (Next.js), public payment link page, Node.js API, and accounting integrations require fullstack architecture.

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, auth, payments, data, integrations, analytics
capabilities.rationale: Dashboard UI, API backend, Auth0 authentication, Stripe payment processing, PostgreSQL data layer, QuickBooks/Xero integration, and payment analytics.

complexity_level.rationale: Stripe Payment Intents + webhooks, OAuth accounting integration, and cryptographically signed URLs add moderate complexity over a standard CRUD app.

risk_level.rationale: PCI-DSS SAQ A compliance, financial data, and trust accounting rules for legal firms.

data_sensitivity.rationale: Invoice amounts, payment status, and accounting system sync data are financial records.

external_exposure.rationale: Payment link pages are accessed by clients who are not practitioners and have no account — fully public URLs.

access_model.value: Auth0 for practitioner authentication (email/password + optional MFA); payment link pages are public (no auth for clients); API routes protected by Auth0 JWT; accounting OAuth tokens stored per practitioner
access_model.rationale: Practitioners need persistent auth; clients pay without friction via the one-time link.

feature_scope.value: Practitioner dashboard: client management, invoice creation, payment link generation, payment status tracking, revenue analytics. Payment link page: branded with practitioner firm name, Stripe-hosted card fields, one-time payment, receipt email. Accounting sync: automatic QuickBooks and Xero invoice-paid sync via OAuth. Admin: practitioner onboarding, Stripe Connect account setup.
feature_scope.rationale: Complete feature set for professional services payment acceptance.

mvp_scope.value: Payment link generation with Stripe, practitioner dashboard with client and invoice CRUD, receipt email, QuickBooks sync. Xero and analytics in phase 2.
mvp_scope.rationale: QuickBooks dominant in target market; Xero phase 2 after validation.

security_baseline.value: PCI SAQ A: Stripe-hosted fields only, no PAN on server, HTTPS enforced, Stripe webhook signature verification. Auth0 with MFA option. AES-256 encryption for accounting OAuth tokens at rest. Content-Security-Policy blocking non-Stripe scripts on payment page. GDPR: consent banner for EU clients.
security_baseline.rationale: PCI SAQ A scope reduction via Stripe-hosted fields; GDPR for EU client data.

frontend_stack.value: Next.js 14 with TypeScript, App Router, Tailwind CSS
frontend_stack.rationale: Next.js App Router for practitioner dashboard SSR and public payment link pages; single framework for both surfaces.

backend_stack.value: Node.js 22 with Hono framework, REST API, Stripe Payment Intents
backend_stack.rationale: Hono chosen for lightweight handler composition; no ORM overhead; raw pg for PostgreSQL queries.

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 (RDS), Redis 7 (ElastiCache) for payment link nonce cache and rate limiting
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL for relational invoice and payment data; Redis for payment link single-use enforcement and API rate limiting.

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate for API, Vercel for Next.js, RDS PostgreSQL 16 in us-east-1
hosting_target.rationale: Vercel for optimal Next.js deployment; ECS Fargate for stateless API with AWS VPC alignment for RDS.

privacy_retention_policy.value: Invoice and payment records retained 7 years per financial records law; client email addresses retained while practitioner account active + 2 years; OAuth tokens for accounting systems deleted on practitioner account closure; GDPR deletion requests processed within 30 days for EU clients
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Financial records retention obligations and GDPR data subject rights.

future_scope.value: Xero integration, Stripe recurring payment links for retainer billing, trust accounting ledger view for law firms, mobile app for practitioners
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2/3 features after market validation.

constraints.value: PCI SAQ A scope: no card data touches application servers; payment link must be single-use (one successful payment per link); accounting sync must complete within 60 seconds of Stripe webhook confirmation; AWS us-east-1 only
constraints.rationale: PCI scope reduction strategy and accounting sync SLA for practitioner experience.

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM for ECS Fargate API, structured JSON logging, CloudWatch for RDS and infrastructure metrics, Stripe Dashboard for payment health
observability_baseline.rationale: Datadog corporate standard; Stripe Dashboard covers payment funnel metrics.

execution_preference.value: Two-week sprints with Stripe integration validated in sprint 1; accounting sync validated in sprint 3
execution_preference.rationale: Payment integration is highest risk; validate early.

compliance_context.value: PCI-DSS SAQ A: Stripe-hosted payment fields, no PAN storage or processing on application servers, HTTPS on all payment pages, Stripe webhook signature verification. Trust accounting: law firms subject to state bar rules on client funds — payment receipt must be identifiable to client matter. GDPR: consent and deletion rights for EU client personal data.
compliance_context.rationale: PCI SAQ A, trust accounting rules, and GDPR trigger compliance context.

project.goal: PCI-compliant payment link platform for professional services with accounting sync
project.target_users: Practitioners (dashboard), clients (payment link page)

frontend.styling: Tailwind CSS with practitioner-brandable color theme
frontend.state: React Query (TanStack) for dashboard data fetching

security.auth: Auth0 M2M for API + user auth for practitioners
security.pci: Stripe Payment Intents + Stripe.js hosted fields (SAQ A)

data.cache: ElastiCache Redis 7 for link nonce and rate limiting

constraints.sync_sla: Accounting sync within 60s of Stripe webhook

reasoner_reviews.product: The payment link model maps cleanly to PCI SAQ A scope — Stripe hosts the card fields iframe, and the application server only handles the Payment Intent creation and confirmation webhook. The single-use constraint is the key UX and fraud prevention feature. QuickBooks sync via OAuth is the highest-value integration for the target market. The practitioner onboarding flow (Stripe Connect setup, accounting OAuth) is the critical path for time-to-first-payment-link.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The cryptographically signed payment link URL is the right architecture for single-use enforcement. Link URL should embed a signed token (HMAC-SHA256 with server secret) containing payment_link_id and expiry; the server verifies the signature and checks payment_link status = open before rendering the payment page. Redis SET NX on the payment_link_id during checkout initiation prevents race conditions. Stripe webhook is the source of truth for payment completion; the accounting sync must be triggered from the webhook handler, not from the client-side return URL.
reasoner_reviews.security: PCI SAQ A requires: (1) HTTPS on all payment pages with no mixed content, (2) no card data fields rendered by the application (Stripe.js only), (3) Content-Security-Policy on the payment page that allows stripe.com frames and scripts and nothing else, (4) Stripe webhook signature verification with stripe.webhooks.constructEvent before processing. Accounting OAuth tokens contain sensitive credentials — must be encrypted at rest with a KMS-backed key, not just database-level encryption.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Single-use payment link enforcement: Redis SET NX on payment_link_id:checkout_in_progress at checkout initiation prevents concurrent payment attempts. On Stripe payment_intent.succeeded webhook: set payment_link status = completed atomically. The 60-second accounting sync SLA requires the webhook handler to enqueue the QuickBooks sync job immediately on payment success, with a background worker executing within the SLA window. Retry with exponential backoff for QuickBooks API transient failures.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The practitioner branding requirement (firm name on payment page) needs specificity: how is the branded payment page URL structured? Recommend subdomain-per-firm (firm.pay.example.com) requires wildcard SSL and DNS automation, which adds ops complexity. Alternatively, path-based (/pay/{firm_slug}/{link_token}) is simpler for MVP. The plan should explicitly choose one model.

frontend.app_structure: Next.js App Router with two distinct layout trees: (1) /app/(dashboard)/... for authenticated practitioner dashboard with Auth0 session middleware. (2) /app/pay/[firm_slug]/[link_token]/... for public payment link pages with no auth, minimal JS, strict CSP.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Dashboard pages: /dashboard (overview: recent payments, revenue this month), /clients (list, create, edit), /invoices (list with status badges, create invoice), /invoices/[id]/link (generate payment link, copy URL, view status), /settings/accounting (QuickBooks OAuth connect/disconnect, sync status), /settings/firm (firm name, logo upload for payment page branding). Payment link pages: /pay/[firm_slug]/[link_token] (firm-branded header, invoice amount and description, Stripe Elements card form, pay button, success state with receipt confirmation).
frontend.state_management: Dashboard: React Query (TanStack) for all server state (clients, invoices, payment links, sync status). Stripe Elements managed by @stripe/react-stripe-js context. Payment link page: server-rendered (Next.js RSC) for SEO and performance; Stripe.js loaded client-side only on payment page.
frontend.frontend_security_notes: Payment page CSP: Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'; frame-src https://js.stripe.com; script-src 'self' https://js.stripe.com; connect-src https://api.stripe.com; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'. No other third-party scripts on payment page. HTTPS enforced by Vercel. Auth0 session stored in encrypted cookie (next-auth iron-session pattern).
frontend.frontend_risks: Risk: Stripe.js CDN unavailability breaks payment page. Mitigation: fallback message and ops alert, but no self-hosting of Stripe.js permitted under SAQ A. Risk: firm_slug collision if two firms have same name. Mitigation: firm_slug generated from firm name + UUID suffix, editable but unique-constrained in database.

backend.service_design: Hono API on ECS Fargate with route groups: /api/v1/clients, /api/v1/invoices, /api/v1/payment-links, /api/v1/stripe-webhooks (no auth), /api/v1/accounting/quickbooks. All routes except webhook and public payment link data require Auth0 JWT middleware.
backend.api_patterns: REST with OpenAPI 3.0 doc. Payment link creation: POST /api/v1/payment-links → creates payment_link record, generates HMAC-SHA256 signed URL token, returns link URL. Public payment data fetch: GET /api/v1/pay/{link_token} (no auth) → verifies token signature, checks link status=open, returns {amount, description, firm_name, stripe_publishable_key, client_secret from Payment Intent}. Stripe webhook: POST /api/v1/stripe-webhooks → verifySignature → dispatch to handler.
backend.background_jobs: QuickBooks sync worker: polls SQS queue for sync_job messages (enqueued by webhook handler), calls QuickBooks Invoices API to mark invoice paid, updates accounting_sync_logs. Retry: 3 attempts with exponential backoff (30s, 2min, 8min); dead-letter queue after 3 failures with PagerDuty alert.
backend.failure_handling: Stripe webhook delivery failure: Stripe retries for 72 hours; idempotent webhook handler using Stripe event ID stored in processed_stripe_events table (INSERT ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING). QuickBooks sync failure: SQS retry → DLQ → PagerDuty → manual retry UI in dashboard. Payment Intent creation failure: return 503 to client with user-friendly message; no partial state written.

security.auth_design: Auth0 tenant with practitioner user pool. JWT RS256 verified in Hono middleware using Auth0 JWKS endpoint. Session: Auth0 session cookie in Next.js dashboard. API token: Auth0 M2M token for server-to-server calls. Payment page: no auth; link token signature verification only.
security.secrets_management: AWS Secrets Manager: Auth0 client secret, Stripe secret key, HMAC signing key for payment link tokens, KMS key ARN for OAuth token encryption. ECS task role with Secrets Manager GetSecretValue permission. Rotating: Stripe restricted keys rotated every 90 days.
security.abuse_prevention: Payment link rate limiting: Redis INCR on ip:{ip}:pay_attempts with 1-hour TTL, max 10 attempts per IP per hour. Practitioner API: 60 requests/minute per Auth0 sub via Redis sliding window. Payment Intent creation: only one active PaymentIntent per payment_link_id (enforced by payment_links.stripe_payment_intent_id UNIQUE constraint).
security.security_risks: Risk: HMAC signing key compromise allows forging payment link tokens. Mitigation: key stored in Secrets Manager, never in environment variables; rotate key quarterly with dual-key transition period. Risk: QuickBooks OAuth token exposure via database read. Mitigation: KMS-encrypted at column level before INSERT; decrypted at runtime in sync worker only.

data.entities: practitioners (id UUID PK, auth0_sub VARCHAR UNIQUE, firm_name VARCHAR(100), firm_slug VARCHAR(50) UNIQUE, logo_s3_key VARCHAR, stripe_connect_account_id VARCHAR, created_at). clients (id UUID PK, practitioner_id FK, name VARCHAR(100), email VARCHAR(254), matter_reference VARCHAR(50), created_at). invoices (id UUID PK, practitioner_id FK, client_id FK, description TEXT, amount_cents BIGINT NOT NULL CHECK(amount_cents > 0), currency_code CHAR(3), status ENUM draft/link_generated/paid/archived, created_at). payment_links (id UUID PK, invoice_id FK UNIQUE, link_token VARCHAR(128) UNIQUE, signed_url TEXT, status ENUM open/completed/expired, stripe_payment_intent_id VARCHAR UNIQUE, expires_at TIMESTAMP, paid_at TIMESTAMP NULLABLE, created_at). payment_attempts (id UUID PK, payment_link_id FK, stripe_payment_intent_id VARCHAR, status ENUM initiated/succeeded/failed, client_ip INET, attempted_at TIMESTAMP). receipts (id UUID PK, payment_link_id FK, stripe_charge_id VARCHAR, amount_cents BIGINT, receipt_email VARCHAR(254), sent_at TIMESTAMP). accounting_sync_logs (id UUID PK, payment_link_id FK, accounting_system ENUM quickbooks/xero, external_invoice_id VARCHAR, status ENUM pending/synced/failed, error_message TEXT NULLABLE, synced_at TIMESTAMP NULLABLE).
data.storage_design: RDS PostgreSQL 16 (db.t4g.medium, us-east-1, Multi-AZ for production). ElastiCache Redis 7 (cache.t4g.medium): payment link checkout locks (SET NX, 30-min TTL), IP rate limiting counters, session cache. S3: practitioner logo uploads (private bucket, CloudFront for serving on payment pages). SQS: accounting-sync-queue (standard queue, visibility timeout 90s) + DLQ.
data.retention_and_deletion: Invoice and payment records: 7-year retention, archived to S3 cold storage after 2 years via RDS export. Client email: retained while practitioner active + 2 years, then soft-delete. GDPR deletion: soft-delete client record and anonymize email within 30 days of request. OAuth tokens: hard-deleted on practitioner account closure.

devops.deployment_topology: Vercel: Next.js app deployed to Vercel Edge Network (production domain: pay.firmhub.com, custom domains per firm via CNAME). ECS Fargate: Hono API service (2 tasks, 0.5 vCPU / 1GB, auto-scale to 8 tasks on CPU 60%). RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ in private subnet. ElastiCache Redis (single-AZ for MVP, Multi-AZ phase 2). SQS accounting-sync-queue + DLQ. S3 logo bucket + CloudFront.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: PR → TypeScript typecheck + ESLint + Prettier → Jest unit tests + integration tests (Testcontainers PostgreSQL + Redis) → Playwright E2E (payment link generation and checkout flow against Stripe test mode) → merge to main → Vercel preview deploy + ECS staging deploy → manual approval → Vercel production promote + ECS production rolling deploy.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM: ECS Fargate API traces with span tags payment_link_id, practitioner_id. CloudWatch Logs: ECS task structured JSON logs, RDS slow query log. Custom Datadog metrics: payment_link_creation_rate, stripe_webhook_processing_latency_ms, accounting_sync_success_rate, accounting_sync_latency_ms. PagerDuty: accounting_sync DLQ message count > 0 → P2, stripe_webhook_processing_error_rate > 0.1% → P1.
devops.devops_risks: Risk: Vercel deployment and ECS API deployment out of sync during rolling deploy. Mitigation: API version header in all requests; Next.js API calls include X-API-Version header; ECS supports N-1 API version for 10-minute window during rollout. Risk: RDS failover during active payment flow. Mitigation: Stripe Payment Intent creation is idempotent; payment link checkout flow retries on 503 with user message.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a PCI SAQ A payment link platform for professional services firms. The key architectural decisions: (1) cryptographically signed link tokens (HMAC-SHA256) for single-use enforcement without database lookup on every page load; (2) Stripe Payment Intents with hosted fields keeps card data entirely off application servers, maintaining SAQ A scope; (3) webhook-to-SQS pattern for accounting sync decouples the payment confirmation flow from the QuickBooks API latency; (4) per-firm branding via firm_slug path prefix is chosen over subdomain-per-firm to avoid wildcard SSL complexity for MVP. Security hardening throughout: KMS-encrypted OAuth tokens, strict CSP on payment pages, Redis rate limiting on payment attempts.
target_output.title: Professional Services Payment Link Platform Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A Next.js 14 + Node.js 22 (Hono) fullstack platform enabling law firms, accountants, and consultants to generate cryptographically signed one-time payment links for client invoices, accept credit cards via Stripe-hosted fields (maintaining PCI SAQ A scope), and automatically sync confirmed payments to QuickBooks Online. Practitioner dashboard on Vercel, API on ECS Fargate, PostgreSQL 16 on RDS, with Redis-backed link locking and SQS-based accounting sync queue.
target_output.architecture_overview: Next.js 14 App Router provides two distinct surfaces: the practitioner dashboard (Auth0-authenticated, SSR with React Query) and public payment link pages (/pay/{firm_slug}/{link_token}) rendered server-side with no authentication and a strict Content-Security-Policy that permits only Stripe.js and stripe.com frames. The Hono API on ECS Fargate handles all business logic: payment link creation (HMAC-SHA256 signed token), Stripe Payment Intent creation, webhook processing (signature-verified), and QuickBooks OAuth sync. PostgreSQL 16 on RDS stores invoice, client, and payment records; Redis enforces single-use checkout locks (SET NX) and IP rate limits; SQS queues accounting sync jobs from the webhook handler to a background worker. Card data never touches application servers — Stripe Elements renders the card fields in an iframe served from Stripe's domain.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 14 App Router, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, @stripe/react-stripe-js, React Query (TanStack). Backend: Node.js 22, Hono framework, raw pg driver, stripe Node.js SDK. Auth: Auth0 (practitioner user pool, JWT RS256). Payments: Stripe Payment Intents + Stripe.js hosted fields (SAQ A). Data: RDS PostgreSQL 16 (Multi-AZ), ElastiCache Redis 7. Queue: SQS standard queue + DLQ. Accounting: QuickBooks Online API v3, OAuth 2.0. Hosting: Vercel (Next.js), ECS Fargate (API). CI/CD: GitHub Actions. Monitoring: Datadog APM, Stripe Dashboard.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Payment Link Generation: practitioner selects client and invoice, sets expiry, system creates payment_link record and HMAC-SHA256 signed URL token ({payment_link_id}.{expiry_ts}.{hmac}), returns shareable URL. Public Payment Page: server-side renders firm name, invoice description and amount; creates Stripe PaymentIntent (server-side); loads Stripe.js client-side; renders Stripe Elements card form; on submit, Stripe processes card data directly. Accounting Sync: Stripe payment_intent.succeeded webhook → enqueue SQS sync job → QuickBooks worker marks invoice paid, logs to accounting_sync_logs. Receipt Delivery: Stripe sends hosted receipt email; practitioner receipt stored as receipts record for compliance. Practitioner Analytics: monthly revenue totals, payment success rate, average days-to-payment per client.
target_output.system_components: Next.js dashboard app on Vercel (practitioner-authenticated pages and public payment pages). Hono API on ECS Fargate (2-8 tasks, auth middleware, payment link and invoice routes, Stripe webhook endpoint). HMAC-SHA256 payment link token signer/verifier (server-side, key from Secrets Manager). Stripe Payment Intent service (creates Payment Intent on payment page load, validates on webhook). QuickBooks sync worker (SQS consumer on ECS Fargate, QuickBooks OAuth API calls, retry logic). Redis 7 (checkout lock SET NX, IP rate limiting, session nonce cache). RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ (practitioners, clients, invoices, payment_links, payment_attempts, receipts, accounting_sync_logs). SQS accounting-sync-queue + DLQ. S3 (practitioner logo uploads) + CloudFront. AWS Secrets Manager (Auth0 secret, Stripe key, HMAC signing key, KMS ARN).
target_output.workflows: Payment Link Creation Flow: Practitioner fills invoice detail in dashboard → POST /api/v1/invoices (creates invoice record) → POST /api/v1/payment-links/{invoice_id} → server generates HMAC token: HMAC-SHA256(key, '{payment_link_id}.{expiry_unix}') → base64url-encode → construct URL /pay/{firm_slug}/{link_token} → store payment_links record (link_token, status=open, expires_at) → return URL to dashboard for copy/share. Client Payment Flow: Client opens payment link URL → Next.js RSC: parse link_token, verify HMAC signature, check expiry, check payment_links.status=open → server calls stripe.paymentIntents.create({amount, currency, metadata: {payment_link_id}}) → render payment page with Stripe Elements and client_secret → client enters card in Stripe iframe → stripe.confirmPayment() called client-side → Stripe processes card → redirects to /pay/{firm_slug}/{link_token}/success or /failed. Webhook Processing Flow: Stripe POST /api/v1/stripe-webhooks → Hono handler calls stripe.webhooks.constructEvent(body, sig, webhookSecret) → verify event type payment_intent.succeeded → check processed_stripe_events (INSERT ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING for idempotency) → update payment_links.status=completed and invoices.status=paid in single PostgreSQL transaction → enqueue SQS message {payment_link_id, quickbooks_sync} → respond 200 to Stripe. QuickBooks Sync Flow: SQS worker receives message → fetch invoice and payment details from PostgreSQL → call QuickBooks API PUT /v3/company/{realmId}/invoice (paid=true, payment_reference=stripe_charge_id) using practitioner's decrypted OAuth token → update accounting_sync_logs.status=synced → acknowledge SQS message. On failure: SQS visibility timeout expires → retry up to 3 times → DLQ → PagerDuty P2 alert.
target_output.data_model: practitioners (id UUID PK, auth0_sub VARCHAR(128) UNIQUE NOT NULL, firm_name VARCHAR(100), firm_slug VARCHAR(50) UNIQUE NOT NULL, logo_s3_key VARCHAR, stripe_connect_account_id VARCHAR, quickbooks_realm_id VARCHAR, quickbooks_oauth_token_encrypted BYTEA, quickbooks_token_expires_at TIMESTAMP, created_at TIMESTAMP). clients (id UUID PK, practitioner_id UUID FK NOT NULL, name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL, email VARCHAR(254) NOT NULL, matter_reference VARCHAR(50), created_at TIMESTAMP). invoices (id UUID PK, practitioner_id UUID FK NOT NULL, client_id UUID FK NOT NULL, description TEXT NOT NULL, amount_cents BIGINT NOT NULL CHECK(amount_cents > 0), currency_code CHAR(3) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'USD', status ENUM draft/link_generated/paid/archived, created_at TIMESTAMP). payment_links (id UUID PK, invoice_id UUID FK UNIQUE NOT NULL, link_token VARCHAR(128) UNIQUE NOT NULL, signed_url TEXT, status ENUM open/completed/expired, stripe_payment_intent_id VARCHAR UNIQUE, expires_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL, paid_at TIMESTAMP, created_at TIMESTAMP). payment_attempts (id UUID PK, payment_link_id UUID FK, stripe_payment_intent_id VARCHAR, status ENUM initiated/succeeded/failed, client_ip INET, attempted_at TIMESTAMP). receipts (id UUID PK, payment_link_id UUID FK NOT NULL, stripe_charge_id VARCHAR NOT NULL, amount_cents BIGINT, receipt_email VARCHAR(254), sent_at TIMESTAMP). accounting_sync_logs (id UUID PK, payment_link_id UUID FK NOT NULL, accounting_system ENUM quickbooks/xero NOT NULL, external_invoice_id VARCHAR, status ENUM pending/synced/failed NOT NULL, error_message TEXT, synced_at TIMESTAMP). processed_stripe_events (stripe_event_id VARCHAR(128) PK, processed_at TIMESTAMP) -- idempotency table.
target_output.api_design: POST /api/v1/invoices (auth: JWT) → {id, description, amount_cents, client_id, status}. POST /api/v1/payment-links (auth: JWT) → {payment_link_id, signed_url, expires_at}. GET /api/v1/pay/{link_token} (no auth) → {amount_cents, currency, description, firm_name, stripe_client_secret, firm_logo_url} — verifies HMAC token signature and status=open before returning client_secret. PATCH /api/v1/payment-links/{id}/expire (auth: JWT) → sets status=expired for practitioner-initiated cancellation. POST /api/v1/stripe-webhooks (no auth, signature-verified) → processes payment_intent.succeeded, payment_intent.payment_failed. GET /api/v1/invoices?status=paid&from=2026-01-01 (auth: JWT) → paginated invoice list with accounting_sync status. GET /api/v1/accounting/quickbooks/connect (auth: JWT) → redirects to QuickBooks OAuth authorize URL. GET /api/v1/accounting/quickbooks/callback (auth: JWT session) → exchanges code for tokens, encrypts with KMS, stores on practitioners record.
target_output.security_and_compliance: PCI SAQ A controls: HTTPS enforced on all payment pages (Vercel HSTS). No card data fields rendered by application — Stripe Elements iframe served from js.stripe.com only. Payment page CSP: 'Content-Security-Policy: default-src self; frame-src https://js.stripe.com https://hooks.stripe.com; script-src self https://js.stripe.com; connect-src https://api.stripe.com; style-src self unsafe-inline'. Stripe webhook: constructEvent signature verification with STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET before any processing. QuickBooks OAuth tokens encrypted with AWS KMS CMK before INSERT into practitioners.quickbooks_oauth_token_encrypted; decrypted at runtime in sync worker only. Auth0 JWT validation in Hono middleware using JWKS endpoint; no auth bypass paths. HMAC-SHA256 payment link token: key from Secrets Manager, rotated quarterly with 30-day dual-key transition (old key accepted for links created before rotation). Rate limiting: 10 payment page loads per IP per hour (Redis INCR + TTL); 3 failed payment attempts per payment_link_id (Redis INCR). GDPR: consent banner on payment pages for EU clients (EU country detection via CloudFront geo header); client email deletion within 30 days via soft-delete + anonymize migration.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions: PR → bun run typecheck + bun run lint + bun run test (Jest + Testcontainers) + Playwright E2E (Stripe test mode, payment link generation + checkout flow) → merge main → Vercel preview + ECS staging deploy (ECR push + ECS rolling update) → manual production approval → Vercel promote + ECS rolling deploy (min healthy 100%). ECS Fargate: 2 tasks baseline, auto-scale to 8 on CPU 60% with 3-minute cooldown. RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ: automated backups 7-day retention, point-in-time recovery. Database migrations: node-pg-migrate via ECS one-off task before rolling deploy. Secrets: all credentials in AWS Secrets Manager; ECS task role for access; no credentials in environment variables or ECR image. Staging: separate RDS instance, Stripe test mode keys, QuickBooks sandbox.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM: ECS Fargate traces for all Hono routes, QuickBooks sync worker, Stripe webhook handler — span tags: practitioner_id (hashed), payment_link_id, accounting_system. Custom Datadog metrics: payment_link_creation_count, stripe_webhook_processing_latency_ms (alert >2s), accounting_sync_success_rate (alert <95%), accounting_sync_latency_ms (alert >60s SLA), payment_page_load_latency_ms. CloudWatch: RDS FreeStorageSpace (alert <10GB), ElastiCache Redis memory utilization (alert >80%). Stripe Dashboard: payment success rate, refund rate, dispute rate — reviewed weekly. PagerDuty: accounting_sync DLQ depth >0 → P2, stripe_webhook_error_rate >0.1% → P1, RDS multi-AZ failover event → P2.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: ECS Fargate (2-8 tasks × 0.5 vCPU/1GB): ~$80-320/month. RDS PostgreSQL db.t4g.medium Multi-AZ: ~$120/month. ElastiCache Redis cache.t4g.medium: ~$50/month. Vercel Pro: ~$20/month. SQS: ~$1/month. S3 + CloudFront logos: ~$5/month. Datadog APM (5 hosts): ~$150/month. Secrets Manager: ~$5/month. Total ~$430-630/month at launch scale. At 1000 practitioners with 10K monthly payments: ECS scales to 4 tasks (~$160), RDS upgrade to db.t4g.large (~$200), total ~$700/month. Cost optimization: RDS reserved instance after 3 months of stable load saves 40%.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 — Core Payment Flow (weeks 1-8): Practitioner onboarding with Auth0, firm profile and firm_slug setup, Stripe Connect account linking. Client and invoice CRUD. Payment link generation with HMAC-SHA256 token. Stripe Elements payment page with SAQ A controls. Stripe webhook handler with idempotency. Receipt records. Redis single-use checkout lock. Phase 2 — Accounting Integration (weeks 9-12): QuickBooks OAuth connect flow with KMS-encrypted token storage. SQS-based sync worker with retry and DLQ. accounting_sync_logs table. Practitioner sync status dashboard. Stripe-to-QuickBooks invoice reconciliation validation. Phase 3 — Analytics and Compliance (weeks 13-16): Practitioner revenue analytics (monthly totals, payment velocity, client payment history). GDPR consent banner and deletion workflow. Payment link expiry management. Playwright E2E test suite for full payment flow. PagerDuty escalation finalized. Xero integration design (phase 2 scope).
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode across Next.js and Hono codebase. Biome for linting (replaces ESLint + Prettier). Bun as package manager and test runner. Jest for unit tests; Testcontainers for PostgreSQL and Redis integration tests; Playwright for E2E in Stripe test mode. No raw SQL in route handlers — all queries in /lib/db/*.ts modules with named exports. Stripe SDK calls isolated in /lib/stripe.ts; never instantiated in route files directly. All financial amounts in integer cents (BIGINT) — no floating point arithmetic. PR gate: typecheck + lint + test must pass; minimum 80% coverage for payment flow modules. Accounting OAuth token never logged — scrubbed from all Datadog traces by custom Hono middleware.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: QuickBooks API deprecates OAuth 1.0 tokens or changes invoice-paid endpoint, breaking sync. Mitigation: adapter interface for accounting integrations; QuickBooks implementation behind /lib/accounting/quickbooks.ts; Xero can be added as alternative implementation without API changes. Risk: Stripe webhook delivery failure after payment success causes accounting sync to never trigger. Mitigation: Stripe retries for 72 hours; practitioner dashboard shows accounting sync status; manual retry button for failed syncs; DLQ PagerDuty alert. Tradeoff: HMAC link tokens vs. opaque random tokens in database — HMAC chosen so server can verify token validity without a database lookup (important for public page load latency); tradeoff is that key rotation requires a dual-key transition period. Tradeoff: SQS for accounting sync vs. direct API call in webhook handler — SQS decouples payment confirmation from QuickBooks API latency (up to 5s) and provides retry semantics without blocking the Stripe webhook response.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Path-based payment URLs (/pay/{firm_slug}/{link_token}) chosen over subdomain-per-firm to avoid wildcard SSL complexity for MVP. HMAC-SHA256 token format confirmed: {payment_link_id}.{expiry_unix_ts}.{hmac_base64url}. Stripe Connect standard account chosen over custom account for faster practitioner onboarding. QuickBooks OAuth 2.0 token stored KMS-encrypted in practitioners table (not a separate vault service) for MVP simplicity.
